                              * Should never share tables with consumer users to reduce risk of cross-scope bugs.
________________


13.1.2 Telephony, routing, and inbound number entities
Phone number (User number mapping)
                              * Represents the dedicated inbound AI number assigned to the user in Phase 1.
                              * Stores:
                              * the dedicated AI number (E.164 format)
                              * provisioned_at
                              * provider identifiers (Twilio SID equivalents)
                              * status (active, pending, suspended)
                              * Relationship:
                              * User 1-to-1 (or 1-to-many if future expansion) Dedicated AI number mapping
Forwarding configuration
                              * Represents the rules that decide when the AI should answer via forwarding scenarios:
                              * busy
                              * unreachable
                              * busy + unreachable
                              * Stores:
                              * forwarding enabled
                              * selected condition(s)
                              * forwarding verification status (because carrier behavior varies)
                              * last_verified_at
                              * Relationship:
                              * Forwarding configuration belongs to one User
Live handoff configuration
                              * Represents user rules for when the AI offers handoff during a call:
                              * VIP, urgent, or both
                              * Relationship:
                              * Live handoff configuration belongs to one User
Telephony provider call linkage
                              * A call must be traceable to provider artifacts:
                              * inbound call id (example: Twilio CallSid)
                              * recording ids (if enabled)
                              * messaging ids (for text back)
                              * Relationship:
                              * Provider call linkage belongs to one Call record
________________


13.1.3 Call record and call artifacts
Call record
                              * This is the central Phase 1 object. Every handled call must produce a usable call record even if downstream steps fail.
                              * Stores minimum call history metadata:
                              * user_id (owner)
                              * source type (dedicated vs forwarded)
                              * caller display (derived display string)
                              * caller number (raw and normalized)
                              * timestamps: started_at, ended_at
                              * processing state: created, in_progress, completed, partial, failed
                              * one-line reason snippet and urgency badge inputs for list view expectations
                              * Relationships:
                              * Call record belongs to one User
                              * Call record 0-to-1 Transcript artifact
                              * Call record 0-to-1 Summary artifact
                              * Call record 0-to-1 Recording artifact (optional, off by default)
                              * Call record 1-to-many Labels applied
                              * Call record 1-to-many Notes
                              * Call record 1-to-many Actions (callback, text, reminder creation)
                              * Call record 1-to-many Audit events (scoped subset)
                              * Call record 0-to-many Linked reminders
                              * Call record stores Live handoff timeline fields (see below)
Transcript
                              * Stored as an artifact of the call record.
                              * Must support partial transcripts when calls end early or audio is unclear.
                              * Should support a status model:
                              * pending, succeeded, failed, partial
                              * failure_reason, retry_count
                              * Relationship:
                              * Transcript belongs to one Call record
Summary
                              * Stored as an artifact of the call record.
                              * Must support fallback state: if summary fails, record still exists and summary may show an unavailable placeholder with potential retry.
                              * Relationship:
                              * Summary belongs to one Call record
Recording
                              * Optional. Off by default.
                              * If enabled, must be protected behind authentication and biometrics, and must follow retention and deletion rules.
                              * Relationship:
                              * Recording belongs to one Call record
                              * Recording storage points to object storage location or provider recording id
________________


13.1.4 Live handoff timeline fields
Live handoff is not just a UI event, it is a stored state machine that must be recorded for trust and debugging.
The call record must store the handoff timeline:
                              * handoff_offered_at
                              * handoff_offer_reason (VIP, urgent, both)
                              * handoff_accepted_at (nullable)
                              * handoff_accepted_device_id (nullable)
                              * handoff_result (success, failed, expired, ignored)
This stored timeline supports:
                              * multi-device race safety (first accept wins, others blocked)
                              * investigating late prompts or failed connects
                              * auditing user-impacting call actions
Edge behavior for unreachable user must be recorded as failure state, and the system must resume AI screening immediately rather than dropping the call.
________________


13.1.5 Labels, lists, and caller controls
Label taxonomy
                              * Represents what labels exist and how they are applied.
                              * At minimum, Phase 1 needs labels that drive user experience in call list and importance logic:
                              * VIP
                              * spam
                              * urgent
                              * blocked
                              * unknown
                              * Relationship:
                              * Label definitions are global constants (or system-owned records)
                              * Applied labels are per Call record (many-to-many via join entity)
VIP list
                              * User-managed list of prioritized numbers.
                              * Relationship:
                              * VIP list belongs to one User
                              * VIP entries each store the normalized number and optional display name
                              * VIP calls are marked Important and can influence behaviors like handoff triggers and duration policies.
Block list
                              * User-managed list of blocked numbers.
                              * Relationship:
                              * Block list belongs to one User
                              * Block entries store the normalized number, reason, created_at
                              * Blocked behavior must be consistent and should not create noise by default.
Spam classification state
                              * Even if spam detection is assisted, the stored state must be explicit:
                              * spam flag + reason + who set it (AI suggestion vs user confirmed)
                              * reversible changes with audit trail where appropriate
________________


13.1.6 Actions, approvals, and outbound messaging
Action history
                              * Represents all follow-up actions attached to a call record:
                              * callback initiated
                              * text back drafted, approved, sent
                              * reminder created
                              * note added
                              * Action history must appear on the call record and be retained and deleted consistently with call data.
Outbound message (SMS)
                              * Stores a specific outbound message attempt:
                              * content (final approved text)
                              * timestamps (created, sent)
                              * delivery status (if available)
                              * provider message id
                              * Relationship:
                              * Message belongs to one User
                              * Message optionally links to one Call record (for context)
                              * Approval-first is mandatory:
                              * draft suggestions may exist, but nothing is sent automatically without user approval.
Approval record
                              * Represents the approval decision for an outbound action:
                              * who approved (user id)
                              * approved_at
                              * version hash of message content at approval time
                              * Relationship:
                              * Approval record belongs to one Outbound message (or more generally one Action)
________________


13.1.7 Reminders and notification-related entities
Reminder
                              * Represents a scheduled notification linked to a call record or standalone.
                              * Stores:
                              * due_at
                              * reminder type (one-time, potentially recurring if later)
                              * status (pending, fired, cancelled)
                              * Relationship:
                              * Reminder belongs to one User
                              * Reminder optionally links to one Call record
Notification delivery record (optional but recommended)
                              * Tracks reliability and debugging:
                              * notification type
                              * provider result
                              * retry count
                              * last_attempt_at
                              * This supports “retry and delivery tracking” expectations and failure investigation.
Privacy mode setting
                              * Stored as user setting that impacts notification payload shaping:
                              * private by default requirement is a core Phase 1 behavior.
________________


13.1.8 Memory entities
Memory item
                              * Represents a single memory entry that the AI can use later.
                              * Must be visible and deletable in-app, with a dedicated memory page.
                              * Stores:
                              * type category (caller preference, user preference)
                              * associated caller (if relevant)
                              * created_at
                              * optional source call link
                              * Relationship:
                              * Memory item belongs to one User
                              * Memory item optionally links to one Call record as provenance
Memory off behavior must be explicit in stored settings, and should offer a clear user choice about what happens to existing memory.
________________


13.1.9 Audit and operational entities
Audit event
                              * Represents an immutable security and accountability record.
                              * Must exist for specific event types:
                              * login, logout
                              * device added, removed
                              * privacy-critical settings changes (notification privacy, recording on/off, retention changes, memory on/off, live handoff rules)
                              * deletions (single call, delete all call data, delete account)
                              * outbound actions (text sent)
                              * handoff events (offered, accepted, failed, expired)
                              * Rule: audit logs must not expose full transcript content.
Retention policy and deletion tracking
                              * Retention must be enforced automatically and cover:
                              * summaries, transcripts, recordings, notes, action history, linked reminders
                              * Verified deletion requirements imply we need internal deletion job tracking states:
                              * what was deleted
                              * when deletion ran
                              * whether derived artifacts were removed
                              * whether device caches will be cleared on next sync
                              * retries and alert status if failures happen
________________


13.2 Identifier strategy
Phase 1 must use a consistent identifier strategy that supports:
                              * secure APIs (non-guessable ids)
                              * internal joins and indexing
                              * provider artifact correlation
                              * idempotency for webhooks and background jobs
13.2.1 Primary keys and public ids
Internal primary keys
                              * Use UUIDs for all primary entities (user, device, call record, memory item, reminders, message, audit event).
                              * Benefits:
                              * reduces predictability compared to sequential ids
                              * simplifies multi-service generation
Public id policy
                              * The API should expose only UUID-based identifiers for user-owned resources.
                              * Never expose provider ids (Twilio SIDs, etc.) as user-facing identifiers.
13.2.2 Provider identifiers (external ids)
Store provider ids for correlation, never as primary keys:
                              * telephony_call_sid (per call)
                              * telephony_recording_sid (per recording)
                              * sms_message_sid (per outbound message)
These fields must be indexed because they are used to process inbound webhooks and to reconcile failures.
13.2.3 Idempotency keys and deduplication
Idempotency is required for correctness because retries happen:
                              * telephony webhooks can deliver duplicates
                              * background jobs retry on failure
                              * notifications and deletion jobs can fail and must retry
Strategy:
                              * Each webhook and job type must have a deterministic idempotency key.
                              * Store an idempotency record keyed by:
                              * provider_event_id if available
                              * else a hash of (provider call id, event type, timestamp bucket)
                              * Enforce uniqueness so duplicate delivery cannot create duplicate call records, duplicate summaries, or duplicate outbound texts.
________________


13.3 Data classification
This section defines what data is sensitive and what rules apply, so later chapters can enforce encryption, logging rules, retention, and safe caching.
13.3.1 Classification levels
Use four practical levels for Phase 1:
Level A: Public
                              * Non-user-specific documentation strings, static app copy, non-sensitive feature flags metadata.
Level B: Internal
                              * Operational metadata that should not be public but is not user-private content:
                              * internal service health metrics
                              * anonymized aggregate counters
Level C: Confidential
                              * Personal metadata that identifies a person or their communication pattern:
                              * user profile fields
                              * phone numbers
                              * call timestamps and durations
                              * VIP and block lists
                              * reminder schedules
Level D: Restricted
                              * Highly sensitive call content and derived content:
                              * transcripts
                              * summaries (still sensitive because they contain content)
                              * recordings
                              * memory items (preferences, caller context)
                              * Rule: these must never appear in plaintext logs, and access should be guarded strongly.
13.3.2 Logging and monitoring constraints
Audit logs and operational logs must be useful without leaking Restricted content:
                              * Audit events record that something happened, but must not include full transcript content.
                              * Operational monitoring should track success rates and failure reasons at the system level:
                              * call success rate
                              * transcript success rate
                              * summary success rate
                              * notification delivery success
                              * retention deletion job success
13.3.3 Device caching implications
Caching exists for speed, but is part of the privacy surface:
                              * Allowed to cache call list entries, summary text, minimal metadata.
                              * Full transcripts and recordings should only be cached if encrypted at rest on device, protected by biometrics, and cleared on logout, device revoke, delete call data, delete account.
13.3.4 Retention and deletion classification mapping
Retention must apply consistently and automatically, and deletion must remove:
                              * server copy
                              * derived artifacts
                              * device caches on next sync
                              * any ability to access deleted content again
Therefore, each object in Chapter 14 must carry a retention category tag so deletion jobs can reliably traverse and remove:
                              * call record core row
                              * transcript
                              * summary
                              * recording file reference and storage object
                              * notes
                              * action history
                              * linked reminders
                              * any memory items linked to that call, if product policy defines that linkage
________________


According to a documenAccording to a document from 2026.


# Chapter 14: Database Schema Specification


## 14.0 Schema goals and rules


### 14.0.1 Goals


* Store all Phase 1 objects required for inbound AI calls, forwarding, live handoff, approval-first texting, reminders, memory, privacy controls, and auditing.
* Enforce strict per-user isolation at the data model level.
* Support reliable retries and deduplication for provider webhooks and background jobs.
* Make retention and deletion enforceable and verifiable.


### 14.0.2 Core rules (non-negotiable)


* Every user-owned row has `owner_user_id`.
* Every sensitive content field is either encrypted at the application layer or explicitly classified as Restricted and protected by strict access controls.
* Call deletion must cascade to all call artifacts.
* Account deletion must be able to purge all user data.
* Provider events must be idempotent.


### 14.0.3 Postgres prerequisites


Enable extensions:


* `pgcrypto` (UUID generation, hashes)
* `citext` (case-insensitive email)
  Optional later:
* `pg_trgm` (search performance if you add search)


Use `timestamptz` everywhere.


---


## 14.1 ERD diagram section


```mermaid
erDiagram
  users ||--o{ auth_identities : has
  users ||--o{ mfa_methods : has
  users ||--o{ recovery_codes : has
  users ||--o{ devices : has
  devices ||--o{ sessions : has
  users ||--|| user_settings : has
  users ||--o{ user_numbers : has
  users ||--|| forwarding_configs : has
  users ||--|| handoff_configs : has
  users ||--o{ contacts : has
  users ||--o{ calls : has
  calls ||--o{ call_events : has
  calls ||--|| call_transcripts : has
  calls ||--|| call_summaries : has
  calls ||--|| call_recordings : has
  calls ||--o{ call_notes : has
  calls ||--o{ call_actions : has
  calls ||--o{ call_labels : has
  labels ||--o{ call_labels : applies
  users ||--o{ vip_entries : has
  users ||--o{ block_entries : has
  users ||--o{ memory_items : has
  users ||--o{ reminders : has
  users ||--o{ outbound_messages : has
  outbound_messages ||--o{ message_drafts : has
  outbound_messages ||--o{ approvals : has
  devices ||--o{ push_tokens : has
  users ||--o{ audit_events : has
  provider_events }o--|| calls : links
  idempotency_keys }o--|| users : scopes
  outbox_events }o--|| users : scopes


  admin_users ||--o{ admin_sessions : has
  admin_users ||--o{ admin_user_roles : has
  admin_roles ||--o{ admin_user_roles : assigns
  admin_users ||--o{ break_glass_events : triggers
```


---


## 14.2 Table-by-table specification


### 14.2.0 Common column conventions


**Primary key**


* `id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()`


**Ownership**


* `owner_user_id uuid not null references users(id)`


**Timestamps**


* `created_at timestamptz not null default now()`
* `updated_at timestamptz not null default now()` (maintain via app or trigger)


**Encryption column naming**
For sensitive fields that require application-layer encryption:


* `<field>_ciphertext bytea not null`
* `<field>_nonce bytea not null`
* `<field>_key_version int not null`
  For searchable sensitive identifiers (phone numbers):
* `<field>_hash char(64) not null` (hex SHA-256)
* `<field>_last4 varchar(4) null`


**Retention category**
Each table is tagged with a retention category used by deletion jobs:


* ACCOUNT: user account core
* SECURITY: auth, MFA, sessions
* SETTINGS: user settings
* TELEPHONY: number mappings, forwarding
* CALL_CORE: call metadata and state
* CALL_CONTENT: transcript, summary, memory content derived from calls
* MEDIA: recordings references
* MESSAGING: drafts, approvals, outbound SMS logs
* LISTS: VIP, block, spam markers
* NOTIFICATIONS: push tokens and delivery attempts
* AUDIT: audit trail
* OPS: provider events, idempotency, outbox


---


### 14.2.1 `users` (ACCOUNT)


**Purpose**


* Canonical personal user account.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `email citext unique null` (null allowed if OAuth-only, but recommended to always store email)
* `email_verified boolean not null default false`
* `status text not null` with CHECK in (`active`,`pending_verification`,`locked`,`deleted`)
* `display_name text null`
* `default_timezone text not null` (IANA string)
* `language_code text not null default 'en'`
* `deleted_at timestamptz null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* Unique email when not null.
* CHECK that `deleted_at` is not null only when status is `deleted`.


**Indexes**


* `users_email_idx` on (email)
* `users_status_idx` on (status)


**Retention**


* ACCOUNT


**Encryption**


* None required for base account fields beyond DB-level encryption, but any optional sensitive profile fields added later should follow encryption rules.


---


### 14.2.2 `auth_identities` (SECURITY)


**Purpose**


* Link login methods to a user (email-password, Google, Apple).


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `provider text not null` CHECK in (`email_password`,`google`,`apple`)
* `provider_subject text not null` (OAuth sub, or email for email-password identity)
* `provider_email citext null`
* `provider_email_verified boolean null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* Unique per provider subject:


  * `unique(provider, provider_subject)`
* For provider = email_password:


  * `provider_subject` must equal the user email


**Indexes**


* `auth_identities_user_idx` on (owner_user_id)
* `auth_identities_provider_sub_idx` on (provider, provider_subject)


**Retention**


* SECURITY


**Encryption**


* Provider subject is not encrypted because it is required for login lookup, but it is Confidential and must never be logged in plaintext.


---


### 14.2.3 `mfa_methods` (SECURITY)


**Purpose**


* Store enabled MFA methods for a user.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `method_type text not null` CHECK in (`totp`,`email_otp`)
* `totp_secret_ciphertext bytea null`
* `totp_secret_nonce bytea null`
* `totp_secret_key_version int null`
* `is_primary boolean not null default false`
* `enabled_at timestamptz null`
* `disabled_at timestamptz null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* Only one enabled primary method per user:


  * partial unique index on (owner_user_id) where `is_primary = true and disabled_at is null`
* If method_type = totp:


  * totp secret fields must be not null when enabled


**Indexes**


* `mfa_methods_user_idx` on (owner_user_id)


**Retention**


* SECURITY


**Encryption**


* TOTP secret must be application-layer encrypted.


---


### 14.2.4 `recovery_codes` (SECURITY)


**Purpose**


* One-time recovery codes for MFA recovery.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `code_hash char(64) not null`
* `used_at timestamptz null`
* `created_at`


**Constraints**


* `unique(owner_user_id, code_hash)`


**Indexes**


* `recovery_codes_user_idx` on (owner_user_id)


**Retention**


* SECURITY


**Encryption**


* Store only hashes, never plaintext codes.


---


### 14.2.5 `devices` (SECURITY)


**Purpose**


* Multi-device support, push delivery, revoke.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `platform text not null` CHECK in (`ios`,`android`,`web`)
* `device_name text null`
* `app_version text null`
* `os_version text null`
* `last_seen_at timestamptz null`
* `revoked_at timestamptz null`
* `revoke_reason text null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* None beyond FK.


**Indexes**


* `devices_user_idx` on (owner_user_id)
* `devices_user_active_idx` on (owner_user_id) where revoked_at is null


**Retention**


* SECURITY


**Encryption**


* None.


---


### 14.2.6 `sessions` (SECURITY)


**Purpose**


* Per-device sessions, token rotation, revoke.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `device_id uuid not null fk devices(id) on delete cascade`
* `access_token_id uuid not null` (opaque token identifier)
* `access_token_hash char(64) not null`
* `access_expires_at timestamptz not null`
* `refresh_token_hash char(64) not null`
* `refresh_expires_at timestamptz not null`
* `last_refresh_at timestamptz null`
* `revoked_at timestamptz null`
* `revoke_reason text null`
* `ip_created inet null`
* `ip_last inet null`
* `user_agent text null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* `unique(access_token_hash)`
* `unique(refresh_token_hash)`


**Indexes**


* `sessions_user_idx` on (owner_user_id)
* `sessions_device_idx` on (device_id)
* `sessions_active_by_device_idx` on (device_id) where revoked_at is null
* `sessions_refresh_exp_idx` on (refresh_expires_at)


**Retention**


* SECURITY


**Encryption**


* Store only hashes, never plaintext tokens.


---


### 14.2.7 `push_tokens` (NOTIFICATIONS)


**Purpose**


* Track push tokens per device (rotate and revoke cleanly).


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `device_id uuid not null fk devices(id) on delete cascade`
* `provider text not null` CHECK in (`fcm`)
* `token text not null`
* `created_at`
* `revoked_at timestamptz null`


**Constraints**


* `unique(provider, token)`


**Indexes**


* `push_tokens_device_idx` on (device_id)


**Retention**


* NOTIFICATIONS


**Encryption**


* Token is Confidential. Optionally encrypt `token` if you want stronger DB compromise resistance.


---


### 14.2.8 `user_settings` (SETTINGS)


**Purpose**


* Store settings used during call handling and privacy enforcement.


**Columns**


* `owner_user_id uuid pk fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `privacy_notifications boolean not null default true`
* `quiet_hours_enabled boolean not null default true`
* `quiet_hours_start time null`
* `quiet_hours_end time null`
* `timezone text not null`
* `memory_enabled boolean not null default true`
* `retention_days smallint not null default 90`
* `biometric_policy text not null` CHECK in (`gate_app_open`,`gate_call_details`,`gate_recordings`,`gate_all_sensitive`)
* `important_rule text not null` CHECK in (`vip_only`,`urgent_only`,`vip_and_urgent`)
* `call_objective text not null` CHECK in (`screen_and_summarize`,`take_message_only`,`determine_urgency_only`)
* `call_duration_limit_minutes smallint not null default 20`
* `vip_call_duration_limit_minutes smallint not null default 30`
* `recording_enabled boolean not null default false`
* `transcript_disclosure_mode text not null` CHECK in (`ai_says_it`,`legal_text_only`)
* `recording_announcement_required boolean not null default true`
* `handoff_enabled boolean not null default true`
* `handoff_trigger text not null` CHECK in (`vip_only`,`urgent_only`,`vip_and_urgent`)
* `handoff_target_type text not null` CHECK in (`real_phone_number`)
* `handoff_target_phone_ciphertext bytea null`
* `handoff_target_phone_nonce bytea null`
* `handoff_target_phone_key_version int null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* If handoff_enabled = true and target_type = real_phone_number, target encrypted fields must be not null.
* retention_days CHECK between 7 and 365 (recommended safety bound).


**Indexes**


* None required beyond PK.


**Retention**


* SETTINGS


**Encryption**


* Handoff destination phone must be application-layer encrypted.


---


### 14.2.9 `user_numbers` (TELEPHONY)


**Purpose**


* Dedicated inbound AI number per user, provider mapping.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `ai_number_e164 text not null` (E.164)
* `ai_number_hash char(64) not null`
* `ai_number_last4 varchar(4) not null`
* `provider text not null` CHECK in (`twilio`)
* `provider_number_sid text not null`
* `status text not null` CHECK in (`active`,`pending`,`suspended`)
* `provisioned_at timestamptz null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* `unique(owner_user_id)` for Phase 1 (one AI number per user)
* `unique(provider, provider_number_sid)`
* `unique(ai_number_hash)` to avoid accidental reuse


**Indexes**


* `user_numbers_user_idx` on (owner_user_id)
* `user_numbers_hash_idx` on (ai_number_hash)


**Retention**


* TELEPHONY


**Encryption**


* AI number can be stored plaintext because it is not the user’s personal number, but it still identifies the user’s service number and is Confidential.


---


### 14.2.10 `forwarding_configs` (TELEPHONY)


**Purpose**


* Forwarding mode rules and verification state.


**Columns**


* `owner_user_id uuid pk fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `enabled boolean not null default true`
* `forward_on_busy boolean not null default true`
* `forward_on_unreachable boolean not null default true`
* `verification_status text not null` CHECK in (`unverified`,`verified`,`failed`)
* `last_verified_at timestamptz null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* At least one of busy or unreachable must be true when enabled.


**Retention**


* TELEPHONY


---


### 14.2.11 `handoff_configs` (TELEPHONY)


**Purpose**


* Handoff behavior rules beyond settings if you prefer a dedicated table.


**Columns**


* `owner_user_id uuid pk fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `enabled boolean not null default true`
* `trigger_rule text not null` CHECK in (`vip_only`,`urgent_only`,`vip_and_urgent`)
* `offer_timeout_seconds int not null default 20`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Retention**


* TELEPHONY


---


### 14.2.12 `contacts` (LISTS)


**Purpose**


* Optional first-class contact feature to map numbers to names for UX and memory.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `phone_e164_ciphertext bytea not null`
* `phone_e164_nonce bytea not null`
* `phone_e164_key_version int not null`
* `phone_e164_hash char(64) not null`
* `phone_last4 varchar(4) not null`
* `display_name text null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* `unique(owner_user_id, phone_e164_hash)`


**Indexes**


* `contacts_user_phone_hash_idx` on (owner_user_id, phone_e164_hash)


**Retention**


* LISTS


**Encryption**


* Phone must be encrypted, with hash for lookups.


---


### 14.2.13 `labels` (CALL_CORE)


**Purpose**


* Global label definitions.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `key text not null` CHECK in (`vip`,`spam`,`urgent`,`blocked`,`unknown`)
* `display_name text not null`
* `created_at`


**Constraints**


* `unique(key)`


**Retention**


* CALL_CORE


---


### 14.2.14 `calls` (CALL_CORE)


**Purpose**


* Central call record. Must exist even if downstream fails.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `source_type text not null` CHECK in (`dedicated_number`,`forwarded`)
* `direction text not null default 'inbound'` CHECK in (`inbound`)
* `status text not null` CHECK in (`created`,`in_progress`,`completed`,`partial`,`failed`)
* `started_at timestamptz not null`
* `ended_at timestamptz null`
* `duration_seconds int null`
* Caller identity:


  * `caller_phone_ciphertext bytea not null`
  * `caller_phone_nonce bytea not null`
  * `caller_phone_key_version int not null`
  * `caller_phone_hash char(64) not null`
  * `caller_phone_last4 varchar(4) not null`
  * `caller_display text null` (derived safe display name)
* Provider linkage:


  * `provider text not null` CHECK in (`twilio`)
  * `provider_call_sid text not null`
  * `provider_parent_call_sid text null` (forwarding correlation)
* Forwarding diagnostics:


  * `forwarding_detected boolean not null default false`
  * `forwarding_verification_hint text null`
* Screening outputs (lightweight fields for list view):


  * `reason_short text null`
  * `urgency_level text not null default 'unknown'` CHECK in (`vip`,`urgent`,`normal`,`unknown`)
  * `best_callback_time text null`
* Handoff timeline:


  * `handoff_offered_at timestamptz null`
  * `handoff_offer_reason text null` CHECK in (`vip`,`urgent`,`vip_and_urgent`)
  * `handoff_accepted_at timestamptz null`
  * `handoff_accepted_device_id uuid null fk devices(id)`
  * `handoff_result text null` CHECK in (`success`,`failed`,`expired`,`ignored`)
* Retention:


  * `retention_expires_at timestamptz not null`
  * `deleted_at timestamptz null` (soft delete marker for per-call delete)
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* `unique(provider, provider_call_sid)`
* `caller_phone_hash` indexed for list matching.
* If ended_at is not null then duration_seconds must be >= 0.
* If status in (`completed`,`partial`,`failed`) then ended_at must be not null.
* If deleted_at is not null then status must remain terminal.


**Indexes**


* Primary list view:


  * `calls_user_started_idx` on (owner_user_id, started_at desc)
* Status filtering:


  * `calls_user_status_idx` on (owner_user_id, status, started_at desc)
* Retention job:


  * `calls_retention_idx` on (retention_expires_at) where deleted_at is null
* Provider lookups:


  * `calls_provider_sid_idx` on (provider_call_sid)
* Caller lookups:


  * `calls_user_caller_hash_idx` on (owner_user_id, caller_phone_hash)


**Retention**


* CALL_CORE


**Encryption**


* Caller phone must be application-layer encrypted.


---


### 14.2.15 `call_events` (CALL_CORE)


**Purpose**


* Append-only timeline for call state transitions and significant events.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `call_id uuid not null fk calls(id) on delete cascade`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `event_type text not null` CHECK in (
  `call_started`,`call_answered`,`screening_started`,`screening_completed`,
  `handoff_offered`,`handoff_accepted`,`handoff_failed`,`call_ended`,
  `transcript_ready`,`summary_ready`,`recording_ready`,
  `provider_error`,`system_error`
  )
* `event_at timestamptz not null default now()`
* `details jsonb null` (must not include full transcript)
* `created_at`


**Indexes**


* `call_events_call_idx` on (call_id, event_at asc)
* `call_events_user_idx` on (owner_user_id, event_at desc)


**Retention**


* CALL_CORE


---


### 14.2.16 `call_transcripts` (CALL_CONTENT)


**Purpose**


* Transcript artifact per call.


**Columns**


* `call_id uuid pk fk calls(id) on delete cascade`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `status text not null` CHECK in (`pending`,`succeeded`,`failed`,`partial`)
* `transcript_ciphertext bytea null`
* `transcript_nonce bytea null`
* `transcript_key_version int null`
* `provider_job_id text null`
* `failure_reason text null`
* `retry_count int not null default 0`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* If status in (`succeeded`,`partial`) transcript fields must be not null.
* If status = failed then failure_reason must be not null.


**Indexes**


* `call_transcripts_status_idx` on (status)


**Retention**


* CALL_CONTENT


**Encryption**


* Transcript must be application-layer encrypted.


---


### 14.2.17 `call_summaries` (CALL_CONTENT)


**Purpose**


* Summary and structured extraction per call.


**Columns**


* `call_id uuid pk fk calls(id) on delete cascade`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `status text not null` CHECK in (`pending`,`succeeded`,`failed`,`partial`)
* `summary_ciphertext bytea null`
* `summary_nonce bytea null`
* `summary_key_version int null`
* Extracted fields:


  * `caller_name text null`
  * `reason text null`
  * `callback_number_hash char(64) null`
  * `urgency text null` CHECK in (`vip`,`urgent`,`normal`,`unknown`)
  * `best_callback_time text null`
* `failure_reason text null`
* `retry_count int not null default 0`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* If status in (`succeeded`,`partial`) summary fields must be not null.
* If status = failed then failure_reason must be not null.


**Indexes**


* `call_summaries_status_idx` on (status)


**Retention**


* CALL_CONTENT


**Encryption**


* Summary must be application-layer encrypted.


---


### 14.2.18 `call_recordings` (MEDIA)


**Purpose**


* Recording reference per call when enabled.


**Columns**


* `call_id uuid pk fk calls(id) on delete cascade`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `status text not null` CHECK in (`not_recorded`,`pending`,`available`,`failed`,`deleted`)
* `provider_recording_sid text null`
* `s3_key_ciphertext bytea null`
* `s3_key_nonce bytea null`
* `s3_key_key_version int null`
* `content_type text null`
* `byte_size bigint null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Indexes**


* `call_recordings_status_idx` on (status)
* `call_recordings_provider_idx` on (provider_recording_sid)


**Retention**


* MEDIA


**Encryption**


* Storage key must be application-layer encrypted.


---


### 14.2.19 `call_notes` (CALL_CONTENT)


**Purpose**


* User notes attached to calls.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `call_id uuid not null fk calls(id) on delete cascade`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `note_ciphertext bytea not null`
* `note_nonce bytea not null`
* `note_key_version int not null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Indexes**


* `call_notes_call_idx` on (call_id, created_at asc)


**Retention**


* CALL_CONTENT


**Encryption**


* Note content must be application-layer encrypted.


---


### 14.2.20 `call_actions` (CALL_CORE)


**Purpose**


* Track follow-up actions and outcomes.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `call_id uuid not null fk calls(id) on delete cascade`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `action_type text not null` CHECK in (`callback`,`text_draft_created`,`text_sent`,`reminder_created`,`mark_spam`,`mark_vip`,`block_number`)
* `status text not null` CHECK in (`created`,`completed`,`failed`)
* `details jsonb null` (no message bodies)
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Indexes**


* `call_actions_call_idx` on (call_id, created_at asc)
* `call_actions_user_idx` on (owner_user_id, created_at desc)


**Retention**


* CALL_CORE


---


### 14.2.21 `call_labels` (CALL_CORE)


**Purpose**


* Apply labels to calls.


**Columns**


* `call_id uuid not null fk calls(id) on delete cascade`
* `label_id uuid not null fk labels(id)`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `applied_by text not null` CHECK in (`system`,`user`,`admin`)
* `applied_reason text null`
* `applied_at timestamptz not null default now()`


**Constraints**


* Primary key: `(call_id, label_id)`


**Indexes**


* `call_labels_user_label_idx` on (owner_user_id, label_id, applied_at desc)


**Retention**


* CALL_CORE


---


### 14.2.22 `vip_entries` (LISTS)


**Purpose**


* User VIP list for importance and behavior triggers.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `phone_ciphertext bytea not null`
* `phone_nonce bytea not null`
* `phone_key_version int not null`
* `phone_hash char(64) not null`
* `phone_last4 varchar(4) not null`
* `display_name text null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* `unique(owner_user_id, phone_hash)`


**Indexes**


* `vip_entries_user_phone_idx` on (owner_user_id, phone_hash)


**Retention**


* LISTS


**Encryption**


* Phone must be encrypted.


---


### 14.2.23 `block_entries` (LISTS)


**Purpose**


* User block list.


**Columns**


* Same structure as vip_entries plus:


  * `reason text null`


**Constraints**


* `unique(owner_user_id, phone_hash)`


**Retention**


* LISTS


**Encryption**


* Phone must be encrypted.


---


### 14.2.24 `memory_items` (CALL_CONTENT)


**Purpose**


* Memory records used by the assistant, visible and deletable.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `source_call_id uuid null fk calls(id) on delete set null`
* `caller_phone_hash char(64) null`
* `memory_type text not null` CHECK in (`caller_preference`,`user_preference`,`note`)
* `content_ciphertext bytea not null`
* `content_nonce bytea not null`
* `content_key_version int not null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Indexes**


* `memory_items_user_idx` on (owner_user_id, created_at desc)
* `memory_items_user_caller_hash_idx` on (owner_user_id, caller_phone_hash)


**Retention**


* CALL_CONTENT (or a dedicated MEMORY category if you prefer)


**Encryption**


* Memory content must be encrypted.


---


### 14.2.25 `reminders` (CALL_CORE)


**Purpose**


* Reminder scheduling and status.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `call_id uuid null fk calls(id) on delete set null`
* `due_at timestamptz not null`
* `status text not null` CHECK in (`pending`,`fired`,`cancelled`,`failed`)
* `title text null` (short safe text)
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Indexes**


* `reminders_user_due_idx` on (owner_user_id, due_at asc)
* `reminders_due_pending_idx` on (due_at asc) where status = 'pending'


**Retention**


* CALL_CORE


**Encryption**


* If you allow reminder free-text content beyond a short title, encrypt it.


---


### 14.2.26 `outbound_messages` (MESSAGING)


**Purpose**


* Outbound SMS record linked to a call or standalone, approval-first.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `call_id uuid null fk calls(id) on delete set null`
* `to_phone_ciphertext bytea not null`
* `to_phone_nonce bytea not null`
* `to_phone_key_version int not null`
* `to_phone_hash char(64) not null`
* `to_phone_last4 varchar(4) not null`
* `status text not null` CHECK in (`draft`,`awaiting_approval`,`approved`,`sending`,`sent`,`failed`,`cancelled`)
* Provider linkage:


  * `provider text not null` CHECK in (`twilio`)
  * `provider_message_sid text null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Constraints**


* `unique(provider, provider_message_sid)` where provider_message_sid is not null


**Indexes**


* `outbound_messages_user_idx` on (owner_user_id, created_at desc)
* `outbound_messages_call_idx` on (call_id)
* `outbound_messages_user_to_hash_idx` on (owner_user_id, to_phone_hash)


**Retention**


* MESSAGING


**Encryption**


* Recipient phone must be encrypted.


---


### 14.2.27 `message_drafts` (MESSAGING)


**Purpose**


* Store AI draft text and final approved text versions.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `message_id uuid not null fk outbound_messages(id) on delete cascade`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `draft_version int not null`
* `draft_ciphertext bytea not null`
* `draft_nonce bytea not null`
* `draft_key_version int not null`
* `created_by text not null` CHECK in (`ai`,`user`)
* `created_at`


**Constraints**


* `unique(message_id, draft_version)`


**Indexes**


* `message_drafts_message_idx` on (message_id, draft_version desc)


**Retention**


* MESSAGING


**Encryption**


* Draft content must be encrypted.


---


### 14.2.28 `approvals` (MESSAGING)


**Purpose**


* Approval records for outbound messages.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `message_id uuid not null fk outbound_messages(id) on delete cascade`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `status text not null` CHECK in (`approved`,`rejected`)
* `approved_at timestamptz not null default now()`
* `approved_by_device_id uuid null fk devices(id)`
* `content_hash char(64) not null` (hash of the approved draft ciphertext or plaintext before encryption, consistent choice)


**Indexes**


* `approvals_message_idx` on (message_id)


**Retention**


* MESSAGING


---


### 14.2.29 `audit_events` (AUDIT)


**Purpose**


* Immutable security and accountability record.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `actor_type text not null` CHECK in (`user`,`admin`,`system`,`service`)
* `actor_id uuid null` (device_id for user actions, admin_user_id for admin actions)
* `event_type text not null` (enumerate in code and enforce via CHECK)
* `event_at timestamptz not null default now()`
* `target_type text null`
* `target_id uuid null`
* `ip inet null`
* `user_agent text null`
* `details jsonb null` (must never include transcripts, summaries, message bodies)
* `created_at`


**Indexes**


* `audit_events_user_time_idx` on (owner_user_id, event_at desc)
* `audit_events_type_time_idx` on (event_type, event_at desc)


**Retention**


* AUDIT (recommended retention longer than call content, example 180 to 365 days)


---


### 14.2.30 `provider_events` (OPS)


**Purpose**


* Deduplicate incoming provider webhooks and correlate provider state.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `provider text not null` CHECK in (`twilio`,`elevenlabs`)
* `provider_event_id text null`
* `provider_call_sid text null`
* `event_type text not null`
* `received_at timestamptz not null default now()`
* `processed_at timestamptz null`
* `process_status text not null` CHECK in (`received`,`processed`,`failed`)
* `failure_reason text null`
* `call_id uuid null fk calls(id) on delete set null`
* `owner_user_id uuid null fk users(id) on delete set null`
* `payload_hash char(64) not null`
* `created_at`


**Constraints**


* If provider_event_id is present: `unique(provider, provider_event_id)`
* Else: `unique(provider, event_type, payload_hash)`


**Indexes**


* `provider_events_status_idx` on (process_status, received_at asc)
* `provider_events_call_sid_idx` on (provider_call_sid)


**Retention**


* OPS (shorter retention, example 30 to 90 days)


---


### 14.2.31 `idempotency_keys` (OPS)


**Purpose**


* Prevent duplicates for user-triggered actions and internal workflows.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `scope text not null` (example: `create_call`, `send_sms`, `create_reminder`)
* `idempotency_key text not null`
* `request_hash char(64) not null`
* `response_body jsonb null` (safe summary response only, never sensitive content)
* `status text not null` CHECK in (`started`,`succeeded`,`failed`)
* `created_at`
* `expires_at timestamptz not null`


**Constraints**


* `unique(owner_user_id, scope, idempotency_key)`


**Indexes**


* `idempotency_exp_idx` on (expires_at)


**Retention**


* OPS (short retention, example 7 to 30 days)


---


### 14.2.32 `outbox_events` (OPS)


**Purpose**


* Reliable dispatch of side effects (queue jobs, notifications) without losing events on process crashes.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade`
* `event_type text not null` (example: `call_completed`, `summary_ready`, `reminder_due`)
* `aggregate_type text not null` (example: `call`, `reminder`)
* `aggregate_id uuid not null`
* `payload jsonb not null` (no Restricted text)
* `status text not null` CHECK in (`pending`,`sent`,`failed`)
* `attempt_count int not null default 0`
* `next_attempt_at timestamptz not null default now()`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Indexes**


* `outbox_pending_idx` on (status, next_attempt_at asc)
* `outbox_user_idx` on (owner_user_id, created_at desc)


**Retention**


* OPS (short retention, example 7 to 30 days)


---


### 14.2.33 `admin_users` (SECURITY)


**Purpose**


* Internal portal identities.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `email citext unique not null`
* `status text not null` CHECK in (`active`,`locked`,`deleted`)
* `totp_secret_ciphertext bytea not null`
* `totp_secret_nonce bytea not null`
* `totp_secret_key_version int not null`
* `created_at`, `updated_at`


**Retention**


* SECURITY


**Encryption**


* TOTP secret must be encrypted.


---


### 14.2.34 `admin_roles` and `admin_user_roles` (SECURITY)


**Purpose**


* RBAC assignments.


`admin_roles`


* `id uuid pk`
* `key text unique not null` CHECK in (`admin`,`support`,`viewer`)
* `created_at`


`admin_user_roles`


* `admin_user_id uuid not null fk admin_users(id) on delete cascade`
* `role_id uuid not null fk admin_roles(id)`
* `assigned_at timestamptz not null default now()`
* Primary key: `(admin_user_id, role_id)`


**Retention**


* SECURITY


---


### 14.2.35 `admin_sessions` (SECURITY)


**Purpose**


* Admin portal sessions with strong revoke.


**Columns**


* Similar to `sessions` but scoped to admin_users:


  * `admin_user_id uuid not null fk admin_users(id) on delete cascade`
  * token hashes and expiry
  * ip and user agent
  * revoked fields


**Retention**


* SECURITY


---


### 14.2.36 `break_glass_events` (AUDIT)


**Purpose**


* Record any elevated access to sensitive user content by admin.


**Columns**


* `id uuid pk`
* `admin_user_id uuid not null fk admin_users(id)`
* `target_user_id uuid not null fk users(id)`
* `target_call_id uuid null fk calls(id)`
* `reason_code text not null`
* `justification text not null`
* `granted_at timestamptz not null default now()`
* `expires_at timestamptz not null`
* `revoked_at timestamptz null`
* `created_at`


**Indexes**


* `break_glass_admin_time_idx` on (admin_user_id, granted_at desc)
* `break_glass_target_user_idx` on (target_user_id, granted_at desc)


**Retention**


* AUDIT


---


## 14.2.37 Encryption requirements summary


### What must be encrypted at the application layer (Restricted)


* Call transcripts: `call_transcripts.transcript_*`
* Call summaries: `call_summaries.summary_*`
* Call notes: `call_notes.note_*`
* Memory items: `memory_items.content_*`
* Outbound message drafts: `message_drafts.draft_*`
* Handoff destination phone: `user_settings.handoff_target_phone_*`
* Recording storage keys: `call_recordings.s3_key_*`
* All stored phone numbers for callers, contacts, VIP and block lists:


  * ciphertext + nonce + key_version
  * plus a hash for lookups


### What can rely on DB-level encryption at rest (Confidential)


* User email (citext), device metadata, call timestamps, provider IDs
* These must still be protected by strict authorization and must never be logged with sensitive context.


### Key versioning


Every encrypted column set includes `*_key_version` so you can rotate keys:


* New writes use the latest version.
* Reads support older versions.
* Background rotation jobs can re-encrypt gradually if required.


---


## 14.3 Migration strategy


### 14.3.1 Versioned migrations


* Use Alembic migrations (Python) as the single source of truth.
* Every schema change is an explicit migration with:


  * forward upgrade
  * tested downgrade where feasible


### 14.3.2 Safe rollout rules


* Additive changes first:


  * add columns nullable
  * deploy code that writes both old and new if needed
  * backfill
  * then enforce NOT NULL and drop old fields later
* Never drop columns in the same deployment that stops writing them.


### 14.3.3 Enum evolution rule


Avoid Postgres ENUM types if you want easy rollback.
Preferred:


* `text` columns with CHECK constraints for allowed values.
* Update CHECK constraints via migrations when adding states.


### 14.3.4 Index creation strategy


* Use `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` for large tables in production.
* Always add the most common query indexes early:


  * call list and filtering indexes
  * provider sid lookups
  * retention job index


### 14.3.5 Rollback strategy


* Rollbacks must be possible for:


  * application deployments
  * schema deployments
* Schema rollback policy:


  * Always safe for additive migrations.
  * For destructive migrations (rare in Phase 1), require:


    * snapshot backup before change
    * explicit approval
    * a tested restore procedure.


---


## 14.4 Schema completeness checklist


Phase 1 schema is complete when it supports end-to-end:


* User signup, verification, MFA, sessions, multi-device revoke
* Dedicated AI number provisioning and forwarding configuration
* Call record creation for every inbound call
* Transcript, summary, recording artifacts with retry states
* Labels, VIP and block lists, spam markers
* Live handoff timeline fields and multi-device arbitration storage
* Approval-first texting with drafts and approvals
* Reminders and due scheduling
* Memory storage plus user-visible deletion
* Full audit events plus break-glass logging
* Provider webhook dedupe and idempotency for retries
* Retention and deletion execution driven by `retention_expires_at` and soft-delete markers
t from 2026.
Chapter 14: Database Schema Specification
14.0 Schema goals and rules
14.0.1 Goals
                              * Store all Phase 1 objects required for inbound AI calls, forwarding, live handoff, approval-first texting, reminders, memory, privacy controls, and auditing.
                              * Enforce strict per-user isolation at the data model level.
                              * Support reliable retries and deduplication for provider webhooks and background jobs.
                              * Make retention and deletion enforceable and verifiable.
14.0.2 Core rules (non-negotiable)
                              * Every user-owned row has owner_user_id.
                              * Every sensitive content field is either encrypted at the application layer or explicitly classified as Restricted and protected by strict access controls.
                              * Call deletion must cascade to all call artifacts.
                              * Account deletion must be able to purge all user data.
                              * Provider events must be idempotent.
14.0.3 Postgres prerequisites
Enable extensions:
                              * pgcrypto (UUID generation, hashes)
                              * citext (case-insensitive email)
Optional later:
                              * pg_trgm (search performance if you add search)
Use timestamptz everywhere.
________________

________________
14.2 Table-by-table specification
14.2.0 Common column conventions
Primary key
                                 * id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()
Ownership
                                 * owner_user_id uuid not null references users(id)
Timestamps
                                 * created_at timestamptz not null default now()
                                 * updated_at timestamptz not null default now() (maintain via app or trigger)
Encryption column naming
For sensitive fields that require application-layer encryption:
                                 * <field>_ciphertext bytea not null
                                 * <field>_nonce bytea not null
                                 * <field>_key_version int not null
For searchable sensitive identifiers (phone numbers):
                                 * <field>_hash char(64) not null (hex SHA-256)
                                 * <field>_last4 varchar(4) null
Retention category
Each table is tagged with a retention category used by deletion jobs:
                                    * ACCOUNT: user account core
                                    * SECURITY: auth, MFA, sessions
                                    * SETTINGS: user settings
                                    * TELEPHONY: number mappings, forwarding
                                    * CALL_CORE: call metadata and state
                                    * CALL_CONTENT: transcript, summary, memory content derived from calls
                                    * MEDIA: recordings references
                                    * MESSAGING: drafts, approvals, outbound SMS logs
                                    * LISTS: VIP, block, spam markers
                                    * NOTIFICATIONS: push tokens and delivery attempts
                                    * AUDIT: audit trail
                                    * OPS: provider events, idempotency, outbox
________________


14.2.1 users (ACCOUNT)
Purpose
                                    * Canonical personal user account.
Columns
                                    * id uuid pk
                                    * email citext unique null (null allowed if OAuth-only, but recommended to always store email)
                                    * email_verified boolean not null default false
                                    * status text not null with CHECK in (active,pending_verification,locked,deleted)
                                    * display_name text null
                                    * default_timezone text not null (IANA string)
                                    * language_code text not null default 'en'
                                    * deleted_at timestamptz null
                                    * created_at, updated_at
Constraints
                                    * Unique email when not null.
                                    * CHECK that deleted_at is not null only when status is deleted.
Indexes
                                    * users_email_idx on (email)
                                    * users_status_idx on (status)
Retention
                                    * ACCOUNT
Encryption
                                    * None required for base account fields beyond DB-level encryption, but any optional sensitive profile fields added later should follow encryption rules.
________________


14.2.2 auth_identities (SECURITY)
Purpose
                                    * Link login methods to a user (email-password, Google, Apple).
Columns
                                    * id uuid pk
                                    * owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                    * provider text not null CHECK in (email_password,google,apple)
                                    * provider_subject text not null (OAuth sub, or email for email-password identity)
                                    * provider_email citext null
                                    * provider_email_verified boolean null
                                    * created_at, updated_at
Constraints
                                    * Unique per provider subject:
                                    * unique(provider, provider_subject)
                                    * For provider = email_password:
                                    * provider_subject must equal the user email
Indexes
                                    * auth_identities_user_idx on (owner_user_id)
                                    * auth_identities_provider_sub_idx on (provider, provider_subject)
Retention
                                    * SECURITY
Encryption
                                    * Provider subject is not encrypted because it is required for login lookup, but it is Confidential and must never be logged in plaintext.
________________


14.2.3 mfa_methods (SECURITY)
Purpose
                                    * Store enabled MFA methods for a user.
Columns
                                    * id uuid pk
                                    * owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                    * method_type text not null CHECK in (totp,email_otp)
                                    * totp_secret_ciphertext bytea null
                                    * totp_secret_nonce bytea null
                                    * totp_secret_key_version int null
                                    * is_primary boolean not null default false
                                    * enabled_at timestamptz null
                                    * disabled_at timestamptz null
                                    * created_at, updated_at
Constraints
                                    * Only one enabled primary method per user:
                                    * partial unique index on (owner_user_id) where is_primary = true and disabled_at is null
                                    * If method_type = totp:
                                    * totp secret fields must be not null when enabled
Indexes
                                    * mfa_methods_user_idx on (owner_user_id)
Retention
                                    * SECURITY
Encryption
                                    * TOTP secret must be application-layer encrypted.
________________


14.2.4 recovery_codes (SECURITY)
Purpose
                                    * One-time recovery codes for MFA recovery.
Columns
                                    * id uuid pk
                                    * owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                    * code_hash char(64) not null
                                    * used_at timestamptz null
                                    * created_at
Constraints
                                    * unique(owner_user_id, code_hash)
Indexes
                                    * recovery_codes_user_idx on (owner_user_id)
Retention
                                    * SECURITY
Encryption
                                    * Store only hashes, never plaintext codes.
________________


14.2.5 devices (SECURITY)
Purpose
                                    * Multi-device support, push delivery, revoke.
Columns
                                    * id uuid pk
                                    * owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                    * platform text not null CHECK in (ios,android,web)
                                    * device_name text null
                                    * app_version text null
                                    * os_version text null
                                    * last_seen_at timestamptz null
                                    * revoked_at timestamptz null
                                    * revoke_reason text null
                                    * created_at, updated_at
Constraints
                                    * None beyond FK.
Indexes
                                    * devices_user_idx on (owner_user_id)
                                    * devices_user_active_idx on (owner_user_id) where revoked_at is null
Retention
                                    * SECURITY
Encryption
                                    * None.
________________


14.2.6 sessions (SECURITY)
Purpose
                                    * Per-device sessions, token rotation, revoke.
Columns
                                    * id uuid pk
                                    * owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                    * device_id uuid not null fk devices(id) on delete cascade
                                    * access_token_id uuid not null (opaque token identifier)
                                    * access_token_hash char(64) not null
                                    * access_expires_at timestamptz not null
                                    * refresh_token_hash char(64) not null
                                    * refresh_expires_at timestamptz not null
                                    * last_refresh_at timestamptz null
                                    * revoked_at timestamptz null
                                    * revoke_reason text null
                                    * ip_created inet null
                                    * ip_last inet null
                                    * user_agent text null
                                    * created_at, updated_at
Constraints
                                    * unique(access_token_hash)
                                    * unique(refresh_token_hash)
Indexes
                                    * sessions_user_idx on (owner_user_id)
                                    * sessions_device_idx on (device_id)
                                    * sessions_active_by_device_idx on (device_id) where revoked_at is null
                                    * sessions_refresh_exp_idx on (refresh_expires_at)
Retention
                                    * SECURITY
Encryption
                                    * Store only hashes, never plaintext tokens.
________________


14.2.7 push_tokens (NOTIFICATIONS)
Purpose
                                    * Track push tokens per device (rotate and revoke cleanly).
Columns
                                    * id uuid pk
                                    * device_id uuid not null fk devices(id) on delete cascade
                                    * provider text not null CHECK in (fcm)
                                    * token text not null
                                    * created_at
                                    * revoked_at timestamptz null
Constraints
                                    * unique(provider, token)
Indexes
                                    * push_tokens_device_idx on (device_id)
Retention
                                    * NOTIFICATIONS
Encryption
                                    * Token is Confidential. Optionally encrypt token if you want stronger DB compromise resistance.
________________


14.2.8 user_settings (SETTINGS)
Purpose
                                    * Store settings used during call handling and privacy enforcement.
Columns
                                    * owner_user_id uuid pk fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                    * privacy_notifications boolean not null default true
                                    * quiet_hours_enabled boolean not null default true
                                    * quiet_hours_start time null
                                    * quiet_hours_end time null
                                    * timezone text not null
                                    * memory_enabled boolean not null default true
                                    * retention_days smallint not null default 90
                                    * biometric_policy text not null CHECK in (gate_app_open,gate_call_details,gate_recordings,gate_all_sensitive)
                                    * important_rule text not null CHECK in (vip_only,urgent_only,vip_and_urgent)
                                    * call_objective text not null CHECK in (screen_and_summarize,take_message_only,determine_urgency_only)
                                    * call_duration_limit_minutes smallint not null default 20
                                    * vip_call_duration_limit_minutes smallint not null default 30
                                    * recording_enabled boolean not null default false
                                    * transcript_disclosure_mode text not null CHECK in (ai_says_it,legal_text_only)
                                    * recording_announcement_required boolean not null default true
                                    * handoff_enabled boolean not null default true
                                    * handoff_trigger text not null CHECK in (vip_only,urgent_only,vip_and_urgent)
                                    * handoff_target_type text not null CHECK in (real_phone_number)
                                    * handoff_target_phone_ciphertext bytea null
                                    * handoff_target_phone_nonce bytea null
                                    * handoff_target_phone_key_version int null
                                    * created_at, updated_at
Constraints
                                    * If handoff_enabled = true and target_type = real_phone_number, target encrypted fields must be not null.
                                    * retention_days CHECK between 7 and 365 (recommended safety bound).
Indexes
                                    * None required beyond PK.
Retention
                                    * SETTINGS
Encryption
                                    * Handoff destination phone must be application-layer encrypted.
________________


14.2.9 user_numbers (TELEPHONY)
Purpose
                                    * Dedicated inbound AI number per user, provider mapping.
Columns
                                    * id uuid pk
                                    * owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                    * ai_number_e164 text not null (E.164)
                                    * ai_number_hash char(64) not null
                                    * ai_number_last4 varchar(4) not null
                                    * provider text not null CHECK in (twilio)
                                    * provider_number_sid text not null
                                    * status text not null CHECK in (active,pending,suspended)
                                    * provisioned_at timestamptz null
                                    * created_at, updated_at
Constraints
                                    * unique(owner_user_id) for Phase 1 (one AI number per user)
                                    * unique(provider, provider_number_sid)
                                    * unique(ai_number_hash) to avoid accidental reuse
Indexes
                                    * user_numbers_user_idx on (owner_user_id)
                                    * user_numbers_hash_idx on (ai_number_hash)
Retention
                                    * TELEPHONY
Encryption
                                    * AI number can be stored plaintext because it is not the user’s personal number, but it still identifies the user’s service number and is Confidential.
________________

                                    * forward_on_unreachable boolean not null default true
                                    * verification_status text not null CHECK in (unverified,verified,failed)
                                    * last_verified_at timestamptz null
                                    * created_at, updated_at
Constraints
                                    * At least one of busy or unreachable must be true when enabled.
Retention
                                    * TELEPHONY
________________
