14.2.14 calls (CALL_CORE)
Purpose
                                    * Central call record. Must exist even if downstream fails.
Columns
                                    * id uuid pk
                                    * owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                    * source_type text not null CHECK in (dedicated_number,forwarded)
                                    * direction text not null default 'inbound' CHECK in (inbound)
                                    * status text not null CHECK in (created,in_progress,completed,partial,failed)
                                    * started_at timestamptz not null
                                    * ended_at timestamptz null
                                    * duration_seconds int null
                                    * Caller identity:
                                    * caller_phone_ciphertext bytea not null
                                    * caller_phone_nonce bytea not null
                                    * caller_phone_key_version int not null
                                    * caller_phone_hash char(64) not null
                                    * caller_phone_last4 varchar(4) not null
                                    * caller_display text null (derived safe display name)
                                    * Provider linkage:
                                    * provider text not null CHECK in (twilio)
                                    * provider_call_sid text not null
                                    * provider_parent_call_sid text null (forwarding correlation)
                                    * Forwarding diagnostics:
                                    * forwarding_detected boolean not null default false
                                    * forwarding_verification_hint text null
                                    * Screening outputs (lightweight fields for list view):
                                    * reason_short text null
                                    * urgency_level text not null default 'unknown' CHECK in (vip,urgent,normal,unknown)
                                    * best_callback_time text null
                                    * Handoff timeline:
                                    * handoff_offered_at timestamptz null
                                    * handoff_offer_reason text null CHECK in (vip,urgent,vip_and_urgent)
                                    * handoff_accepted_at timestamptz null
                                    * handoff_accepted_device_id uuid null fk devices(id)
                                    * handoff_result text null CHECK in (success,failed,expired,ignored)
                                    * Retention:
                                    * retention_expires_at timestamptz not null
                                    * deleted_at timestamptz null (soft delete marker for per-call delete)
                                    * created_at, updated_at
Constraints
                                    * unique(provider, provider_call_sid)
                                    * caller_phone_hash indexed for list matching.
                                    * If ended_at is not null then duration_seconds must be >= 0.
                                    * If status in (completed,partial,failed) then ended_at must be not null.
                                    * If deleted_at is not null then status must remain terminal.
Indexes
                                    * Primary list view:
                                    * calls_user_started_idx on (owner_user_id, started_at desc)
                                    * Status filtering:
                                    * calls_user_status_idx on (owner_user_id, status, started_at desc)
                                    * Retention job:
                                    * calls_retention_idx on (retention_expires_at) where deleted_at is null
                                    * Provider lookups:
                                    * calls_provider_sid_idx on (provider_call_sid)
                                    * Caller lookups:
                                    * calls_user_caller_hash_idx on (owner_user_id, caller_phone_hash)
Retention
                                    * CALL_CORE
Encryption
                                    * Caller phone must be application-layer encrypted.
________________


14.2.15 call_events (CALL_CORE)
Purpose
                                    * Append-only timeline for call state transitions and significant events.
Columns
                                    * id uuid pk
                                    * call_id uuid not null fk calls(id) on delete cascade
                                    * owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                    * event_type text not null CHECK in (
call_started,call_answered,screening_started,screening_completed,
handoff_offered,handoff_accepted,handoff_failed,call_ended,
transcript_ready,summary_ready,recording_ready,
provider_error,system_error
)
                                    * event_at timestamptz not null default now()
                                    * details jsonb null (must not include full transcript)
                                    * created_at
Indexes
                                       * call_events_call_idx on (call_id, event_at asc)
                                       * call_events_user_idx on (owner_user_id, event_at desc)
Retention
                                       * CALL_CORE
________________


14.2.16 call_transcripts (CALL_CONTENT)

                                       * Transcript artifact per call.
Columns
                                       * call_id uuid pk fk calls(id) on delete cascade
                                       * owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                       * status text not null CHECK in (pending,succeeded,failed,partial)
                                       * transcript_ciphertext bytea null
                                       * transcript_nonce bytea null
                                       * transcript_key_version int null
                                       * provider_job_id text null
                                       * failure_reason text null
                                       * retry_count int not null default 0
                                       * created_at, updated_at
Constraints
                                       * If status in (succeeded,partial) transcript fields must be not null.
                                       * If status = failed then failure_reason must be not null.
Indexes
                                       * call_transcripts_status_idx on (status)
Retention
                                       * CALL_CONTENT
Encryption
                                       * Transcript must be application-layer encrypted.

14.2.17 call_summaries (CALL_CONTENT)

Columns
                                       * call_id uuid pk fk calls(id) on delete cascade
                                       * owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                       * status text not null CHECK in (pending,succeeded,failed,partial)
                                       * summary_ciphertext bytea null
                                       * summary_nonce bytea null
                                       * summary_key_version int null
                                       * Extracted fields:
                                       * caller_name text null
                                       * reason text null
                                       * callback_number_hash char(64) null
                                       * urgency text null CHECK in (vip,urgent,normal,unknown)
                                       * best_callback_time text null
                                       * failure_reason text null
                                       * retry_count int not null default 0
                                       * created_at, updated_at
Constraints
                                       * If status in (succeeded,partial) summary fields must be not null.
                                       * If status = failed then failure_reason must be not null.
Indexes
                                       * call_summaries_status_idx on (status)
Retention
                                       * CALL_CONTENT
Encryption
                                       * Summary must be application-layer encrypted.

                                       * to_phone_key_version int not null
                                       * to_phone_hash char(64) not null
                                       * to_phone_last4 varchar(4) not null
                                       * status text not null CHECK in (draft,awaiting_approval,approved,sending,sent,failed,cancelled)
                                       * Provider linkage:
                                       * provider text not null CHECK in (twilio)
                                       * provider_message_sid text null
                                       * created_at, updated_at
Constraints
                                       * unique(provider, provider_message_sid) where provider_message_sid is not null
Indexes
                                       * outbound_messages_user_idx on (owner_user_id, created_at desc)

Retention
                                       * MESSAGING

14.2.29 audit_events (AUDIT)
Purpose
                                       * Immutable security and accountability record.
Columns
                                       * id uuid pk
                                       * owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                       * actor_type text not null CHECK in (user,admin,system,service)
                                       * actor_id uuid null (device_id for user actions, admin_user_id for admin actions)
                                       * event_type text not null (enumerate in code and enforce via CHECK)
                                       * event_at timestamptz not null default now()
                                       * target_type text null
                                       * target_id uuid null
                                       * ip inet null
                                       * user_agent text null
                                       * details jsonb null (must never include transcripts, summaries, message bodies)
                                       * created_at
Indexes
                                       * audit_events_user_time_idx on (owner_user_id, event_at desc)
                                       * audit_events_type_time_idx on (event_type, event_at desc)
Retention
                                       * AUDIT (recommended retention longer than call content, example 180 to 365 days)
________________


14.2.30 provider_events (OPS)
Purpose
                                       * Deduplicate incoming provider webhooks and correlate provider state.
Columns
                                       * id uuid pk
                                       * provider text not null CHECK in (twilio,elevenlabs)
                                       * provider_event_id text null
                                       * provider_call_sid text null
                                       * event_type text not null
                                       * received_at timestamptz not null default now()
                                       * processed_at timestamptz null
                                       * process_status text not null CHECK in (received,processed,failed)
                                       * failure_reason text null
                                       * call_id uuid null fk calls(id) on delete set null
                                       * owner_user_id uuid null fk users(id) on delete set null
                                       * payload_hash char(64) not null
                                       * created_at
Constraints
                                       * If provider_event_id is present: unique(provider, provider_event_id)
                                       * Else: unique(provider, event_type, payload_hash)
Indexes
                                       * provider_events_status_idx on (process_status, received_at asc)
                                       * provider_events_call_sid_idx on (provider_call_sid)
Retention
                                       * OPS (shorter retention, example 30 to 90 days)
________________


14.2.31 idempotency_keys (OPS)
Purpose
                                       * Prevent duplicates for user-triggered actions and internal workflows.
Columns
                                       * id uuid pk
                                       * owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                       * scope text not null (example: create_call, send_sms, create_reminder)
                                       * idempotency_key text not null
                                       * request_hash char(64) not null
                                       * response_body jsonb null (safe summary response only, never sensitive content)
                                       * status text not null CHECK in (started,succeeded,failed)
                                       * created_at
                                       * expires_at timestamptz not null
Constraints
                                       * unique(owner_user_id, scope, idempotency_key)
Indexes
                                       * idempotency_exp_idx on (expires_at)
Retention
                                       * OPS (short retention, example 7 to 30 days)
________________


14.2.32 outbox_events (OPS)
Purpose
                                       * Reliable dispatch of side effects (queue jobs, notifications) without losing events on process crashes.
Columns
                                       * id uuid pk
                                       * owner_user_id uuid not null fk users(id) on delete cascade
                                       * event_type text not null (example: call_completed, summary_ready, reminder_due)
                                       * aggregate_type text not null (example: call, reminder)
                                       * aggregate_id uuid not null
                                       * payload jsonb not null (no Restricted text)
                                       * status text not null CHECK in (pending,sent,failed)
                                       * attempt_count int not null default 0
                                       * next_attempt_at timestamptz not null default now()
                                       * created_at, updated_at
Indexes
                                       * outbox_pending_idx on (status, next_attempt_at asc)
                                       * outbox_user_idx on (owner_user_id, created_at desc)
Retention
                                       * OPS (short retention, example 7 to 30 days)
________________


14.2.33 admin_users (SECURITY)
Purpose
                                       * Internal portal identities.
Columns
                                       * id uuid pk
                                       * email citext unique not null
                                       * status text not null CHECK in (active,locked,deleted)
                                       * totp_secret_ciphertext bytea not null
                                       * totp_secret_nonce bytea not null
                                       * totp_secret_key_version int not null
                                       * created_at, updated_at
Retention
                                       * SECURITY
Encryption
                                       * TOTP secret must be encrypted.
________________


14.2.34 admin_roles and admin_user_roles (SECURITY)
Purpose
                                       * RBAC assignments.
admin_roles
                                       * id uuid pk
                                       * key text unique not null CHECK in (admin,support,viewer)
                                       * created_at
admin_user_roles
                                       * admin_user_id uuid not null fk admin_users(id) on delete cascade
                                       * role_id uuid not null fk admin_roles(id)
                                       * assigned_at timestamptz not null default now()
                                       * Primary key: (admin_user_id, role_id)
Retention
                                       * SECURITY
________________


14.2.35 admin_sessions (SECURITY)
Purpose
                                       * Admin portal sessions with strong revoke.
Columns
                                       * Similar to sessions but scoped to admin_users:
                                       * admin_user_id uuid not null fk admin_users(id) on delete cascade
                                       * token hashes and expiry
                                       * ip and user agent
                                       * revoked fields
Retention
                                       * SECURITY
________________


14.2.36 break_glass_events (AUDIT)
Purpose
                                       * Record any elevated access to sensitive user content by admin.
Columns
                                       * id uuid pk
                                       * admin_user_id uuid not null fk admin_users(id)
                                       * target_user_id uuid not null fk users(id)
                                       * target_call_id uuid null fk calls(id)
                                       * reason_code text not null
                                       * justification text not null
                                       * granted_at timestamptz not null default now()
                                       * expires_at timestamptz not null
                                       * revoked_at timestamptz null
                                       * created_at
Indexes
                                       * break_glass_admin_time_idx on (admin_user_id, granted_at desc)
                                       * break_glass_target_user_idx on (target_user_id, granted_at desc)
Retention
                                       * AUDIT
________________


14.2.37 Encryption requirements summary
What must be encrypted at the application layer (Restricted)
                                       * Call transcripts: call_transcripts.transcript_*
                                       * Call summaries: call_summaries.summary_*
                                       * Call notes: call_notes.note_*
                                       * Memory items: memory_items.content_*
                                       * Outbound message drafts: message_drafts.draft_*
                                       * Handoff destination phone: user_settings.handoff_target_phone_*
                                       * Recording storage keys: call_recordings.s3_key_*
                                       * All stored phone numbers for callers, contacts, VIP and block lists:
                                       * ciphertext + nonce + key_version
                                       * plus a hash for lookups
What can rely on DB-level encryption at rest (Confidential)
                                       * User email (citext), device metadata, call timestamps, provider IDs
                                       * These must still be protected by strict authorization and must never be logged with sensitive context.
Key versioning
Every encrypted column set includes *_key_version so you can rotate keys:
                                       * New writes use the latest version.
                                       * Reads support older versions.
                                       * Background rotation jobs can re-encrypt gradually if required.
________________


14.3 Migration strategy
14.3.1 Versioned migrations
                                       * Use Alembic migrations (Python) as the single source of truth.
                                       * Every schema change is an explicit migration with:
                                       * forward upgrade
                                       * tested downgrade where feasible
14.3.2 Safe rollout rules
                                       * Additive changes first:
                                       * add columns nullable
                                       * deploy code that writes both old and new if needed
                                       * backfill
                                       * then enforce NOT NULL and drop old fields later
                                       * Never drop columns in the same deployment that stops writing them.
14.3.3 Enum evolution rule
Avoid Postgres ENUM types if you want easy rollback.
Preferred:
                                       * text columns with CHECK constraints for allowed values.
                                       * Update CHECK constraints via migrations when adding states.
14.3.4 Index creation strategy
                                       * Use CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY for large tables in production.
                                       * Always add the most common query indexes early:
                                       * call list and filtering indexes
                                       * provider sid lookups
                                       * retention job index
14.3.5 Rollback strategy
                                       * Rollbacks must be possible for:
                                       * application deployments
                                       * schema deployments
                                       * Schema rollback policy:
                                       * Always safe for additive migrations.
                                       * For destructive migrations (rare in Phase 1), require:
                                       * snapshot backup before change
                                       * explicit approval
                                       * a tested restore procedure.
________________


14.4 Schema completeness checklist
Phase 1 schema is complete when it supports end-to-end:
                                       * User signup, verification, MFA, sessions, multi-device revoke
                                       * Dedicated AI number provisioning and forwarding configuration
                                       * Call record creation for every inbound call
                                       * Transcript, summary, recording artifacts with retry states
                                       * Labels, VIP and block lists, spam markers
                                       * Live handoff timeline fields and multi-device arbitration storage
                                       * Approval-first texting with drafts and approvals
                                       * Reminders and due scheduling
                                       * Memory storage plus user-visible deletion
                                       * Full audit events plus break-glass logging
                                       * Provider webhook dedupe and idempotency for retries
                                       * Retention and deletion execution driven by retention_expires_at and soft-delete markers
________________


According to a document from 2026.
Chapter 15: Storage and Media Handling
15.0 Scope and updated storage assumption
Phase 1 stores call media and transcripts in ElevenLabs, not in our own object storage. This changes the storage responsibilities:
What we store locally (our system):
                                       * Call metadata and state machine fields (timestamps, status, labels, urgency, forwarding mode, handoff timeline)
                                       * Provider correlation identifiers (Twilio CallSid, ElevenLabs conversation or job identifiers)
                                       * Summary and extracted fields needed for fast UI rendering (recommended to store encrypted in Postgres)
                                       * Operational and compliance records (audit events, idempotency, provider event receipts)
What we do not store locally:
                                       * Full transcript text as a persistent blob
                                       * Full audio recording files as a persistent blob
What we still need storage for:
                                       * Operational logs (ALB logs, WAF logs if enabled, app logs export if you later choose)
                                       * Optional future artifacts (exports, diagnostics bundles) even if Phase 1 does not expose “export my data”
This chapter defines:
                                       * How we structure any S3 usage that remains
                                       * How we securely access ElevenLabs-hosted transcript and media
                                       * How recording and transcript lifecycle is managed when the source of truth is external
________________


15.1 S3 bucket layout (Phase 1 minimal usage)
Even if we are not storing transcripts or recordings, S3 remains useful for operations. Keep the S3 footprint minimal and predictable.
15.1.1 Buckets per environment
Create one S3 bucket per environment:
                                       * mattbot-dev-storage
                                       * mattbot-prod-storage
If you want tighter separation later, split into:
                                       * mattbot-<env>-ops-logs
                                       * mattbot-<env>-app-storage
Phase 1 can use the single-bucket-per-env approach.
15.1.2 Prefix conventions
Use strict prefixes to keep data organized and support lifecycle policies.
Recommended prefix structure:
                                       * ops/alb-access-logs/YYYY/MM/DD/
                                       * ops/waf-logs/YYYY/MM/DD/ (only if enabled)
                                       * ops/debug-bundles/YYYY/MM/DD/ (optional, internal)
                                       * internal/tmp/YYYY/MM/DD/ (short-lived scratch, strongly limited)
Do not create user prefixes for transcripts or recordings in Phase 1 because those are not stored in S3.
15.1.3 Lifecycle policies
Apply lifecycle rules to reduce cost and avoid accidental long-term retention.
Recommended policies:
                                       * ops/alb-access-logs/* expire after 30 to 90 days (choose one value and keep consistent with your audit and incident needs)
                                       * ops/waf-logs/* expire after 30 to 90 days
                                       * internal/tmp/* expire after 7 days
                                       * ops/debug-bundles/* expire after 7 to 30 days
________________


15.2 Object security (S3) and access strategy
15.2.1 No public access, ever
For each bucket:
                                       * Block all public access (S3 “Block Public Access” ON)
                                       * No public ACLs
                                       * No public bucket policies
                                       * Deny any request that is not using TLS
Bucket policy requirements:
                                       * Deny s3:* if aws:SecureTransport is false
                                       * Deny any PutObject that attempts to set a public ACL
15.2.2 Encryption at rest for S3 objects
Use S3-managed encryption (SSE-S3) by default because it is simple and avoids KMS costs.
Rules:
                                       * Enforce default bucket encryption: SSE-S3
                                       * Deny uploads without encryption headers if you want strict enforcement
15.2.3 IAM access model
Only backend compute (EC2 role) should access the bucket.
Principle of least privilege:
                                       * Allow read and write only to required prefixes
                                       * Separate dev and prod roles
                                       * Do not allow wildcard access to all buckets in the account
Example intent (policy behavior, not the exact JSON):
                                       * Dev backend role:
                                       * s3:GetObject only on mattbot-dev-storage/ops/* and mattbot-dev-storage/internal/*
                                       * s3:PutObject only on mattbot-dev-storage/ops/* and mattbot-dev-storage/internal/*
                                       * Prod backend role:
                                       * same pattern but only prod bucket
15.2.4 Signed URL vs proxy strategy (S3)
Because Phase 1 does not store user media in S3, signed URLs are mainly for internal operators or future features. Define the standard now to prevent unsafe patterns later.
Default choice:
                                       * Use backend-minted signed URLs with short TTL for non-sensitive objects.
For anything that could be sensitive in the future:
                                       * Use a backend proxy endpoint that enforces authorization and logs access, then streams the object.
                                       * This avoids leaking object keys and allows tighter control.
Signed URL rules if used:
                                       * TTL: 60 to 300 seconds
                                       * Mint only after auth and RBAC checks
                                       * Never mint URLs directly from the client without the backend
________________


15.3 ElevenLabs transcript and media handling (source of truth external)
In Phase 1, ElevenLabs is the storage system for:
                                       * transcript
                                       * call media (if provided by ElevenLabs for the chosen integration)
Our backend acts as:
                                       * the policy engine
                                       * the access gate
                                       * the cache coordinator (ephemeral only)
                                       * the retention and deletion orchestrator
15.3.1 What is stored in our database
We store references, status, and minimal UI-ready fields, not the media blob.
Minimum fields to store per call:
                                       * provider_call_sid (Twilio)
                                       * elevenlabs_conversation_id or equivalent artifact identifier
                                       * transcript status: pending | available | failed
                                       * recording status: not_recorded | available | failed | deleted
                                       * summary status: pending | available | failed
                                       * summary and extracted fields (recommended to store encrypted in Postgres for fast UI and offline use)
If you want a dedicated table, create:
                                       * call_external_artifacts
                                       * call_id
                                       * provider
                                       * artifact_type (transcript, recording, summary)
                                       * artifact_id
                                       * status
                                       * last_synced_at
                                       * failure_reason
15.3.2 Access model for transcripts and recordings
Clients must never talk directly to ElevenLabs for transcript or recording retrieval. All access goes through our backend so we can enforce:
                                       * user ownership
                                       * biometric gating policy (client side) plus server-side authorization
                                       * audit events for sensitive access (optional for consumer, required for admin break-glass)
Preferred retrieval methods:
Option A: Backend proxy (recommended)
                                       * Client requests /calls/{id}/transcript
                                       * Backend verifies ownership and session
                                       * Backend fetches transcript from ElevenLabs using server credentials
                                       * Backend returns transcript to the client
                                       * Backend may apply response shaping based on privacy settings (example: partial redaction if future policy needs it)
Option B: Backend issues short-lived provider access token or signed URL (only if ElevenLabs supports it cleanly)
                                       * Backend verifies ownership
                                       * Backend requests a time-limited URL from ElevenLabs
                                       * Backend returns the URL to the client with TTL and one-time semantics
Phase 1 default: Option A, backend proxy, because it avoids exposing provider URLs and makes authorization simpler.
15.3.3 Ephemeral caching rules (no persistent transcript storage)
Because transcripts are not stored in our system, we still need to avoid hammering ElevenLabs on repeated UI opens.
Phase 1 caching approach:
                                       * In-memory cache inside the backend process is allowed because Phase 1 is deployed as Docker on a single VM.
                                       * Cache TTL: 2 to 5 minutes
                                       * Cache key: (call_id, artifact_type, artifact_version) if ElevenLabs provides a version, otherwise (call_id, last_synced_at)
                                       * Cache must be cleared on:
                                       * user logout on that device does not matter server-side, but server cache must respect TTL
                                       * per-call delete request
                                       * account delete request
                                       * retention deletion job for the call
Hard rule:
                                       * No transcript content is written to Postgres or S3 as part of caching.
15.3.4 Summary storage decision
Even if ElevenLabs can generate summaries, Phase 1 should store summaries and extracted fields in Postgres because:
                                       * UI needs fast loading for the call list and call details
                                       * Retention and deletion need deterministic control
                                       * Notifications and reminders may need to reference summary-level metadata without refetching from provider
Storage rule:
                                       * Summary text is stored encrypted at the application layer in Postgres.
                                       * Extracted fields used for filtering and badges (urgency, VIP marker) can be stored as plaintext metadata because they are already derived and limited, but they are still Confidential and must be protected by authorization and must not be logged.
If you decide to not store summary text at all:
                                       * The call detail screen must fetch summary from ElevenLabs on demand using the same proxy rules as transcripts.
                                       * You still store summary status and minimal extracted metadata.
Phase 1 recommended: store summary encrypted.
________________


15.4 Recording lifecycle (Phase 1 with external storage)
15.4.1 Default off
Recording is off by default.
When a user enables recording:
                                       * The system must ensure the AI announces recording at the start of the call.
                                       * The setting change must be audited.
15.4.2 Consent announcement rules
When recording is enabled:
                                       * The assistant must announce recording at call start.
                                       * If local laws require stronger consent in some regions, Phase 1 US-only still keeps the announcement enabled as a safe baseline.
Store in DB:
                                       * per-call flag indicating whether announcement was played successfully
                                       * failure reason if the announcement could not be played
15.4.3 Recording availability and failure modes
Because recording is stored externally, recording status must be explicit:
                                       * not_recorded (default)
                                       * pending (call ended, provider artifact not ready)
                                       * available (provider artifact ready)
                                       * failed (provider could not generate or retrieve)
                                       * deleted (we requested deletion and confirmed, or we revoked access)
Failure handling:
                                       * If recording fails but transcript and summary succeed, call record remains valid.
                                       * If both recording and transcript fail, call record still remains valid with summary if available, otherwise a “content unavailable” state.
15.4.4 Playback authorization
Playback must enforce:
                                       * server-side ownership checks
                                       * client-side biometric gate if the user selected a strict biometric policy
Backend flow for playback:
                                       1. Client requests playback for call id
                                       2. Backend checks ownership and session
                                       3. Backend checks call retention status and not deleted
                                       4. Backend fetches recording stream or recording URL from ElevenLabs
                                       5. Backend streams to client or returns a short-lived playback token
Do not expose:
                                       * ElevenLabs permanent URLs
                                       * provider credentials
                                       * raw artifact identifiers in ways that allow enumeration
15.4.5 Deletion behavior
Deletion must affect:
                                       * our database record (call record and artifact status)
                                       * access to the external artifact
Per-call deletion:
                                       * Mark call as deleted in our DB immediately
                                       * Trigger a background job to request deletion from ElevenLabs if their API supports deletion
                                       * If deletion API does not exist, revoke access by:
                                       * removing stored artifact identifiers from our DB
                                       * ensuring the backend will never fetch or serve the artifact again
Account deletion:
                                       * Immediately lock the account and invalidate sessions
                                       * Trigger deletion jobs for all related call records and their external artifact references
                                       * Attempt provider deletion if supported, otherwise revoke access as above
Retention deletion:
                                       * When retention expires for a call, treat it like a deletion job
                                       * Remove local metadata according to retention policy
                                       * Remove external access by deletion request or access revocation
Important rule:
                                       * If external deletion cannot be guaranteed, Phase 1 still guarantees that the user cannot access content through our system after deletion. The artifact identifiers must be removed so retrieval is impossible through our API.
15.4.6 Verified deletion tracking
Because media is external, store deletion tracking records so you can prove the system attempted deletion and know whether it succeeded.
Add a table or fields to track:
                                       * deletion_requested_at
                                       * deletion_completed_at
                                       * deletion_provider_status
                                       * deletion_failure_reason
                                       * attempt_count
________________


15.5 Transcript lifecycle (Phase 1 with external storage)
15.5.1 Transcript states
Track transcript lifecycle without persisting transcript text:
                                       * pending
                                       * available
                                       * failed
                                       * partial
Store:
                                       * status
                                       * failure_reason
                                       * provider artifact id
                                       * last_synced_at
15.5.2 Retrieval rules
Transcript is retrieved on demand through the backend proxy.
Backend must:
                                       * enforce ownership
                                       * enforce retention and deletion checks
                                       * ensure no transcript content is logged
                                       * optionally apply response size limits and pagination if transcripts are long
15.5.3 Partial transcripts
If ElevenLabs returns partial transcripts:
                                       * expose partial transcript with a clear status
                                       * keep retry logic for a limited window
                                       * do not block the call record UI if transcript is partial
________________


15.6 Data encryption checklist for storage and artifacts
15.6.1 Encrypted in Postgres (application-layer)
Even with external transcript storage, these still must be encrypted locally:
                                       * call summaries (if stored)
                                       * call notes
                                       * memory items
                                       * outbound message drafts
                                       * handoff destination phone number
                                       * caller phone number fields (store ciphertext plus hash for matching)
                                       * VIP and block list phone numbers
15.6.2 Not stored locally
                                       * transcript text
                                       * recording audio blob
15.6.3 Encrypted in S3 (SSE-S3)
For operational S3 objects:
                                       * ALB logs and WAF logs are encrypted with SSE-S3
                                       * No public access
                                       * Access restricted to backend role and designated operator roles
________________


15.7 Operational safeguards
15.7.1 Preventing accidental transcript persistence
Add guardrails:
                                       * A unit test that asserts transcript responses are not written to DB models
                                       * Code review checklist item: “No transcript persistence”
                                       * Logging middleware redaction: ensure transcript payloads are redacted by default
15.7.2 Observability without leaking content
When logging transcript or recording failures:
                                       * log only call_id, provider ids, status codes, and error categories
                                       * never log returned transcript text
                                       * never log recording URLs
15.7.3 Backpressure and cost control
Because transcripts and recordings are external, repeated fetches can create cost or throttling risk.
Controls:
                                       * short TTL in-memory caching
                                       * request rate limits per user and per device for transcript and playback endpoints
                                       * clear error messaging for throttled calls
________________


15.8 Completion checklist for Chapter 15
Chapter 15 is complete when:
                                       * S3 buckets exist per environment with public access blocked, SSE-S3 enabled, lifecycle rules applied
                                       * Backend can retrieve transcripts and recordings from ElevenLabs only through server-side authorization
                                       * No transcript or audio blob is persisted to Postgres or S3
                                       * Summary storage decision is implemented (recommended: encrypted summary stored in Postgres)
                                       * Per-call delete, retention delete, and account delete remove local access and trigger provider deletion or access revocation
                                       * Caching exists only in memory with short TTL, and is cleared logically on deletion events
                                       * Logs and error handling never leak transcript text or recording URLs
________________


According to a document from 2026.
Chapter 16: Queueing, Jobs, and Background Processing
16.0 Purpose and principles
Phase 1 must respond to real-time call events quickly and reliably, while still producing durable call records, summaries, labels, notifications, reminders, and retention deletions. The job system exists to move all non-real-time work off the webhook and real-time audio path.
Core principles:
                                       * Webhooks and realtime call paths do the minimum: validate, persist minimal state, enqueue jobs, then return fast.
                                       * All background jobs are idempotent because the queue is at-least-once delivery.
                                       * Every job has a clear ownership scope (owner_user_id) and must never operate outside that scope.
                                       * Every job has explicit retry rules and a terminal failure path (DLQ plus alerting).
                                       * The call record is the source of truth even when artifacts fail.
________________


16.1 SQS queues list
Phase 1 uses SQS Standard queues (at-least-once delivery, duplicates possible). Ordering is not assumed. Correctness is achieved through idempotency keys and state checks in the database.
16.1.1 Queue set (minimal but complete)
                                       1. q-call-postprocess
Purpose:
                                          * All async work that happens after the call ends or after a meaningful call milestone.
Job types carried:
                                          * transcript_sync (status and external id sync only)
                                          * summary_generate (store encrypted summary in DB)
                                          * label_compute (urgent, spam, VIP tagging decisions)
                                          * notify_call_screened (push notification creation and delivery tracking)
                                          * handoff_finalize (store final handoff result states if late callbacks occur)
                                          2. q-messaging
Purpose:
                                             * Approval-first outbound SMS sending and delivery tracking.
Job types carried:
                                             * sms_send (after approval only)
                                             * sms_status_sync (optional, if you reconcile delivery receipts)
                                             3. q-maintenance
Purpose:
                                                * Scheduled or periodic system maintenance tasks that operate on due records.
Job types carried:
                                                * reminder_fire (deliver reminder notifications)
                                                * retention_delete_call (per-call deletion by retention)
                                                * retention_delete_account (account deletion cleanup)
                                                * provider_event_retry (reprocess previously failed provider events)
                                                4. q-notifications
Purpose:
                                                   * Push notification delivery tasks and retry logic.
Job types carried:
                                                   * push_send (send to device tokens)
                                                   * push_retry (retry failed pushes)
If you want fewer queues, you may merge q-notifications into q-call-postprocess and keep only three queues. Keeping a dedicated notifications queue is cleaner because notification retries can create noise and should not block call postprocessing.
16.1.2 Dead-letter queues (DLQs)
Each queue must have a paired DLQ:
                                                   * q-call-postprocess-dlq
                                                   * q-messaging-dlq
                                                   * q-maintenance-dlq
                                                   * q-notifications-dlq
DLQ redrive policy:
                                                   * After maxReceiveCount is exceeded (see 16.4), message is moved to DLQ.
                                                   * DLQ messages trigger immediate alerting and require operator action.
16.1.3 Standard message envelope format
All jobs use a shared envelope so workers are consistent.
Required fields:
                                                   * job_id (uuid)
                                                   * job_type (string)
                                                   * owner_user_id (uuid, required for all user-scoped jobs)
                                                   * created_at (ISO timestamptz)
                                                   * attempt (int, starts at 0)
                                                   * trace_id (string, correlates logs and Sentry)
                                                   * idempotency_scope (string)
                                                   * idempotency_key (string)
                                                   * payload (object)
Optional fields:
                                                   * not_before (ISO timestamptz), used when the producer schedules with delay semantics
                                                   * priority (small int), for internal ordering decisions inside the worker
Payload rules:
                                                   * Payload must never contain Restricted content such as transcript text, recording URLs, or full summary text.
                                                   * Payload may contain identifiers needed to fetch content from providers, such as provider call id, ElevenLabs conversation id, message id, reminder id.
________________


16.2 Job definitions
This section defines the Phase 1 job types, their inputs, expected outputs, state transitions, and how they interact with external systems.
16.2.1 transcript_sync (external transcript status sync only)
Purpose:
                                                   * ElevenLabs stores the transcript. We do not persist transcript text in our database.
                                                   * We must still track transcript availability, failure, and external identifiers so the app can show correct UI states and the backend can proxy transcript retrieval on demand.
Trigger:
                                                   * Enqueued when a call ends, or when ElevenLabs sends a post-call completion webhook, or both.
Input payload:
                                                   * call_id
                                                   * provider_call_sid (Twilio)
                                                   * elevenlabs_conversation_id or equivalent external id (if known)
                                                   * ended_at
DB reads:
                                                   * calls row for ownership and status
                                                   * provider_events for dedup if the trigger is from a webhook
DB writes:
                                                   * set transcript status in a call artifact reference table or in call metadata fields:
                                                   * status: pending, available, failed, partial
                                                   * last_synced_at
                                                   * failure_reason (category only)
                                                   * external artifact id
External calls:
                                                   * Query ElevenLabs for transcript status and artifact id if not already present.
                                                   * Do not fetch and store transcript text as persistent data.
Output:
                                                   * Update call record artifact state so UI can show transcript readiness.
                                                   * Optionally enqueue summary_generate if summary depends on transcript completion.
Failure handling:
                                                   * If ElevenLabs is temporarily unavailable, retry with backoff.
                                                   * If the external id is invalid or missing after a reasonable window, mark as failed and stop retrying.
Idempotency:
                                                   * Scope: transcript_sync
                                                   * Key: call_id + external_artifact_version if available, otherwise call_id + ended_at
________________


16.2.2 summary_generate (store encrypted summary locally)
Purpose:
                                                   * Summary is the primary UI artifact and must load quickly.
                                                   * Even if ElevenLabs can store a summary, Phase 1 stores summary in Postgres encrypted so the app can render call list and call detail without repeated provider calls.
                                                   * Summary content is Restricted and must be application-layer encrypted.
Trigger:
                                                   * After transcript_sync marks transcript available or partial, or after call ended if ElevenLabs summary is generated independently.
Input payload:
                                                   * call_id
                                                   * elevenlabs_conversation_id (or equivalent)
                                                   * summary_provider_preference (optional, defaults to elevenlabs)
DB reads:
                                                   * call ownership and state
                                                   * existing summary status for the call to avoid duplicates
External calls:
                                                   * Preferred: request summary from ElevenLabs if available.
                                                   * Fallback: call a configured LLM summarizer if ElevenLabs summary is unavailable, disabled, or fails repeatedly.
                                                   * The summary generator must follow the Phase 1 summary format rules used across the product.
DB writes:
                                                   * encrypted summary text
                                                   * extracted fields used for UI badges and follow-up:
                                                   * urgency
                                                   * best callback time
                                                   * caller name if extracted
                                                   * reason short
                                                   * summary status: pending, available, failed, partial
                                                   * retry_count and failure_reason category
Output:
                                                   * Update call list fields used by the UI so the user sees results quickly.
                                                   * Enqueue label_compute after summary is available or partial.
Failure handling:
                                                   * If summary fails, keep call record usable and show a summary unavailable state.
                                                   * Retries use backoff; after max retries, mark failed and alert.
Idempotency:
                                                   * Scope: summary_generate
                                                   * Key: call_id + transcript_version_or_last_synced_at
________________


16.2.3 label_compute (classification and labeling)
Purpose:
                                                   * Apply consistent labels to the call record:
                                                   * VIP
                                                   * spam
                                                   * urgent
                                                   * unknown
                                                   * Labels drive importance notifications and handoff triggers.
Trigger:
                                                   * After summary_generate completes (available or partial).
                                                   * Also triggered when the user changes VIP or block list and requests re-evaluation for recent calls (optional Phase 1).
Input payload:
                                                   * call_id
DB reads:
                                                   * call record
                                                   * VIP list and block list hashes
                                                   * existing labels for idempotency check
Computation:
                                                   * VIP: if caller number matches VIP list by hash.
                                                   * Block: if caller number matches block list by hash.
                                                   * Urgent: if summary urgency indicates urgent, or a configured rule set marks it urgent.
                                                   * Spam: if AI suggests spam and Phase 1 policy allows suggestion, but user confirmation rules are applied if required.
DB writes:
                                                   * call_labels join rows with applied_by and applied_reason
                                                   * call record urgency_level field for list sorting
Output:
                                                   * Enqueue notify_call_screened if label changes affect importance.
Failure handling:
                                                   * Label compute failures must not block call visibility. Mark label as unknown and continue.
Idempotency:
                                                   * Scope: label_compute
                                                   * Key: call_id + summary_last_updated_at
________________


16.2.4 reminder_fire (reminder triggers)
Purpose:
                                                   * Deliver reminders reliably, with retry and delivery tracking.
Trigger:
                                                   * A periodic poller scans due reminders and enqueues reminder_fire jobs.
                                                   * Reminders are never scheduled solely through SQS delay because SQS delay is limited and not precise enough for long future times.
Input payload:
                                                   * reminder_id
DB reads:
                                                   * reminder row (must be pending)
                                                   * user settings for quiet hours and privacy notifications
                                                   * device push tokens
DB writes:
                                                   * reminder status: pending to fired or failed
                                                   * notification delivery records if you store them
                                                   * audit event if required for reminder delivery actions
External calls:
                                                   * Push provider send (FCM) through the notifications worker
Output:
                                                   * User receives a push that respects privacy mode.
Failure handling:
                                                   * If push fails temporarily, retry notification delivery.
                                                   * Reminder should not fire multiple times: idempotency is required.
Idempotency:
                                                   * Scope: reminder_fire
                                                   * Key: reminder_id + due_at
________________


16.2.5 retention_delete_call (retention deletion jobs)
Purpose:
                                                   * Enforce retention by deleting call records and all associated local artifacts.
                                                   * Because transcript and media are stored in ElevenLabs, the retention job must also remove local access and attempt external deletion or access revocation.
Trigger:
                                                   * Periodic poller scans calls where retention_expires_at is in the past and deleted_at is null.
Input payload:
                                                   * call_id
DB reads:
                                                   * call record, verify expired and not already deleted
DB writes:
                                                   * mark call deleted locally and cascade delete local artifacts:
                                                   * encrypted summary
                                                   * notes
                                                   * labels
                                                   * reminders linked to call (cancel or detach)
                                                   * outbox events related to the call
                                                   * remove stored external artifact references so our backend can no longer retrieve transcript or recording for this call
External calls:
                                                   * Attempt provider deletion if ElevenLabs supports deletion.
                                                   * If provider deletion is not supported, enforce access revocation by removing artifact ids locally and refusing future retrieval.
Output:
                                                   * Call is no longer visible to the user.
                                                   * Backend refuses to serve transcript, summary, or recording after deletion.
Failure handling:
                                                   * If external deletion fails but local deletion succeeds, the system still meets the core product promise: user cannot access it through our system.
                                                   * External deletion failures must be logged and retried with a capped policy.
Idempotency:
                                                   * Scope: retention_delete_call
                                                   * Key: call_id + retention_expires_at
________________


16.2.6 provider_event_retry (webhook retry jobs)
Purpose:
                                                   * Provider webhooks can fail processing due to temporary database outages, temporary provider data not ready, or internal errors.
                                                   * We store the receipt in provider_events with a processing status. Failed ones need controlled reprocessing.
Trigger:
                                                   * Periodic poller scans provider_events where process_status is failed and attempts remain.
Input payload:
                                                   * provider
                                                   * provider_event_id or fallback dedupe key
                                                   * provider_call_sid
                                                   * provider_event_row_id
DB reads:
                                                   * provider_events row to ensure it still needs processing
                                                   * related call record if already created
DB writes:
                                                   * update provider_events process_status and processed_at
                                                   * create or update call record state when appropriate
                                                   * enqueue downstream postprocess jobs when processing succeeds
Idempotency:
                                                   * Scope: provider_event_process
                                                   * Key: provider + provider_event_id if present, otherwise provider + event_type + payload_hash
________________


16.2.7 sms_send (approval-first SMS sending)
Purpose:
                                                   * Send outbound texts only after user approval.
                                                   * Guarantee no duplicate sends even with retries and duplicate deliveries.
Trigger:
                                                   * Enqueued after an approval record is created and message status becomes approved.
Input payload:
                                                   * message_id
DB reads:
                                                   * outbound_messages row, must be approved and not already sent
                                                   * approval record and approved content hash
                                                   * user settings (quiet hours does not block sending unless product policy says so)
DB writes:
                                                   * message status transitions:
                                                   * approved to sending to sent, or to failed
                                                   * provider message sid
                                                   * delivery tracking state (if used)
External calls:
                                                   * Send SMS via Twilio.
Failure handling:
                                                   * If Twilio returns transient error, retry with backoff.
                                                   * If Twilio returns permanent error (invalid number, blocked, rejected), mark failed without retries.
                                                   * Never retry in a way that can cause duplicate messages:
                                                   * Use idempotency key plus Twilio message status checks.
                                                   * If Twilio supports idempotency keys, use them too.
Idempotency:
                                                   * Scope: sms_send
                                                   * Key: message_id + approved_content_hash
________________


16.2.8 push_send (notification delivery)
Purpose:
                                                   * Deliver push notifications with privacy defaults, retry, and tracking.
Trigger:
                                                   * Enqueued from call postprocess or reminder_fire.
Input payload:
                                                   * owner_user_id
                                                   * notification_type (call_screened, reminder_due, security_event)
                                                   * target_call_id or target_reminder_id (optional)
                                                   * privacy_mode snapshot (optional, backend can re-read settings)
DB reads:
                                                   * user settings for privacy notification
                                                   * device tokens for active devices
DB writes:
                                                   * notification delivery attempt record (recommended)
                                                   * update attempt counters and last_attempt_at
External calls:
                                                   * FCM push send for each token.
Privacy shaping rules:
                                                   * If privacy_notifications is true, payload is generic:
                                                   * title: New call screened or Reminder
                                                   * body: empty or generic, no caller or message content
                                                   * If privacy_notifications is false, you may include limited non-Restricted metadata, but still never include transcript or summary text.
Idempotency:
                                                   * Scope: push_send
                                                   * Key: notification_type + target_id + device_id + content_version
________________


16.3 Idempotency rules
SQS Standard can deliver duplicates. The system must treat duplicates as normal and harmless.
16.3.1 Idempotency storage
Use the idempotency_keys table to store:
                                                   * scope
                                                   * key
                                                   * request hash
                                                   * status: started, succeeded, failed
                                                   * expires_at
Rules:
                                                   * Producers must create an idempotency row for user-triggered jobs where duplicates are dangerous (sms_send).
                                                   * Consumers must check idempotency row before executing and must update it after completion.
If a message arrives and idempotency indicates succeeded:
                                                   * Acknowledge the message immediately.
                                                   * Do not repeat side effects.
If idempotency indicates started but stale:
                                                   * Treat as retry and continue, but ensure side effects are protected by additional state checks.
16.3.2 State-based idempotency for call postprocessing
For call-related jobs, the database is the source of truth.
Consumers must check call state before doing work:
Examples:
                                                   * If summary status is already available, summary_generate job should do nothing and ack.
                                                   * If call is already deleted, transcript_sync must do nothing and ack.
                                                   * If message is already sent, sms_send must do nothing and ack.
16.3.3 Provider webhook idempotency
Use provider_events as the dedupe gate:
                                                   * A provider event is processed only once per provider_event_id or payload_hash strategy.
                                                   * If duplicates arrive, they are recorded but not reprocessed.
16.3.4 Exactly-once outcomes for sensitive operations
Operations that must be exactly-once in outcome:
                                                   * sms_send
                                                   * retention_delete_call local deletion
                                                   * account deletion cleanup actions
                                                   * device revoke cascading token invalidation
Exactly-once is enforced by:
                                                   * idempotency keys
                                                   * unique constraints (provider message sid uniqueness)
                                                   * state transitions guarded by conditional updates
________________


16.4 Retry strategy
16.4.1 Error classification
All jobs classify failures into:
                                                   * Transient: retry (timeouts, 429, 5xx, network errors, provider temporarily unavailable)
                                                   * Permanent: do not retry (validation errors, missing required ids, forbidden, number blocked, malformed payload)
                                                   * Unknown: retry a small number of times, then fail and alert
Workers must log failure category and a short code, never Restricted content.
16.4.2 Backoff policy
Use exponential backoff with jitter.
Recommended default schedule:
                                                   * attempt 1: 5 to 10 seconds
                                                   * attempt 2: 15 to 30 seconds
                                                   * attempt 3: 60 to 120 seconds
                                                   * attempt 4: 5 to 10 minutes
                                                   * attempt 5: 15 minutes
                                                   * attempts beyond: 15 minutes each until max retries
SQS delay limit:
                                                   * DelaySeconds max is 900 seconds.
                                                   * For delays beyond 15 minutes, store next_attempt_at in DB and let a poller re-enqueue later.
16.4.3 Max retries per job type
Recommended caps:
                                                   * transcript_sync: 8 attempts over 2 to 6 hours window
                                                   * summary_generate: 8 attempts, with provider fallback after 2 to 3 failures
                                                   * label_compute: 3 attempts
                                                   * push_send: 5 attempts
                                                   * reminder_fire: 5 attempts
                                                   * retention_delete_call: 5 attempts for external deletion, local deletion must still complete
                                                   * provider_event_retry: 8 attempts
                                                   * sms_send: 3 attempts for transient failures only
16.4.4 Visibility timeout and long jobs
Each queue must set visibility timeout longer than the job’s worst-case execution time.
Recommended starting values:
                                                   * q-call-postprocess: 5 minutes
                                                   * q-messaging: 2 minutes
                                                   * q-notifications: 2 minutes
                                                   * q-maintenance: 10 minutes (retention deletes may touch many rows)
If a job can exceed visibility timeout:
                                                   * Worker must extend visibility using ChangeMessageVisibility.
                                                   * Worker must still be idempotent because the message can reappear.
16.4.5 DLQ handling and operator workflow
When a message lands in a DLQ:
                                                   * Create a high-severity alert (Sentry) with:
                                                   * job_id
                                                   * job_type
                                                   * owner_user_id if present
                                                   * call_id or message_id if present
                                                   * failure category and code
                                                   * last error message redacted
                                                   * Provide an internal runbook action:
                                                   * inspect the associated DB rows
                                                   * decide whether to re-drive to main queue or mark permanently failed
                                                   * if user impact exists (missing summary), show a safe internal status and offer a manual reprocess button in the admin portal (RBAC protected)
________________


16.5 Scheduling model (reminders, retention, retries)
Phase 1 uses pollers inside the maintenance worker because no staging and minimal infrastructure is preferred.
16.5.1 Poller intervals
Recommended:
                                                   * reminders due scan: every 60 seconds
                                                   * retention deletion scan: every 30 minutes (or hourly)
                                                   * provider failed event scan: every 5 minutes
                                                   * outbox dispatch scan: every 5 to 15 seconds (fast loop with sleep)
16.5.2 Poller idempotency
Pollers must:
                                                   * select rows using a safe condition (status pending and due_at <= now)
                                                   * update rows with a claim marker before enqueueing to avoid double enqueue in parallel workers
                                                   * enqueue the job
                                                   * if enqueue fails, release the claim and retry next cycle
Claim pattern:
                                                   * update status from pending to processing with a conditional update
                                                   * include a processing_started_at timestamp
                                                   * if stuck longer than a threshold, allow reclaim
________________


16.6 Completion checklist for Chapter 16
Chapter 16 is complete when:
                                                   * All queues and DLQs exist and are configured with correct visibility timeouts and redrive policies.
                                                   * Webhook handlers do minimal work and enqueue background jobs.
                                                   * Every job type is implemented with:
                                                   * strict ownership scoping
                                                   * state checks
                                                   * idempotency key usage
                                                   * retry and backoff rules
