                                                   * Reminder and retention scheduling works using pollers and does not double-fire.
                                                   * SMS sending is approval-first and exactly-once in outcome.
                                                   * Push notifications respect privacy defaults and have retry plus delivery tracking.
                                                   * DLQ alerts are actionable and do not contain Restricted content.
________________


According to a document from 2026.
CHAPTER 17: API DESIGN OVERVIEW
17.1 Purpose and scope
This chapter defines the external API contract for Phase 1, including:
                                                   * Mobile app to backend APIs (consumer APIs)
                                                   * Web admin portal to backend APIs (admin APIs)
                                                   * Vendor webhooks and callbacks (telephony and AI)
                                                   * Internal service-to-service API boundaries (Python core backend and Node real-time call service)
The API design must support Phase 1 call modes:
                                                   * Dedicated AI number inbound flow
                                                   * Conditional call forwarding fallback for busy and unreachable
                                                   * Live handoff prompting and acceptance flow
                                                   * Approval-first texting after calls
                                                   * Retention and deletion operations
                                                   * Privacy-safe notification behaviors
17.2 API style and conventions
17.2.1 API style
                                                   * Style: REST
                                                   * Payloads: JSON over HTTPS only
                                                   * Time format: ISO 8601 in UTC
                                                   * Identifiers: UUIDs for all primary entities (public IDs are UUIDs, internal IDs may exist but must not be exposed)
OpenAPI:
                                                   * OpenAPI required: Yes
                                                   * The OpenAPI spec is the single source of truth for:
                                                   * Request and response schemas
                                                   * Authentication requirements per endpoint
                                                   * Error codes and meanings
                                                   * Pagination and filtering conventions
17.2.2 URL structure
                                                   * Base path: /api/v1
                                                   * Resources are nouns, not verbs:
                                                   * /calls, /devices, /numbers, /settings, /vip, /blocks, /reminders, /messages, /audit-events
17.2.3 Standard headers
                                                   * Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
                                                   * X-Request-Id: accepted from clients, generated if absent, echoed back in every response
                                                   * X-Correlation-Id: used for cross-service tracing (especially across Python and Node)
                                                   * Idempotency-Key: required for specific write operations (see 17.8)
17.2.4 Response envelope
Responses are plain JSON objects. Lists use:
                                                   * items: array
                                                   * next_cursor: string or null
                                                   * has_more: boolean
17.3 Authentication for APIs
17.3.1 Token model
                                                   * Access token: short-lived bearer token
                                                   * Refresh token: long-lived token, rotated on use
                                                   * Sessions are per-device (each device has its own refresh token)
                                                   * “Sign out all devices” revokes all refresh tokens for the user
17.3.2 MFA behavior
                                                   * MFA is required (TOTP as primary)
                                                   * Email OTP allowed as fallback (weaker)
                                                   * MFA is enforced at token issuance time:
                                                   * No “fully logged in” access token is issued until MFA is satisfied
17.3.3 Auth endpoints (consumer)
                                                   * POST /api/v1/auth/register
                                                   * POST /api/v1/auth/login
                                                   * POST /api/v1/auth/mfa/verify
                                                   * POST /api/v1/auth/token/refresh
                                                   * POST /api/v1/auth/logout
                                                   * POST /api/v1/auth/logout-all
                                                   * POST /api/v1/auth/recovery/start
                                                   * POST /api/v1/auth/recovery/complete
17.3.4 Auth endpoints (admin)
Admin portal uses the same token system, with role-based authorization enforced server-side.
17.4 API versioning and backward compatibility
17.4.1 Versioning strategy
                                                   * Version in path: /api/v1
                                                   * Backward-compatible changes allowed within the same major version:
                                                   * Adding optional fields
                                                   * Adding new endpoints
                                                   * Breaking changes require a new major version (/api/v2) with a deprecation window.
17.4.2 Deprecation rules
                                                   * Deprecations must be documented in OpenAPI
                                                   * Response header Deprecation may be used for endpoints slated for removal
17.5 Error format standard
All non-2xx responses return:
                                                   * error object:
                                                   * code: stable machine code (example: CALL_NOT_FOUND)
                                                   * message: safe human-readable message
                                                   * request_id: the request ID for support and debugging
                                                   * details: optional structured details (field validation errors, etc.)
Error rules:
                                                   * Never return transcript text, message bodies, phone numbers, or secrets in error messages
                                                   * Correlation IDs must allow tracing the request end-to-end across services
17.6 Endpoint catalog (Phase 1)
17.6.1 User and device
                                                   * GET /api/v1/me
                                                   * PATCH /api/v1/me
                                                   * GET /api/v1/devices
                                                   * POST /api/v1/devices/register
Registers push token and device metadata, creates or updates device session metadata.
                                                   * POST /api/v1/devices/{device_id}/revoke
17.6.2 Numbers and call modes
                                                      * POST /api/v1/numbers/provision
Auto-provision a dedicated AI number during onboarding.
                                                      * GET /api/v1/numbers
                                                      * GET /api/v1/numbers/{number_id}
                                                      * POST /api/v1/numbers/{number_id}/test
Creates a test session record used to guide the user through verifying call setup.
Call mode configuration:
                                                         * GET /api/v1/call-modes
                                                         * PATCH /api/v1/call-modes
Stores whether dedicated number mode is active, forwarding mode is configured, and which triggers are intended (busy, unreachable).
Forwarding setup assistance:
                                                            * GET /api/v1/forwarding/setup-guide
Returns carrier-agnostic steps and trigger explanations.
                                                            * POST /api/v1/forwarding/verify
Records a verification attempt and its outcome.
17.6.3 Calls and call records
Core call list and detail:
                                                               * GET /api/v1/calls
Supports pagination, filtering, and sorting.
                                                               * GET /api/v1/calls/{call_id}
                                                               * PATCH /api/v1/calls/{call_id}
Allows user edits to safe fields such as labels, notes, and “important” overrides.
Call state and timeline:
                                                                  * GET /api/v1/calls/{call_id}/events
Returns state transitions and system events for debugging and consistent UI state.
Deletion:
                                                                     * DELETE /api/v1/calls/{call_id}
Deletes the call record and all associated derived data in our system, and triggers vendor artifact deletion where applicable.
                                                                     * POST /api/v1/calls/delete-all
Deletes all call data for the user.
17.6.4 Transcript and recording access (vendor-proxied)
Phase 1 must treat transcripts and recordings as sensitive. If transcripts and recordings are stored in ElevenLabs, the backend must not expose vendor URLs directly.
Transcript access:
                                                                        * GET /api/v1/calls/{call_id}/transcript
Backend fetches from ElevenLabs using stored vendor identifiers, returns content only to an authenticated, authorized user, and applies retention rules.
Recording access (if enabled):
                                                                           * GET /api/v1/calls/{call_id}/recording
Returns either a short-lived signed access token from backend or a backend streaming proxy response. No public URLs.
Security rules:
                                                                              * Access requires authentication
                                                                              * Optional biometric gating is enforced at the app layer for viewing sensitive content, and server-side access is still protected by auth and authorization
                                                                              * Vendor identifiers must be treated as secrets and never logged
17.6.5 Summary and extraction artifacts
                                                                              * GET /api/v1/calls/{call_id}/summary
Returns structured summary fields (reason, urgency, callback number, optional best callback time, etc.) and status fields (ready, unavailable, failed).
                                                                              * POST /api/v1/calls/{call_id}/summary/regenerate
Enqueues a summary regeneration job with idempotency controls.
17.6.6 Labels, VIP list, block list
                                                                                 * GET /api/v1/labels/catalog
                                                                                 * POST /api/v1/calls/{call_id}/labels
                                                                                 * DELETE /api/v1/calls/{call_id}/labels/{label_id}
VIP:
                                                                                 * GET /api/v1/vip
                                                                                 * POST /api/v1/vip
                                                                                 * DELETE /api/v1/vip/{vip_id}
Blocks:
                                                                                 * GET /api/v1/blocks
                                                                                 * POST /api/v1/blocks
                                                                                 * DELETE /api/v1/blocks/{block_id}
These lists must be treated as sensitive and included in deletion rules and audit logs.
17.6.7 Live handoff controls
Handoff prompting and acceptance is time-sensitive and must be consistent across multiple devices:
                                                                                 * GET /api/v1/calls/{call_id}/handoff/status
                                                                                 * POST /api/v1/calls/{call_id}/handoff/accept
                                                                                 * POST /api/v1/calls/{call_id}/handoff/decline
Rules:
                                                                                 * Only one device can accept a given handoff
                                                                                 * If acceptance fails or times out, the call continues in screening mode
                                                                                 * All outcomes are recorded as call events and audit events
17.6.8 Texting drafts and approval-first sending
Drafts:
                                                                                 * GET /api/v1/calls/{call_id}/message-drafts
                                                                                 * POST /api/v1/calls/{call_id}/message-drafts
Creates a draft from template or AI suggestion.
Approval-first send:
                                                                                    * POST /api/v1/messages/send
Requires explicit user approval payload, stores message, and tracks delivery status.
Retries and failures:
                                                                                       * POST /api/v1/messages/{message_id}/retry
17.6.9 Reminders
                                                                                       * GET /api/v1/reminders
                                                                                       * POST /api/v1/reminders
                                                                                       * PATCH /api/v1/reminders/{reminder_id}
                                                                                       * DELETE /api/v1/reminders/{reminder_id}
17.6.10 Settings (privacy, retention, memory, notifications)
                                                                                       * GET /api/v1/settings
                                                                                       * PATCH /api/v1/settings
Settings must include:
                                                                                       * Notification privacy mode (default private)
                                                                                       * Quiet hours
                                                                                       * Recording enabled or disabled
                                                                                       * Retention duration choice
                                                                                       * Memory enabled or disabled
                                                                                       * Handoff eligibility rules
                                                                                       * Biometric gating preferences
17.6.11 Audit events
                                                                                       * GET /api/v1/audit-events
Paginated, filtered by category and date range. Must never include full transcript content.
17.6.12 Admin portal endpoints
Admin endpoints are separate by authorization scope:
                                                                                          * GET /api/v1/admin/users
                                                                                          * GET /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}
                                                                                          * POST /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}/disable
                                                                                          * POST /api/v1/admin/users/{user_id}/revoke-devices
                                                                                          * GET /api/v1/admin/numbers
                                                                                          * POST /api/v1/admin/numbers/{number_id}/release
                                                                                          * GET /api/v1/admin/jobs and GET /api/v1/admin/jobs/{job_id} (read-only visibility into queue failures)
                                                                                          * GET /api/v1/admin/incidents (read-only, sourced from monitoring signals)
17.6.13 Webhooks and vendor callbacks (not authenticated by user tokens)
These endpoints require signature validation and strict allow-lists:
                                                                                          * POST /webhooks/twilio/voice/inbound
Inbound call entrypoint.
                                                                                          * POST /webhooks/twilio/voice/status
Status callback (call lifecycle).
                                                                                          * POST /webhooks/twilio/sms/status
Delivery status updates.
                                                                                          * POST /webhooks/elevenlabs/events
Call transcript availability, post-call artifacts, failure signals.
Webhook rules:
                                                                                             * Validate signatures
                                                                                             * Enforce strict schema checks
                                                                                             * Immediate ACK, then enqueue processing to SQS
                                                                                             * Never log payloads containing transcript text or message content in plaintext
17.7 Pagination, filtering, and sorting
Calls list (GET /calls) must support:
                                                                                             * Pagination: cursor-based (next_cursor)
                                                                                             * Filters:
                                                                                             * label
                                                                                             * is_important
                                                                                             * source_type (dedicated, forwarded)
                                                                                             * date_from, date_to
                                                                                             * Sorting:
                                                                                             * created_at descending default
                                                                                             * Field selection (optional): allow clients to request light payloads for list views
17.8 Idempotency, concurrency, and safe writes
Idempotency is mandatory for:
                                                                                             * Provisioning a number
                                                                                             * Creating a reminder
                                                                                             * Sending a message
                                                                                             * Regenerating a summary
                                                                                             * Any endpoint that triggers a background job
Rules:
                                                                                             * Client provides Idempotency-Key
                                                                                             * Server stores key with request hash and result
                                                                                             * Replays return the same result without duplicating side effects
                                                                                             * Keys expire after a defined window (example: 24 hours)
Concurrency rules:
                                                                                             * Device revoke and logout-all must be globally consistent
                                                                                             * Handoff accept must be atomic, allowing only one device to win
17.9 Rate limiting and abuse controls at API layer
Rate limits must exist for:
                                                                                             * Auth endpoints (login, MFA verify, refresh)
                                                                                             * Calls list and call detail endpoints (to reduce scraping risk)
                                                                                             * Message send and retry endpoints
                                                                                             * Admin endpoints
Strategies:
                                                                                             * Per IP limits at edge
                                                                                             * Per user limits at application layer
                                                                                             * Per phone number limits for operations tied to caller identity
                                                                                             * Hard blocks for repeated violations, logged as audit events
17.10 OpenAPI, client generation, and contract testing
OpenAPI deliverables:
                                                                                             * OpenAPI spec file versioned in the repo
                                                                                             * Generated client SDKs (optional) for:
                                                                                             * Mobile app
                                                                                             * Admin portal
Contract tests:
                                                                                             * CI must run API contract tests that validate:
                                                                                             * Response schemas match OpenAPI
                                                                                             * Auth requirements enforced per endpoint
                                                                                             * Error format always matches standard
                                                                                             * No sensitive fields leak into list endpoints, notifications payload endpoints, or logs
This chapter is complete when:
                                                                                             * REST endpoint set is locked
                                                                                             * OpenAPI is present and validated in CI
                                                                                             * Auth, versioning, and error format are consistent across all endpoints
                                                                                             * Vendor callbacks are signature-validated and processed asynchronously
                                                                                             * Transcript and recording access is protected and never exposes public vendor URLs
________________


According to a document from February 2026
CHAPTER 18: REALTIME COMMUNICATION DESIGN
18.0 Purpose and scope
This chapter defines how Phase 1 delivers live call events to the mobile app in real time, with correct privacy behavior, multi-device support, and safe concurrency rules for actions that must be “single winner” (especially live handoff acceptance).
Phase 1 requirement in scope:
                                                                                             * Websockets for live call events to the app: YES
                                                                                             * Multi-device login: YES
                                                                                             * Private notifications by default: YES (lock screen shows only a generic message)
Important Phase 1 constraint:
                                                                                             * Media and transcripts are stored in ElevenLabs, so our real time layer must treat them as external artifacts and only deliver statuses, metadata, and pointers (not raw audio, not transcript blobs).
________________


18.1 Websocket events catalog
18.1.1 What realtime is used for (and what it is not)
Realtime is used for:
                                                                                             * Live call state changes (call started, call ended)
                                                                                             * Progress updates (screening started, handoff offered)
                                                                                             * Completion signals (transcript ready, summary ready)
                                                                                             * Action outcomes (handoff accepted or failed)
Realtime is not used for:
                                                                                             * Streaming audio to the app
                                                                                             * Showing sensitive content on the lock screen
                                                                                             * Replacing push notifications (push still handles offline devices)
18.1.2 Connection model (high level)
The mobile app maintains a secure websocket connection when:
                                                                                             * The app is in the foreground, or
                                                                                             * The OS allows short background windows (best effort, not guaranteed)
When offline or background restricted:
                                                                                             * Push notifications deliver a privacy-safe alert
                                                                                             * The app pulls details via REST after unlock
18.1.3 Event envelope (standard shape)
All events must share a single envelope format so the client can handle them consistently.
Minimum envelope fields:
                                                                                             * event_id (UUID)
                                                                                             * event_type (string)
                                                                                             * event_version (integer)
                                                                                             * user_id (server-side scope, not exposed if not needed)
                                                                                             * device_id (optional, used for arbitration responses)
                                                                                             * call_id (UUID)
                                                                                             * ts (server timestamp in ISO 8601 UTC)
                                                                                             * seq (monotonic per call, used for replay and ordering)
                                                                                             * privacy_level (public, sensitive)
                                                                                             * payload (object, event-specific)
Privacy rule:
                                                                                             * If privacy_level = sensitive, the event payload must never contain data that could be shown in a notification preview without unlock. The client must gate display behind app unlock and any biometric policy.
18.1.4 Required Phase 1 event types
The following event types are required because they are explicitly called out as Phase 1 needs:
A) Call lifecycle
                                                                                             1. CALL_STARTED
                                                                                             * Meaning: A call has entered the system (dedicated AI number or forwarded path).
                                                                                             * Payload (minimal):
                                                                                             * source_type (dedicated_number, forwarded_busy, forwarded_unreachable)
                                                                                             * from_number_e164 (optional, may be withheld if unknown)
                                                                                             * is_vip (boolean)
                                                                                             * is_blocked (boolean)
                                                                                             * initial_label (unknown, vip, blocked, spam_suspected, normal)
                                                                                             2. CALL_ENDED
                                                                                             * Meaning: The call is complete.
                                                                                             * Payload:
                                                                                             * end_reason (caller_hung_up, ai_completed, handoff_connected, error)
                                                                                             * duration_seconds
                                                                                             * final_label (vip, spam, urgent, normal, unknown, blocked)
                                                                                             * has_summary (boolean)
                                                                                             * has_transcript (boolean)
B) Artifact readiness
                                                                                             3. TRANSCRIPT_READY
                                                                                             * Meaning: ElevenLabs has produced the transcript artifact and it is now available to reference.
                                                                                             * Payload:
                                                                                             * transcript_status (ready, partial, failed)
                                                                                             * provider (elevenlabs)
                                                                                             * artifact_ref (opaque pointer or provider conversation id)
                                                                                             * retry_allowed (boolean)
                                                                                             4. SUMMARY_READY
                                                                                             * Meaning: The structured summary is available to the app.
                                                                                             * Payload:
                                                                                             * summary_status (ready, partial, failed)
                                                                                             * summary_version (integer)
                                                                                             * retry_allowed (boolean)
C) Live handoff states
                                                                                             5. HANDOFF_OFFERED
                                                                                             * Meaning: The system has determined the call is eligible and is offering takeover to the user.
                                                                                             * Payload:
                                                                                             * offer_reason (vip, urgent, vip_or_urgent)
                                                                                             * expires_at (UTC timestamp)
                                                                                             * action_id (server generated id used for acceptance)
                                                                                             6. HANDOFF_ACCEPTED
                                                                                             * Meaning: One device accepted the handoff and the backend is attempting to connect the call.
                                                                                             * Payload:
                                                                                             * accepted_by_device_id
                                                                                             * accepted_at
                                                                                             * connect_target (user_phone_number)
                                                                                             * connect_status (connecting)
                                                                                             7. HANDOFF_FAILED
                                                                                             * Meaning: The handoff could not be completed and the AI flow continues.
                                                                                             * Payload:
                                                                                             * failed_stage (dial_user, bridge, timeout, provider_error)
                                                                                             * failure_code (normalized internal code)
                                                                                             * fallback_mode (continue_screening, end_call_with_message)
18.1.5 Optional but strongly recommended Phase 1 event types
These are not explicitly listed in the outline, but they reduce client polling and improve UX without increasing privacy risk:
                                                                                             * CALL_UPDATED
                                                                                             * For small state changes without introducing many event types (example: “screening in progress”).
                                                                                             * ACTION_STATUS_CHANGED
                                                                                             * For approval-first texting states (draft_saved, sending, delivered, failed), if texting UI is in Phase 1 scope.
                                                                                             * RETENTION_DELETION_COMPLETED
                                                                                             * When the backend completes user-initiated delete actions, to sync all devices quickly.
If included, they must follow the same envelope rules and privacy controls.
________________


18.2 Delivery guarantees
18.2.1 Reliability goals for Phase 1
Because telephony events are time sensitive and devices can be offline, Phase 1 must use a mixed strategy:
                                                                                             * Websocket delivery: real time when connected
                                                                                             * Push notification fallback: best effort delivery to wake the user
                                                                                             * REST reconciliation: source of truth after reconnect
The key is that the app can always reach a consistent state even if it missed some realtime messages.
18.2.2 Event ordering and replay
To prevent missing or mis-ordering events:
                                                                                             * Each call has a seq counter that increases per event.
                                                                                             * The client tracks last_seq_seen per call (or globally).
                                                                                             * On websocket connect or reconnect, the client sends:
                                                                                             * last_global_seq_seen (or per-call cursor)
                                                                                             * The server replays any missed events from durable storage.
Durable storage requirement:
                                                                                             * Call state events must be persisted (example: a call_events table keyed by call_id plus seq), at least for the retention window needed to support reconnect and debugging.
18.2.3 Acknowledgement strategy
Not all events need ack. Phase 1 should split events into two classes:
Class 1, informative events (no ack required):
                                                                                             * CALL_STARTED, CALL_ENDED, TRANSCRIPT_READY, SUMMARY_READY
These can be “at least once” without harm, because the client can dedupe by event_id and reconcile via REST.
Class 2, action-driving events (ack or explicit response required):
                                                                                                * HANDOFF_OFFERED (client should respond by accept or ignore)
                                                                                                * HANDOFF_ACCEPTED and HANDOFF_FAILED (server authoritative, but client must update UI immediately)
For these, the websocket protocol must support:
                                                                                                * A client response message that includes action_id
                                                                                                * A server response that includes a definitive outcome
18.2.4 Offline device behavior
When the device is offline or the OS blocks background sockets:
                                                                                                   1. Backend emits websocket events (will not deliver).
                                                                                                   2. Backend sends a push notification with privacy-safe text:
                                                                                                   * Default: “New call screened”
                                                                                                   * No caller name, no message content.
                                                                                                   3. When the user opens and unlocks the app:
                                                                                                   * App fetches latest call list and call details via REST.
                                                                                                   * App also reconnects websocket and requests replay using cursor.
18.2.5 Multi-device notification behavior
If the user has multiple devices:
                                                                                                   * All devices should receive push notifications (subject to OS permissions).
                                                                                                   * All devices should receive websocket events when connected.
                                                                                                   * If notifications are disabled on one device, other devices still receive them.
18.2.6 Failure handling and backpressure
To avoid realtime infrastructure becoming a stability risk:
                                                                                                   * Set a maximum message rate per connection.
                                                                                                   * Drop non-critical updates first if overloaded (example: CALL_UPDATED), but never drop:
                                                                                                   * HANDOFF_OFFERED, HANDOFF_ACCEPTED, HANDOFF_FAILED, CALL_ENDED
                                                                                                   * Implement ping/pong keepalives and short timeouts to clear dead connections.
                                                                                                   * Enforce per-user and per-IP connection limits.
________________


18.3 Multi-device arbitration rules
Phase 1 explicitly requires that only one device can accept handoff. This must be enforced server-side so it remains correct under race conditions.
18.3.1 Core arbitration principles
                                                                                                   1. Server authoritative
                                                                                                   * The server decides which device “wins” any exclusive action.
                                                                                                   2. First valid accept wins
                                                                                                   * The first acceptance request that reaches the server within the offer window is the winner.
                                                                                                   3. Idempotent outcomes
                                                                                                   * Repeated acceptance attempts from the same device must return the same result (success or already accepted).
                                                                                                   * Acceptance attempts from other devices must return “rejected, already accepted by another device”.
18.3.2 Handoff acceptance flow (race-safe)
When HANDOFF_OFFERED is emitted:
                                                                                                   * Server stores a handoff offer record:
                                                                                                   * call_id, action_id, expires_at, status = offered
When a device attempts acceptance:
                                                                                                   * Client sends: HANDOFF_ACCEPT_REQUEST(action_id, device_id)
                                                                                                   * Server performs an atomic transition:
                                                                                                   * If status is offered and not expired, set:
                                                                                                   * status = accepted
                                                                                                   * accepted_by_device_id = X
                                                                                                   * accepted_at = now
                                                                                                   * Else reject with a definitive reason:
                                                                                                   * expired, already_accepted, not_found, call_ended
Atomicity requirement:
                                                                                                   * This transition must be a single database transaction with a row lock (or an equivalent compare-and-set update) so two devices cannot both win.
18.3.3 Server responses to all devices
After acceptance resolution:
                                                                                                   * Winner device receives HANDOFF_ACCEPTED with accepted_by_device_id = winner.
                                                                                                   * All other connected devices receive:
                                                                                                   * Either HANDOFF_ACCEPTED (so they update UI to “accepted on another device”), or
                                                                                                   * A HANDOFF_REJECTED event (optional) with reason accepted_elsewhere.
If the connect attempt fails:
                                                                                                   * Server emits HANDOFF_FAILED to all devices.
                                                                                                   * The call continues with AI screening (no silence).
18.3.4 Arbitration for other exclusive actions
Even if Phase 1 focuses on handoff, the same rule set should be reusable for:
                                                                                                   * “Delete all call data”
                                                                                                   * “Account deletion”
                                                                                                   * Any future “take over” actions
For these actions:
                                                                                                   * Use server-generated action_id
                                                                                                   * Require authenticated device
                                                                                                   * Use transactional state transitions
                                                                                                   * Emit outcome events so all devices sync immediately
18.3.5 Privacy and UI consequences of arbitration
Because lock screen privacy is default:
                                                                                                   * Devices can show: “Incoming call screened” or “Handoff available” only as generic text.
                                                                                                   * Caller details, reason for call, and summary content require:
                                                                                                   * App unlock
                                                                                                   * Any biometric policy selected by the user for sensitive content
________________


18.4 Implementation notes to lock in for Phase 1
18.4.1 Transport security
                                                                                                   * Websocket endpoint must be TLS only (wss).
                                                                                                   * Authentication must be token-based (same session model as REST).
                                                                                                   * Tokens must be short-lived access tokens, refresh handled via REST.
18.4.2 Service placement
                                                                                                   * A Node.js realtime gateway is preferred for:
                                                                                                   * Websocket fan-out
                                                                                                   * Efficient concurrent connections
                                                                                                   * Python backend remains the source of truth for:
                                                                                                   * Call records
                                                                                                   * State transitions
                                                                                                   * Arbitration decisions
18.4.3 Minimal required storage for realtime correctness
Even if transcripts and media live in ElevenLabs, Phase 1 still needs to store:
                                                                                                   * Call state transitions (call started, handoff offered, accepted, ended)
                                                                                                   * Event cursor data for replay
                                                                                                   * Summary status and references
                                                                                                   * Provider references (opaque ids) for transcript/recording lookup when needed
This storage is necessary so reconnecting devices and multiple devices stay consistent without relying on “best effort” delivery.
________________


18.5 Completion checklist for this chapter
This chapter is complete when:
                                                                                                   * Mobile app receives CALL_STARTED and CALL_ENDED in real time when connected.
                                                                                                   * If the app is offline, a privacy-safe push is delivered and the app can reconcile via REST.
                                                                                                   * TRANSCRIPT_READY and SUMMARY_READY update the UI without manual refresh when connected.
                                                                                                   * Live handoff offer appears in real time and only one device can accept it.
                                                                                                   * Competing handoff acceptance attempts are handled correctly with a single winner and clear outcomes on all devices.
                                                                                                   * No sensitive content is exposed via lock screen notifications by default.
________________


CHAPTER 19: TELEPHONY INTEGRATION DESIGN
19.0 Purpose and scope
This chapter defines how MattBot integrates with the telephony provider to:
                                                                                                   * Provision and manage a dedicated AI phone number per user (Mode A).
                                                                                                   * Receive inbound calls (both dedicated-number calls and carrier-forwarded calls).
                                                                                                   * Stream audio in real time between the caller and the voice agent.
                                                                                                   * Support live handoff to the user’s real phone number with loop prevention and voicemail protection.
                                                                                                   * Provide reliable fallback behaviors when the AI or streaming layer is unavailable.
                                                                                                   * Enforce cost controls and abuse protections at the telephony boundary.
Phase 1 telephony provider: Twilio Programmable Voice plus Twilio Media Streams.
________________


19.1 Telephony provider integration architecture
19.1.1 Why Twilio is required even with ElevenLabs Voice
ElevenLabs provides the voice model, TTS, and STT. It does not provide PSTN phone numbers, carrier interconnect, call setup, or call control. Twilio provides:
                                                                                                   * Phone number ownership and inbound call termination.
                                                                                                   * Call lifecycle callbacks (status events).
                                                                                                   * Real-time audio transport (Media Streams) to our voice gateway.
                                                                                                   * Call control primitives (redirect call, hang up, dial another party, conference if needed).
19.1.2 High-level component map
External
                                                                                                   * Caller phone (PSTN)
                                                                                                   * Twilio Voice
                                                                                                   * Twilio Media Streams (WebSocket audio)
Internal
                                                                                                   * MattBot API (HTTP webhooks for Twilio)
                                                                                                   * Voice Gateway (WebSocket endpoint that bridges Twilio audio <-> ElevenLabs real-time voice)
                                                                                                   * Core DB (calls, call_events, user_numbers, forwarding_configs, handoff configs/settings)
Twilio entrypoints into MattBot
                                                                                                   * POST /webhooks/twilio/voice/inbound
Primary TwiML responder for inbound calls.
                                                                                                   * POST /webhooks/twilio/voice/status
Status callback sink for call lifecycle events and dial leg events (including AMD callbacks when used).
                                                                                                   * WebSocket endpoint (Voice Gateway), for example: wss://voice.<domain>/twilio/media
Receives Twilio Media Streams and sends audio back to Twilio.
Twilio’s inbound request includes key identifiers like CallSid, From, To, and may include ParentCallSid when a call leg is created from another call leg. (Twilio)
19.1.3 Webhook authentication and request validation
All Twilio-to-MattBot HTTP requests must be validated using Twilio request signature validation (X-Twilio-Signature). Reject invalid signatures before any DB write or TwiML generation.
                                                                                                      * Apply validation on:
                                                                                                      * /webhooks/twilio/voice/inbound
                                                                                                      * /webhooks/twilio/voice/status
                                                                                                      * WebSocket validation:
                                                                                                      * Validate Twilio source at the gateway (at minimum, validate the signature approach recommended by Twilio for webhook security for your stack and also require an internal session token passed via Stream custom parameters, described in 19.3.3).
Twilio’s guidance is to validate incoming webhook requests to ensure they originate from Twilio. (Twilio)
19.1.4 Media Streams behavior that impacts our call control
We will use <Connect><Stream> for the main AI conversation because it creates a bidirectional media stream. (Twilio)
Important control-flow detail:
                                                                                                      * With <Connect><Stream>, Twilio does not execute subsequent TwiML instructions until our WebSocket server closes the connection. (Twilio)
This is the mechanism we use for graceful fallback: if the AI layer fails mid-call, the gateway closes the socket, and Twilio continues to the next TwiML verb (a pre-authored fallback message).
Also:
                                                                                                         * The Stream url does not support query-string parameters.
                                                                                                         * We must pass correlation context using nested <Parameter> elements (custom parameters). (Twilio)
________________


19.2 Phone number provisioning and assignment
This section covers provisioning the dedicated AI number per user and linking it to the internal account.
19.2.1 Internal data model (Phase 1)
user_numbers (TELEPHONY) is the canonical mapping of a user to their dedicated AI number and the provider’s identifier:
                                                                                                         * owner_user_id
                                                                                                         * ai_number_e164 (plaintext, still Confidential)
                                                                                                         * provider = twilio
                                                                                                         * provider_number_sid (Twilio IncomingPhoneNumber SID)
                                                                                                         * status (active, pending, suspended)
                                                                                                         * provisioned_at timestamps
(See schema chapter; referenced here because telephony flow depends on these fields.)
19.2.2 Provisioning workflow (two-step)
Twilio provisioning is logically:
                                                                                                         1. Search available numbers
                                                                                                         2. Purchase number and create an IncomingPhoneNumber resource
Twilio describes provisioning as a two-step process: search and then provision. (Twilio)
Provisioning steps in MattBot
                                                                                                         1. App requests provisioning during onboarding if Dedicated AI Number is enabled.
                                                                                                         2. Backend selects a number using configured rules:
                                                                                                         * Country support list (Phase 1 start with a single primary market).
                                                                                                         * Prefer local numbers when inventory exists, fallback to toll-free if required by product policy.
                                                                                                         * Optional: choose by area code or region (only when user supplies).
                                                                                                         3. Backend calls Twilio API to purchase the number.
                                                                                                         4. Backend configures the purchased number:
                                                                                                         * Voice webhook: POST /webhooks/twilio/voice/inbound
                                                                                                         * Voice status callback: POST /webhooks/twilio/voice/status
                                                                                                         * (If SMS is enabled in Phase 1 later) SMS webhook endpoints are configured here, but voice provisioning must not depend on SMS configuration.
                                                                                                         5. Backend writes user_numbers row with provider_number_sid and ai_number_e164.
                                                                                                         6. Backend marks status=active only after Twilio confirms resource creation and webhook configuration.
19.2.3 Idempotency and rollback
Provisioning must be idempotent per user.
                                                                                                         * If user_numbers already exists for owner_user_id, do not buy a second number in Phase 1.
                                                                                                         * If purchase succeeds but DB write fails:
                                                                                                         * Retry DB write.
                                                                                                         * If retry window expires, release the number (delete IncomingPhoneNumber) to prevent orphan billing.
                                                                                                         * If DB write succeeds but webhook configuration fails:
                                                                                                         * Mark status pending.
                                                                                                         * Run a repair job that re-applies webhook settings until successful, then mark active.
19.2.4 Number release and suspension
Release
                                                                                                         * On account deletion or explicit release, delete the Twilio IncomingPhoneNumber resource and set internal status to suspended or hard-delete the row based on retention policy.
Suspension
                                                                                                         * If abuse threshold is exceeded, set user_numbers.status=suspended and immediately reject inbound calls at /voice/inbound for that number.
________________


19.3 Inbound call handling (dedicated and forwarded)
19.3.1 Twilio inbound request parameters we rely on
For inbound TwiML requests, Twilio provides at least:
                                                                                                         * CallSid (unique call ID)
                                                                                                         * From (caller)
                                                                                                         * To (the Twilio number called)
                                                                                                         * CallStatus
                                                                                                         * Possibly ParentCallSid (if this is not the first leg of a call)
                                                                                                         * For forwarding contexts, Twilio may provide additional fields like ForwardedFrom, depending on carrier support. (Twilio)
Forwarding detection input:
                                                                                                         * ForwardedFrom is explicitly documented as carrier-dependent. (Twilio)
19.3.2 Inbound webhook flow: /webhooks/twilio/voice/inbound
Step A: Validate and normalize
                                                                                                         1. Validate X-Twilio-Signature for the request URL and POST body. (Twilio)
                                                                                                         2. Normalize To and From to E.164-like strings when possible. Handle withheld/unknown caller cases (Twilio may send anonymous or unknown). (Twilio)
Step B: Identify the user
3. Look up the called number:
                                                                                                         * Normalize To and compute ai_number_hash.
                                                                                                         * Query user_numbers by ai_number_hash (and status=active).
                                                                                                         4. If no match:
                                                                                                         * Return TwiML <Reject> or a minimal <Say> then <Hangup> (policy decision: prefer <Reject> to avoid costs and reduce abuse surface).
Step C: Apply call access controls before streaming
5. Evaluate “Who can reach my AI number” policy (Everyone, Contacts only, VIP only) and spam/blocked lists:
                                                                                                         * If blocked or not allowed, respond with <Reject>.
Step D: Create the call record (CALL_CORE)
6. Create row in calls with:
                                                                                                         * provider=twilio
                                                                                                         * provider_call_sid = CallSid
                                                                                                         * owner_user_id from user_numbers.owner_user_id
                                                                                                         * source_type initial classification:
                                                                                                         * Default dedicated_number
                                                                                                         * If forwarding indicators exist (see Step E), set forwarded
                                                                                                         * Store caller identity encrypted:
                                                                                                         * caller_phone_ciphertext, nonce, key_version
                                                                                                         * caller_phone_hash, caller_phone_last4
Step E: Forwarding context detection and storage
7. Compute:
                                                                                                         * forwarding_detected = true if ForwardedFrom present, or if ParentCallSid present in a way that indicates a forwarded leg (do not assume; store as diagnostics).
                                                                                                         * provider_parent_call_sid when Twilio provides ParentCallSid. (Twilio)
                                                                                                         * forwarding_verification_hint string that captures what we saw:
                                                                                                         * forwarded_from_present
                                                                                                         * parent_call_sid_present
                                                                                                         * unknown
                                                                                                         8. Store ForwardedFrom value only as a diagnostic if it exists (it is often the originally dialed number, but it is not guaranteed across carriers). (Twilio)
Step F: Return TwiML for the AI conversation
9. Return TwiML that:
                                                                                                         * Starts a bidirectional stream to the Voice Gateway using <Connect><Stream>. (Twilio)
                                                                                                         * Includes custom parameters for correlation using <Parameter name="..." value="..."/>. (Twilio)
                                                                                                         * Includes a fallback message after the <Connect> block that will only run if our gateway closes the socket. (Twilio)
Reference TwiML skeleton (conceptual, with placeholders)
<Response>
  <Connect>
    <Stream url="wss://voice.<domain>/twilio/media">
      <Parameter name="call_id" value="{CALL_UUID}" />
      <Parameter name="user_id" value="{OWNER_USER_ID}" />
      <Parameter name="session_token" value="{SIGNED_EPHEMERAL_TOKEN}" />
    </Stream>
  </Connect>


  <!-- Only executed if our WebSocket server closes the Stream -->
  <Say>The assistant is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.</Say>
  <Hangup/>
</Response>
Key behaviors:
                                                                                                         * No query string on url. (Twilio)
                                                                                                         * Custom parameters delivered to the WebSocket server in the Stream start message. (Twilio)
                                                                                                         * Fallback Say executes only after socket close. (Twilio)
19.3.3 WebSocket session association (Voice Gateway)
When Twilio connects to our WebSocket server, the gateway must:
                                                                                                         1. Require TLS (wss).
                                                                                                         2. Wait for Twilio Start message and extract:
                                                                                                         * callSid
                                                                                                         * streamSid
                                                                                                         * Custom parameters passed via <Parameter> (call_id, user_id, session_token, etc.). (Twilio)
                                                                                                         3. Validate session_token (internal signature, short TTL, bound to call_id and user_id).
                                                                                                         4. Link streamSid to the internal calls.id and create call_events entries:
                                                                                                         * stream_connected
                                                                                                         * stream_start_received
                                                                                                         5. Audio handling:
                                                                                                         * Twilio sends inbound audio in WebSocket media messages as base64 payloads. (Twilio)
                                                                                                         * The gateway forwards audio to ElevenLabs real-time voice.
                                                                                                         * For AI-to-caller audio, the gateway sends Twilio a media message on the outbound track per Twilio Media Streams message format. (Twilio)
                                                                                                         6. On stream stop/error:
                                                                                                         * Log stream-stopped or stream-error events when provided via Stream status callbacks, if configured. (Twilio)
                                                                                                         * Close the socket so Twilio can proceed to fallback TwiML. (Twilio)
19.3.4 Stream status callbacks (optional but recommended)
Stream status callbacks provide lifecycle events like:
                                                                                                         * stream-started, stream-stopped, stream-error plus timestamps and error message. (Twilio)
If enabled, set <Stream statusCallback="https://api.<domain>/webhooks/twilio/voice/status" /> and route StreamEvent payloads into call_events.
________________


19.4 Live handoff and voicemail detection
Phase 1 constraint from the plan and schema: handoff target type is the user’s real phone number (no in-app VoIP takeover in Phase 1).
19.4.1 Handoff objectives and risks
Objectives
                                                                                                         * When the AI classifies a call as VIP/urgent (per handoff rules), offer handoff to the user.
                                                                                                         * If user accepts, connect caller to the user quickly.
                                                                                                         * If user does not answer, is unreachable, or voicemail answers, do not dump the caller into voicemail silently.
                                                                                                         * Prevent handoff loops (especially when the user has unconditional forwarding enabled by mistake).
Primary loop-prevention rule
                                                                                                         * Use a platform-owned handoff caller ID number when dialing the user, not the AI number that the user may have forwarded to. This reduces the chance the user’s carrier forwarding rules route the handoff attempt back into MattBot.
19.4.2 Transfer mechanism (call redirect + Dial leg)
We will implement handoff by updating the in-progress Twilio call to a new TwiML document that attempts a <Dial> to the user’s real phone number.
Twilio supports updating an in-progress call (including redirecting it and also setting completion). (Twilio)
Handoff TwiML pattern
                                                                                                         * Dial the user’s encrypted destination number.
                                                                                                         * Use a platform handoff caller ID.
                                                                                                         * Set a timeout.
                                                                                                         * Configure Answering Machine Detection on the dialed number leg.
19.4.3 Voicemail detection (Answering Machine Detection, AMD)
We use Twilio’s AMD on the dialed user leg to detect voicemail or machines and abort before connecting the caller.
AMD in TwiML is enabled via <Number machineDetection="..."> and supports tuning such as:
                                                                                                         * machineDetectionTimeout
                                                                                                         * machineDetectionSpeechThreshold
                                                                                                         * machineDetectionSpeechEndThreshold
                                                                                                         * machineDetectionSilenceTimeout
and an amdStatusCallback to receive results. (Twilio)
Policy for AMD results
                                                                                                            * If AnsweredBy indicates human: proceed with handoff.
                                                                                                            * If machine/fax/unknown: terminate the dial leg and resume AI flow.
AMD returns result values like human vs machine-related outcomes. (Twilio)
How we terminate the dial leg
                                                                                                            * Capture the child leg CallSid from the dial status callbacks (or the AMD callback).
                                                                                                            * Immediately update that child call to status=completed to stop it. Twilio allows updating calls with status values including completed. (Twilio)
