19.4.4 Returning to AI after failed handoff
Because <Connect><Stream> blocks until socket close, the handoff flow must be structured as a new TwiML segment. After the <Dial> attempt ends, Twilio can request an action URL for next steps and we can:
                                                                                                            * Resume <Connect><Stream> to return control to the AI
                                                                                                            * Or end the call with a polite message if product policy says to stop
We rely on Twilio’s documented behavior that DialCallStatus values are sent to a <Dial> action URL and correspond to CallStatus value set. (Twilio)
________________


19.5 Failure handling and fallback behaviors
19.5.1 AI streaming failure before conversation starts
Failure modes:
                                                                                                            * Voice Gateway cannot accept WebSocket
                                                                                                            * ElevenLabs session cannot be established
                                                                                                            * Internal auth token invalid
Behavior:
                                                                                                            1. Voice Gateway closes the WebSocket immediately.
                                                                                                            2. Twilio resumes the next TwiML instruction after <Connect><Stream>.
                                                                                                            3. We play a short fallback prompt and hang up (or optionally record a message if that feature is enabled later).
This relies on the <Connect><Stream> control-flow rule (subsequent verbs run only after socket close). (Twilio)
19.5.2 Mid-call failures
If the AI fails mid-call:
                                                                                                            * Close the WebSocket so Twilio falls through to fallback TwiML.
                                                                                                            * Write terminal-ish state into calls.status as partial if some conversation occurred.
                                                                                                            * Append call_events:
                                                                                                            * ai_stream_error
                                                                                                            * gateway_closed_stream_for_fallback
19.5.3 Fallback TwiML policy
Default (Phase 1 safe option):
                                                                                                            * Inform caller the assistant is unavailable and ask them to call back.
                                                                                                            * Hang up.
Optional later (if voicemail capture is allowed by product policy):
                                                                                                            * <Record> a short message and store only metadata pointers (not full audio in our DB).
________________


19.6 Cost, performance, and abuse controls at the telephony boundary
19.6.1 Duration caps
Implement a maximum call duration policy with two layers:
                                                                                                            * Soft cap: AI politely ends the call after N minutes (conversation policy).
                                                                                                            * Hard cap: provider call update timeLimit where appropriate for calls we initiate or for enforced shutdown on suspicious calls. Twilio’s call update parameters include timeLimit. (Twilio)
19.6.2 Concurrency controls
Maintain:
                                                                                                            * Global concurrency cap (system wide).
                                                                                                            * Per-user active call cap.
Enforcement point: /webhooks/twilio/voice/inbound before returning <Connect><Stream>.
If cap exceeded:
                                                                                                            * Prefer <Reject> to avoid unnecessary streaming costs.
                                                                                                            * Log abuse/concurrency event to call_events.
19.6.3 Rate limiting
Apply per-user and per-caller rate limits:
                                                                                                            * Requests per minute to /voice/inbound for a given caller_phone_hash.
                                                                                                            * Burst protection for repeat calls to the same AI number.
Actions:
                                                                                                            * Reject immediately for repeated abuse patterns.
                                                                                                            * Temporary suspension flips user_numbers.status to suspended.
19.6.4 Spam blocking and access gating
Use the routing controls already defined in the plan:
                                                                                                            * Everyone, Contacts only, VIP only
                                                                                                            * Spam detection and blocked list checks
Telephony boundary rule:
                                                                                                            * If we already know we will reject (blocked, not allowed), do it before creating a Stream.
19.6.5 Observability at the telephony boundary
Every call should produce enough events to debug end-to-end without storing sensitive audio:
Minimum call_events (examples):
                                                                                                            * twilio_inbound_webhook_received
                                                                                                            * call_record_created
                                                                                                            * stream_connect_initiated
                                                                                                            * stream_start_received (streamSid)
                                                                                                            * ai_session_connected
                                                                                                            * handoff_offered
                                                                                                            * handoff_accepted
                                                                                                            * handoff_dial_started (child callSid)
                                                                                                            * amd_result_received (AnsweredBy)
                                                                                                            * handoff_result_success|failed|expired
                                                                                                            * call_completed (status callback)
Stream status callback events (stream-started, stream-stopped, stream-error) can also be captured when configured. (Twilio)
________________


CHAPTER 20: AI PIPELINE DESIGN
20.0 Purpose and scope
This chapter defines the Phase 1 AI pipeline end to end, including the real-time call experience and the post-call artifact generation. It specifies:
                                                                                                            * How real-time speech-to-speech behavior is produced during a call
                                                                                                            * How speech-to-text is produced for screening decisions and transcript availability
                                                                                                            * How summaries, extracted fields, labels, label reasons, memory updates, and text drafts are generated
                                                                                                            * What is stored locally versus referenced from the AI provider
                                                                                                            * Prompt and policy rules that prevent impersonation, data leakage, and unsafe behavior
                                                                                                            * Failure handling, retries, and quality controls so call records are created even when parts of AI fail
Phase 1 AI provider roles:
                                                                                                            * ElevenLabs: real-time voice agent behavior and speech-to-text output for transcript
                                                                                                            * Backend AI workers: enforce policy, generate post-call artifacts, and apply deterministic validation and storage rules
20.0.1 Definitions used in this chapter
                                                                                                            * Real-time pipeline: the audio-in/audio-out path while the call is ongoing
                                                                                                            * Post-call pipeline: asynchronous jobs that run after the call ends
                                                                                                            * Call record: the user-facing record that summarizes what happened and enables follow-up actions
                                                                                                            * Extracted fields: structured values derived from the call that drive UI and actions
                                                                                                            * Labels: categorical tags such as urgent, spam, VIP, sales, normal, unknown
                                                                                                            * Label reasons: short explanations that justify labels for transparency
                                                                                                            * Prompt version: a versioned instruction set used by the voice agent or summarizer
                                                                                                            * Partial success: call record exists, but some AI artifacts are missing and clearly marked
20.1 AI pipeline high-level lifecycle
20.1.1 Canonical pipeline stages
Every inbound call follows the same AI lifecycle, regardless of whether it came from a dedicated AI number or forwarding:
A) Call start (real-time)
                                                                                                            * Create the call record shell immediately (status: in_progress)
                                                                                                            * Start real-time voice session
                                                                                                            * Run screening conversation policy (collect required fields)
                                                                                                            * Emit real-time control events (handoff offer, refusal handling, end-of-call)
B) Call end (transition)
                                                                                                            * Mark call ended with timestamps and outcome metadata
                                                                                                            * Freeze the real-time session identifiers needed for post-call retrieval
C) Post-call processing (asynchronous)
                                                                                                            * Retrieve or reference final transcript from provider
                                                                                                            * Generate summary and extracted fields
                                                                                                            * Generate labels and label reasons
                                                                                                            * Generate memory updates (default ON) using a strict allowlist
                                                                                                            * Generate text draft (approval-first send happens in Chapter 22)
                                                                                                            * Finalize call record (status: ready or partial_ready)
                                                                                                            * Notify user devices with privacy mode rules
20.1.2 Non-negotiable durability rule
A call record must exist for every answered call even if:
                                                                                                            * The real-time voice session fails mid-call
                                                                                                            * The transcript is missing or delayed
                                                                                                            * Summary or labeling jobs fail
In these cases, the call record must clearly show which artifacts are missing and whether a retry is scheduled.
20.1.3 Local storage rule for Phase 1 (transcripts and media)
Phase 1 treats the AI provider as the storage location for media and transcript artifacts. The MattBot system:
                                                                                                            * Does not store call audio files
                                                                                                            * Does not store the full transcript as a permanent database artifact by default
                                                                                                            * Stores only:
                                                                                                            * Provider references and readiness status
                                                                                                            * Derived artifacts required for product workflows (summary, extracted fields, labels, label reasons, memory entries, text drafts)
If post-call jobs require the transcript text, it may be fetched and processed in-memory or via short-lived encrypted temporary storage, then discarded after artifact generation completes.
20.2 ElevenLabs voice
20.2.1 Real-time voice session responsibilities
The ElevenLabs voice layer is responsible for producing low-latency speech output to the caller. The system must support:
                                                                                                            * Natural turn-taking (avoid long dead air)
                                                                                                            * Short, structured screening questions
                                                                                                            * Interrupt handling (caller talks over the assistant)
                                                                                                            * Recovery when caller refuses screening questions
The system must not allow the voice layer to become the source of truth for call state. Authoritative state transitions are recorded by the backend.
20.2.2 Voice selection and user settings mapping
Each user has voice configuration that maps to an ElevenLabs voice profile. The backend provides the real-time layer with a voice configuration object per call:
                                                                                                            * voice_profile_id (provider identifier)
                                                                                                            * fallback_voice_profile_id (provider identifier)
                                                                                                            * speaking_style_preset (enum, Phase 1: neutral, warm, formal, concise)
                                                                                                            * verbosity_limit (max words per assistant turn, Phase 1 default: short)
                                                                                                            * temperature_like_setting (bounded range, low variance by default)
Rules:
                                                                                                            * If the requested voice_profile_id is invalid or fails to load, use fallback_voice_profile_id
                                                                                                            * If fallback also fails, use the platform default voice profile
                                                                                                            * Voice selection must not block answering a call beyond the time-to-answer budget
20.2.3 Voice fallback hierarchy (must be deterministic)
Voice fallback is evaluated in this order:
                                                                                                            1. User voice profile
                                                                                                            2. User fallback voice profile
                                                                                                            3. Platform default voice profile
                                                                                                            4. Emergency minimal mode: short apology line and immediate message-taking behavior (text-first internally, still spoken to caller)
Each fallback choice must be recorded in call events so support can diagnose voice failures without exposing call content.
20.2.4 Real-time session start contract (Node to Python)
When the real-time service starts an ElevenLabs session, it must emit a single authoritative event to the backend:
Event: ai_session_started
Required fields:
                                                                                                            * call_id
                                                                                                            * provider_session_id
                                                                                                            * voice_profile_id_used
                                                                                                            * prompt_pack_version
                                                                                                            * started_at
This event is idempotent (same call_id, same provider_session_id). If received multiple times, only the first write changes state.
20.3 ElevenLabs STT
20.3.1 Two STT needs in Phase 1
Phase 1 uses speech-to-text in two ways:
A) Real-time assistance (for screening decisions)
                                                                                                            * Used to detect that the caller has answered a question
                                                                                                            * Used to detect urgency phrases and refusal patterns
                                                                                                            * Used to detect key entities (name, phone number spoken, time windows)
B) Post-call transcript availability (for user review and downstream artifacts)
                                                                                                            * Used to produce the transcript shown in the call record
                                                                                                            * Used as the primary input for summary, extraction, labeling, memory, and draft generation
20.3.2 Real-time STT policy (what it is allowed to do)
Real-time STT is allowed to influence only:
                                                                                                            * Conversation flow decisions (ask follow-up, clarify, move to next question)
                                                                                                            * Handoff eligibility signals (urgent detection)
                                                                                                            * Spam suspicion signals (robotic patterns, refusal, repeated pitch content)
Real-time STT must not:
                                                                                                            * Write memory entries directly
                                                                                                            * Finalize labels or summaries
                                                                                                            * Trigger outbound messages
All user-visible artifacts are produced in post-call jobs where validation and retries exist.
20.3.3 Transcript retrieval model
For each call, the system stores transcript reference metadata:
                                                                                                            * transcript_provider = elevenlabs
                                                                                                            * transcript_provider_id (or a composite key derived from provider_session_id)
                                                                                                            * transcript_status: pending, ready, failed, not_available
                                                                                                            * transcript_last_checked_at
                                                                                                            * transcript_retry_count
Post-call jobs use this reference to retrieve the transcript text when needed. The transcript text is processed and then discarded unless a short-lived encrypted cache is explicitly required for retry.
20.3.4 Transcript readiness detection strategies
Phase 1 supports both of these strategies, selectable per environment:
Strategy A: Provider callback driven
                                                                                                            * Provider signals transcript ready
                                                                                                            * Backend enqueues post-call jobs immediately
Strategy B: Polling driven
                                                                                                            * Worker checks transcript readiness using the provider reference on a schedule
                                                                                                            * Once ready, worker proceeds with artifact generation
Only one strategy should be enabled in production to avoid duplicate work. If both exist, the backend must enforce idempotency keys so duplicated triggers do not produce duplicated artifacts.
20.3.5 Speaker separation and transcript normalization
When transcript text is retrieved, it must be normalized into a canonical internal structure for processing:
                                                                                                            * An ordered list of segments
                                                                                                            * Each segment includes:
                                                                                                            * speaker_role: caller or assistant
                                                                                                            * start_ms, end_ms (best available)
                                                                                                            * text
                                                                                                            * confidence (if available)
Normalization rules:
                                                                                                            * Remove duplicated partials (keep final form where available)
                                                                                                            * Collapse repeated filler tokens when produced by STT partial streaming
                                                                                                            * Preserve timestamps if they exist (they are useful for label reasons and debugging)
20.4 Conversation policy and prompts
20.4.1 Prompt architecture (versioned prompt packs)
The system uses prompt packs to ensure consistent behavior and safe upgrades.
A prompt pack includes:
                                                                                                            * base_identity_prompt
                                                                                                            * screening_flow_prompt
                                                                                                            * refusal_and_message_taking_prompt
                                                                                                            * urgent_and_handoff_prompt
                                                                                                            * anti_spam_and_sales_prompt
                                                                                                            * closing_prompt
                                                                                                            * disclosure_prompt
                                                                                                            * forbidden_behavior_rules
Each pack has:
                                                                                                            * prompt_pack_version (integer or semantic version)
                                                                                                            * effective_date
                                                                                                            * changelog summary stored internally (not shown to end users)
Every call record stores:
                                                                                                            * prompt_pack_version used for the call
                                                                                                            * voice_profile_id used
                                                                                                            * summarizer_version used (for post-call artifacts)
20.4.2 Identity and disclosure requirements
The assistant must:
                                                                                                            * Present itself as an assistant acting on behalf of the user
                                                                                                            * Never claim to be the user
                                                                                                            * Never imply it has authority it does not have
                                                                                                            * Keep the call focused on capturing a message
Required disclosures during call opening:
                                                                                                            * The assistant is an AI assistant for the user
                                                                                                            * The call is being transcribed and summarized for the user
                                                                                                            * If recording is enabled: the assistant announces recording at the start
Disclosure must be concise and must not derail the screening flow.
20.4.3 Allowed and disallowed behaviors
Allowed:
                                                                                                            * Polite, brief small talk only when needed to keep the caller engaged
                                                                                                            * Repeating back captured information to confirm accuracy
                                                                                                            * Asking one question at a time
                                                                                                            * Asking the caller to spell names when unclear
                                                                                                            * Offering to take a message if the caller refuses screening
Disallowed:
                                                                                                            * Harassment, insults, threats
                                                                                                            * Sharing any sensitive information about the user
                                                                                                            * Making strong factual claims beyond what the caller said
                                                                                                            * Giving legal, medical, or financial advice as if authoritative
                                                                                                            * Inventing caller identity details
                                                                                                            * Stating or implying the user has approved actions that are not approved
20.4.4 Screening state machine (canonical flow)
The screening conversation must follow a predictable state machine so it is testable and debuggable.
State S0: Opening
                                                                                                            * Greeting
                                                                                                            * Identity and disclosure
                                                                                                            * Transition to S1
State S1: Identify caller
                                                                                                            * Ask for name
                                                                                                            * If caller refuses, allow “anonymous” but continue
                                                                                                            * Transition to S2
State S2: Reason for call
                                                                                                            * Ask why they are calling
                                                                                                            * Capture a one sentence reason plus details if offered
                                                                                                            * Transition to S3
State S3: Urgency
                                                                                                            * Ask how urgent it is
                                                                                                            * If caller says urgent or time-sensitive, set urgency_candidate = high
                                                                                                            * Transition to S4
State S4: Callback details
                                                                                                            * Confirm best callback number
                                                                                                            * If the caller is calling from a number they cannot be reached on, capture alternate number
                                                                                                            * Optionally ask best time window
                                                                                                            * Transition to S5
State S5: Confirmation and closing
                                                                                                            * Read back the captured message in a concise recap
                                                                                                            * Confirm accuracy
                                                                                                            * Close politely
                                                                                                            * End call
Fallback branch F1: Refusal handling
If caller refuses to answer any of S1 to S4:
                                                                                                            * Stop pressing for details
                                                                                                            * Switch to message-taking:
                                                                                                            * “What message would you like me to pass along?”
                                                                                                            * Capture message text
                                                                                                            * Ask one callback question if possible
                                                                                                            * Close
Fallback branch F2: Incoherent or abusive caller
If caller is abusive or incoherent:
                                                                                                            * Do not escalate
                                                                                                            * Offer one redirect:
                                                                                                            * “I can pass along a short message if you keep it respectful.”
                                                                                                            * If continues, end call politely
Special branch B1: Emergency phrasing
If caller indicates an immediate emergency:
                                                                                                            * Instruct them to contact emergency services
                                                                                                            * Do not attempt handoff as a substitute for emergency response
                                                                                                            * Close call
20.4.5 Handoff trigger signals from conversation
The conversation layer can produce a handoff_candidate signal when:
                                                                                                            * Caller is VIP (from VIP list match)
                                                                                                            * Caller indicates urgent need
                                                                                                            * Caller requests to speak to the user immediately
The conversation must confirm urgency when ambiguous by asking one short question (the urgency confirmation question rule). The backend will decide final eligibility in Chapter 21 logic, but the AI pipeline must produce the signal and the evidence snippets that caused it.
20.4.6 Control events emitted during real-time calls
The real-time service emits structured control events to the backend. These events are separate from transcript text and are used for state transitions.
Required events:
                                                                                                            * ai_session_started
                                                                                                            * ai_disclosure_delivered (boolean flags: transcription_disclosed, recording_disclosed)
                                                                                                            * screening_field_captured (field_name, field_value, confidence)
                                                                                                            * handoff_candidate (reason, urgency_candidate, evidence_text_short)
                                                                                                            * message_taking_mode_entered
                                                                                                            * ai_session_ended (reason: caller_hangup, assistant_end, transfer, error)
All events:
                                                                                                            * Must include call_id
                                                                                                            * Must be idempotent or safely de-duplicated
                                                                                                            * Must not include full transcript content beyond short evidence_text_short fragments
20.5 Summary and extraction logic
20.5.1 Output goals and constraints
The summary must:
                                                                                                            * Be fast to read
                                                                                                            * Be consistent across calls
                                                                                                            * Make next actions obvious
                                                                                                            * Be safe to display in-app under biometric gating
The extracted fields must:
                                                                                                            * Be strictly structured (enums, validated phone numbers, bounded text sizes)
                                                                                                            * Be stable enough to power UI and actions
                                                                                                            * Be explainable (linked to transcript evidence when possible)
20.5.2 Summary structure (Phase 1 format)
The stored summary artifact includes:
A) Human-readable summary
                                                                                                            * 3 to 6 bullet points, short lines
                                                                                                            * First bullet must describe the caller and the main reason
                                                                                                            * One bullet for urgency
                                                                                                            * One bullet for requested next step
B) Structured extraction JSON (validated)
Fields:
                                                                                                            * caller_name: string or null
                                                                                                            * caller_phone_last4: string or null
                                                                                                            * callback_phone_e164: string or null
                                                                                                            * reason: short string (max length enforced)
                                                                                                            * details: array of short bullet strings (max item count enforced)
                                                                                                            * urgency_level: enum { low, medium, high, unknown }
                                                                                                            * requested_action: enum { callback, text_back, no_action, unknown }
                                                                                                            * best_callback_window: string or null
                                                                                                            * handoff_recommended: boolean
                                                                                                            * handoff_reason: string or null
                                                                                                            * confidence: object with per-field confidence values (bounded 0 to 1)
C) Evidence map (optional but recommended)
                                                                                                            * For selected fields and labels, include 1 to 3 short transcript snippets (each snippet length bounded)
                                                                                                            * This is used for label reasons and support debugging without storing the full transcript
20.5.3 Summary generation sources and precedence
The pipeline supports multiple summarization sources, but must produce one canonical summary artifact.
Precedence order:
                                                                                                            1. Provider summarization output if available and meets schema requirements
                                                                                                            2. Internal summarizer (text model) over retrieved transcript
                                                                                                            3. Deterministic template fallback using captured screening fields only
Template fallback ensures a readable call record even when transcript or summarizer is unavailable.
20.5.4 Validation, repair, and storage rules
Validation steps:
                                                                                                            * JSON must parse
                                                                                                            * Enums must match allowed values
                                                                                                            * Text fields must be within max length
                                                                                                            * Phone numbers must pass normalization rules before being stored as e164 fields
                                                                                                            * Arrays must respect max item count
Repair steps (one retry pass maximum):
                                                                                                            * If JSON is almost valid (common formatting errors), run a repair transform and re-validate
                                                                                                            * If still invalid, discard structured extraction and fall back to template extraction from screening fields
Storage:
                                                                                                            * Summary text is stored encrypted
                                                                                                            * Structured extraction is stored as validated JSON, and sensitive subfields are encrypted if they contain free-form content
                                                                                                            * Evidence snippets are stored encrypted and truncated to strict limits
20.5.5 Failure handling and retry rules
If summary generation fails:
                                                                                                            * Mark summary_status = failed
                                                                                                            * Store failure_code and failure_stage (no transcript, provider error, model error, validation error)
                                                                                                            * Schedule retry with bounded attempts and backoff
                                                                                                            * Do not block call record availability
If transcript is pending:
                                                                                                            * Mark summary_status = waiting_on_transcript
                                                                                                            * Worker periodically retries transcript retrieval until:
                                                                                                            * transcript becomes ready, then proceeds
                                                                                                            * or max wait window expires, then falls back to template summary
20.6 Labeling logic
20.6.1 Label set (Phase 1)
Phase 1 supports these labels:
                                                                                                            * VIP
                                                                                                            * urgent
                                                                                                            * spam
                                                                                                            * sales
                                                                                                            * normal
                                                                                                            * unknown
Multiple labels may apply, but the UI must have a primary label.
Primary label selection priority:
                                                                                                            1. spam
                                                                                                            2. urgent
                                                                                                            3. VIP
                                                                                                            4. sales
                                                                                                            5. normal
                                                                                                            6. unknown
This priority is for display only. All assigned labels are stored.
20.6.2 Label inputs
Labels are computed from:
                                                                                                            * VIP list match and block list match (deterministic)
                                                                                                            * Screening fields captured during the call
                                                                                                            * Transcript evidence snippets (short, bounded)
                                                                                                            * Call pattern metadata:
                                                                                                            * repeated caller attempts
                                                                                                            * short hangups
                                                                                                            * refusal frequency
                                                                                                            * known spam patterns if configured
20.6.3 Deterministic label rules (must always run)
VIP rule:
                                                                                                            * If caller matches VIP list, assign VIP with reason “Caller matches VIP list.”
unknown rule:
                                                                                                            * If caller provides insufficient information and message-taking also fails, assign unknown.
normal rule:
                                                                                                            * If the call has a coherent reason and no other label triggers strongly, assign normal.
20.6.4 AI-assisted label rules (bounded, explainable)
urgent rule:
                                                                                                            * Assign urgent if:
                                                                                                            * caller states urgency explicitly, or
                                                                                                            * caller demands immediate action and provides a time constraint
                                                                                                            * The label reason must include a short evidence snippet.
sales rule:
                                                                                                            * Assign sales if:
                                                                                                            * caller is pitching a product or service, or
                                                                                                            * call matches common solicitation patterns
                                                                                                            * The label reason must include a short evidence snippet.
spam rule:
                                                                                                            * Assign spam if:
                                                                                                            * caller refuses to identify and repeats scripted content, or
                                                                                                            * call matches high-confidence spam patterns, or
                                                                                                            * caller volume or repetition crosses configured thresholds
                                                                                                            * spam must be conservative by default. If confidence is not high, prefer sales or unknown.
20.6.5 Label reasons format
Each label reason is stored as:
                                                                                                            * label_name
                                                                                                            * reason_text (1 to 2 short sentences)
                                                                                                            * evidence_snippets (0 to 3 short snippets)
                                                                                                            * confidence_level (enum: low, medium, high)
                                                                                                            * produced_by (deterministic or ai_assisted)
                                                                                                            * label_version (for rule evolution)
Reasons must be safe to show only inside the app (biometric gated). Notification previews must never include evidence snippets by default.
20.6.6 User overrides and precedence
User can override labels in the UI.
Rules:
                                                                                                            * Manual override always wins for that call
                                                                                                            * The system retains the original system labels for audit and learning, but they are not shown as primary once overridden
                                                                                                            * Optional propagation:
                                                                                                            * If the user marks a caller as spam or VIP, the system may suggest updating the block list or VIP list, but must not auto-change lists without explicit user action
20.7 Memory extraction and update (Phase 1)
20.7.1 Memory purpose in the AI pipeline
Memory stores small, user-useful facts that improve future call handling and summaries.
Phase 1 memory is ON by default and must be:
                                                                                                            * Visible in-app
                                                                                                            * Deletable per item and deletable in bulk
20.7.2 Memory allowlist (what can be stored automatically)
Only allowlist categories can be created automatically:
                                                                                                            * Caller display name (if provided)
                                                                                                            * Relationship tags if explicitly stated (example: “I am your dentist’s office”)
                                                                                                            * Preferred callback window if explicitly stated
                                                                                                            * Communication preference if explicitly stated (example: “Text is better”)
                                                                                                            * Repeated reason patterns (example: “Monthly billing question”)
Everything else must be stored as a suggested memory item requiring user confirmation, or not stored at all.
20.7.3 Memory item structure
Each memory item stores:
                                                                                                            * memory_type (enum)
                                                                                                            * subject (caller reference or general)
                                                                                                            * value (short string, bounded)
                                                                                                            * source_call_id
                                                                                                            * created_at
                                                                                                            * confidence
                                                                                                            * user_confirmed flag (true for allowlist automatic items, false for suggested items if implemented)
20.7.4 Memory extraction pipeline rules
Memory extraction runs after summary extraction and labeling, using:
                                                                                                            * validated extracted fields
                                                                                                            * transcript snippets only when needed, never the full transcript stored permanently
Memory extraction must never block call record readiness.
20.8 Text draft generation (Phase 1)
20.8.1 Draft generation scope
After each call, the AI pipeline generates a suggested text reply draft for the user, but it is never sent automatically.
Draft must:
                                                                                                            * Reflect the call content accurately
                                                                                                            * Be short and polite
                                                                                                            * Avoid commitments not authorized by the user
                                                                                                            * Avoid sharing sensitive user information
20.8.2 Draft content rules
Draft should include:
                                                                                                            * A greeting
                                                                                                            * A one sentence acknowledgment
                                                                                                            * A question or next step request if needed
                                                                                                            * A closing
Draft should not include:
                                                                                                            * Attachments
                                                                                                            * Links by default
                                                                                                            * Sensitive details from the call unless necessary
Draft generation consumes the summary and extracted fields, not raw transcript, whenever possible.
20.9 Quality controls, monitoring, and cost tracking
20.9.1 Required metrics (Phase 1)
Track these at minimum:
Real-time:
                                                                                                            * time_to_first_audio_out_ms
                                                                                                            * ai_session_start_success_rate
                                                                                                            * real_time_disconnect_rate
Post-call:
                                                                                                            * transcript_ready_rate within target window
                                                                                                            * summary_success_rate
                                                                                                            * labeling_success_rate
                                                                                                            * memory_update_success_rate
                                                                                                            * draft_generation_success_rate
                                                                                                            * partial_ready_rate
Quality:
                                                                                                            * average summary length
                                                                                                            * percentage of calls missing required extracted fields
                                                                                                            * urgent label false-positive review rate (admin sampled)
                                                                                                            * spam label false-positive review rate (admin sampled)
Cost:
                                                                                                            * STT seconds per call
                                                                                                            * TTS seconds per call
                                                                                                            * summarizer tokens or compute per call (if applicable)
20.9.2 Logging and data minimization rules
Logs must never include:
                                                                                                            * Full transcripts
                                                                                                            * Full summaries
                                                                                                            * Raw audio
                                                                                                            * Full phone numbers
Logs may include:
                                                                                                            * call_id
                                                                                                            * artifact statuses
                                                                                                            * failure codes
                                                                                                            * truncated, redacted evidence snippets only when strictly necessary for debugging, and still bounded
20.9.3 Prompt upgrades and regression safety
Prompt changes must be controlled by:
                                                                                                            * prompt_pack_version
                                                                                                            * feature flag gating
                                                                                                            * staged rollout (small percentage, then expand)
                                                                                                            * automated regression tests that verify:
                                                                                                            * required fields are captured
                                                                                                            * refusal fallback triggers correctly
                                                                                                            * impersonation never occurs
                                                                                                            * disclosure is delivered
                                                                                                            * urgent candidate logic triggers only when supported by transcript evidence
20.10 Completion checklist for this chapter
This chapter is complete when:
                                                                                                            * Real-time AI session start and end events are fully specified and implemented
                                                                                                            * Voice selection and fallback hierarchy is implemented and observable
                                                                                                            * Transcript reference model is implemented with clear statuses and bounded retries
                                                                                                            * Summary and extraction schema is implemented with strict validation and deterministic fallback
                                                                                                            * Labeling rules and label reasons are implemented, including override precedence
                                                                                                            * Memory extraction allowlist and item structure are implemented and deletable in-app
                                                                                                            * Text draft generation produces drafts but cannot send without explicit approval
                                                                                                            * All failure modes still result in a durable call record with clear missing-artifact indicators
                                                                                                            * Monitoring metrics exist for real-time and post-call stages, and logs follow data minimization rules
________________


CHAPTER 21: LIVE HANDOFF TECHNICAL DESIGN
21.0 Purpose and scope
This chapter defines the complete Phase 1 live handoff system, meaning how a user can take over a call that the AI is currently screening.
Phase 1 live handoff requirements covered here:
                                                                                                            * Eligibility rules (VIP, urgent, VIP plus urgent, off)
                                                                                                            * Urgency confirmation question rule
                                                                                                            * Predictable timing rules (minimum screening before prompting, unless highly urgent)
                                                                                                            * Handoff user prompt and notification behavior with privacy mode support
                                                                                                            * Multi-device acceptance arbitration (only one device can win)
                                                                                                            * Telephony call control sequence to connect the caller to the user’s real phone number
                                                                                                            * Failure behavior (if handoff fails, AI continues, no dead air)
                                                                                                            * Timeout rules (20 seconds offer window)
                                                                                                            * Loop prevention and suppression rules to avoid repeated forwarding loops and repeated handoff attempts
                                                                                                            * Storage, events, observability, and QA test plan
Phase 1 handoff destination decision:
                                                                                                            * Phase 1 handoff connects the caller to the user’s real phone number using telephony transfer.
                                                                                                            * In-app VoIP takeover is explicitly not required in Phase 1. The system stores hooks so in-app VoIP can be added later without changing core call state or arbitration logic.
21.1 Concepts and data primitives
21.1.1 Handoff offer definition
A handoff offer is a time-bound opportunity for the user to take over a live call.
A handoff offer has:
                                                                                                            * A unique offer identifier
                                                                                                            * A call identifier (call_id)
                                                                                                            * A created timestamp
                                                                                                            * An expires timestamp
                                                                                                            * A status
                                                                                                            * A selected device identifier (only present if accepted)
                                                                                                            * A single attempt counter (Phase 1 max 1)
                                                                                                            * Preview payload fields used for notification and UI
                                                                                                            * Safety flags for loop detection and suppression
21.1.2 Canonical statuses
Handoff offer status enum:
                                                                                                            * not_applicable
                                                                                                            * eligible_pending_minimum_data
                                                                                                            * offered
                                                                                                            * accepted
                                                                                                            * declined
                                                                                                            * expired
                                                                                                            * transfer_starting
                                                                                                            * transfer_connected
                                                                                                            * transfer_failed
                                                                                                            * transfer_cancelled (call ended before completion)
21.1.3 Required call record fields
The call record must store, at minimum:
                                                                                                            * handoff_setting_mode: off, vip_only, urgent_only, vip_and_urgent
                                                                                                            * handoff_eligible: boolean
                                                                                                            * handoff_offered_at
                                                                                                            * handoff_offer_expires_at
                                                                                                            * handoff_accepted_at (nullable)
                                                                                                            * handoff_status (enum above)
                                                                                                            * handoff_selected_device_id (nullable)
                                                                                                            * handoff_attempt_count (integer, Phase 1 max 1)
                                                                                                            * handoff_failure_reason (nullable, bounded string)
                                                                                                            * handoff_loop_detected: boolean
                                                                                                            * handoff_preview: caller display (masked), one-line reason snippet (bounded), urgency badge (enum)
21.1.4 Required events for traceability
The system must write discrete events so handoff behavior is debuggable without storing sensitive content:
                                                                                                            * handoff_eligibility_reached
                                                                                                            * handoff_offer_created
                                                                                                            * handoff_offer_push_sent (per device)
                                                                                                            * handoff_offer_ack_received (optional, if app acks delivery)
                                                                                                            * handoff_accept_received (device_id)
                                                                                                            * handoff_decline_received (device_id)
                                                                                                            * handoff_offer_expired
                                                                                                            * handoff_transfer_starting
                                                                                                            * handoff_transfer_connected
                                                                                                            * handoff_transfer_failed (reason code)
                                                                                                            * handoff_transfer_returned_to_ai
                                                                                                            * handoff_cancelled_call_ended
21.2 Eligibility rules
21.2.1 User setting modes
User setting for live handoff controls whether the system ever offers takeover:
                                                                                                            * Off
                                                                                                            * VIP only
                                                                                                            * Urgent only
                                                                                                            * VIP plus urgent
If Off, no handoff offer is created, no notification is sent, and accept endpoints reject.
21.2.2 Deterministic VIP evaluation
VIP evaluation is deterministic and must not depend on AI inference.
A call is VIP if the caller matches a VIP allow list entry. Matching rules (Phase 1):
                                                                                                            * Primary match: caller phone hash matches a VIP entry phone hash
                                                                                                            * Secondary match (optional): normalized caller name match if the user created name-only VIP entries
VIP match result must be available early in the call so it can influence timing.
21.2.3 Urgent evaluation inputs
Urgency is produced as a candidate signal during screening. Inputs:
                                                                                                            * AI urgent_candidate event with a short evidence snippet (bounded)
                                                                                                            * Structured screening responses (explicit urgency answer)
                                                                                                            * Optional heuristics (time constraints mentioned, emergency language)
Urgency must be conservative. If uncertain, do not trigger urgent by default.
21.2.4 Urgency confirmation question rule
If the call becomes urgent_candidate, the assistant must ask exactly one confirmation question before the system treats the call as urgent for handoff, unless the call is clearly an emergency case.
Rule set:
                                                                                                            * If the caller explicitly says it is urgent and they need the user now, confirmation may be considered implicit and the system can treat it as urgent_confirmed.
                                                                                                            * Otherwise, the assistant asks one short confirmation question. After that single question, it must proceed with screening and must not repeat urgency probing.
Confirmation question outcomes:
                                                                                                            * If confirmed, urgent_confirmed = true
                                                                                                            * If not confirmed or ambiguous, urgent_confirmed = false and the system does not create an urgent-based offer
Emergency phrasing branch:
                                                                                                            * If the caller indicates an immediate emergency, the assistant must instruct emergency services routing and must not use handoff as an emergency substitute. No offer is created unless the product explicitly permits it later.
21.2.5 Combined rule evaluation
Eligibility is computed as:
                                                                                                            * If handoff mode is VIP only: eligible if vip_match true
