                                                                                                            * If handoff mode is Urgent only: eligible if urgent_confirmed true
                                                                                                            * If handoff mode is VIP plus urgent: eligible if vip_match true OR urgent_confirmed true
                                                                                                            * If handoff mode is Off: eligible false
21.2.6 Minimum screening requirement before prompting
Phase 1 must keep handoff timing predictable:
                                                                                                            * Default minimum requirement: capture at least caller name (or anonymous) and a one-line reason before creating an offer
                                                                                                            * Exception: highly urgent calls may bypass minimum and offer earlier
Implementation detail:
                                                                                                            * The AI pipeline emits screening_field_captured events.
                                                                                                            * The backend computes minimum_satisfied when it has both name status and reason.
                                                                                                            * When eligible becomes true but minimum_satisfied is false, the handoff status becomes eligible_pending_minimum_data.
                                                                                                            * When minimum_satisfied becomes true, the system creates the offer.
21.2.7 After-hours interaction
After-hours behavior affects how aggressive handoff is:
                                                                                                            * Default Phase 1 behavior: still allow handoff when eligibility rules match, but reduce non-urgent handoff prompts.
                                                                                                            * Practical enforcement: if after-hours and the user mode is VIP only, still allow. If after-hours and urgent confirmed, allow. Otherwise no offer.
21.2.8 Suppression rules (no repeated offers)
A call must produce at most one handoff offer.
Suppression triggers that permanently block additional offers for the call:
                                                                                                            * offer already created
                                                                                                            * offer declined
                                                                                                            * offer expired
                                                                                                            * transfer attempted (success or failure)
                                                                                                            * loop detected
                                                                                                            * call ended
21.3 Offer creation, preview payload, and privacy modes
21.3.1 Offer creation moment
When eligibility becomes true and minimum screening is satisfied (or bypassed by urgency exception), the backend creates an offer:
                                                                                                            * handoff_status = offered
                                                                                                            * handoff_offered_at = now
                                                                                                            * handoff_offer_expires_at = now + 20 seconds
                                                                                                            * handoff_attempt_count remains unchanged (attempt is counted only when transfer begins)
                                                                                                            * handoff_preview is generated and stored
21.3.2 Preview payload constraints
Preview payload must be useful but safe:
                                                                                                            * caller display: either caller name if captured, or masked number if known, or “Unknown caller”
                                                                                                            * one-line reason snippet: maximum length enforced and content trimmed
                                                                                                            * urgency badge: low, medium, high, unknown
The reason snippet must not include large free-form content. It is a preview, not a transcript.
21.3.3 Privacy mode behavior for notifications
The same offer creates a push notification. Content depends on user notification privacy mode:
Private mode:
                                                                                                            * Title: Call takeover available
                                                                                                            * Body: no caller identity, no reason, no urgency details
Preview mode:
                                                                                                            * Title: Take over call
                                                                                                            * Body: caller name or masked number, one-line reason, urgency badge
21.3.4 Offer delivery targets
The offer notification is sent to all active devices for the user account.
Device eligibility:
                                                                                                            * Must be a valid device session (not revoked)
                                                                                                            * Must have a live push token
                                                                                                            * Optional: if a device has “handoff prompts disabled” locally, skip it (Phase 1 can omit this per-device toggle)
21.3.5 Delivery acknowledgments (optional but recommended)
If the mobile app supports it, it can ack receipt:
                                                                                                            * POST /handoff/offers/{offer_id}/ack
                                                                                                            * Stored as event handoff_offer_ack_received
This improves diagnostics but is not required for correctness.
21.4 Multi-device acceptance arbitration
21.4.1 Goals
                                                                                                            * All devices can be notified quickly
                                                                                                            * Only one device can accept and “win”
                                                                                                            * All other devices are informed immediately that takeover is no longer available
                                                                                                            * Accept and decline are safe under concurrency, retries, and duplicate taps
21.4.2 Server-side lock design
Arbitration is implemented with a single atomic update on the offer row.
Offer table must support conditional update:
                                                                                                            * Accept succeeds only if:
                                                                                                            * status is offered
                                                                                                            * now is before expires_at
                                                                                                            * selected_device_id is null
Accept transaction logic:
                                                                                                            * Begin transaction
                                                                                                            * Attempt conditional update:
                                                                                                            * set status = accepted
                                                                                                            * set selected_device_id = requesting device_id
                                                                                                            * set accepted_at = now
                                                                                                            * If affected rows = 1, this device wins
                                                                                                            * If affected rows = 0, this device loses (already accepted, declined, expired, cancelled, or not offered)
21.4.3 Accept endpoint behavior
POST /handoff/offers/{offer_id}/accept
Request fields:
                                                                                                            * device_id (server-tracked)
                                                                                                            * idempotency_key (per device, per offer)
Response fields:
                                                                                                            * result: won, lost, expired, declined, cancelled
                                                                                                            * next_state: accepted or unchanged
                                                                                                            * server_time
                                                                                                            * offer_expires_at (for client reconciliation)
Client requirements:
                                                                                                            * If won, display “Connecting” state and keep screen awake
                                                                                                            * If lost or expired, close the takeover UI state and continue normal call record view
21.4.4 Decline endpoint behavior
POST /handoff/offers/{offer_id}/decline
Decline semantics in Phase 1:
                                                                                                            * A decline from any device declines the offer globally.
                                                                                                            * This prevents repeated prompts across devices and matches user intent that they do not want to take the call.
Decline transaction:
                                                                                                            * Conditional update:
                                                                                                            * if status is offered and not expired, set status = declined, set declined_at = now
                                                                                                            * Notify other devices that the offer is no longer available
21.4.5 Expiration behavior (20 seconds)
Offer TTL is 20 seconds.
Expiration enforcement points:
                                                                                                            * At accept time: accept must fail if now is after expires_at
                                                                                                            * Background sweeper: a job marks offers expired if status still offered after expires_at
When expired:
                                                                                                            * status = expired
                                                                                                            * event handoff_offer_expired
                                                                                                            * notify devices to dismiss takeover prompt
21.4.6 Race conditions and retries
Design must handle:
                                                                                                            * Two devices accept at the same time
                                                                                                            * One device declines while another accepts
                                                                                                            * Push arrives late and accept happens after expiry
                                                                                                            * App retries accept due to network failures
Idempotency rules:
                                                                                                            * Accept is idempotent per device using idempotency_key. If the same device repeats accept with same key, return same outcome.
                                                                                                            * Decline is idempotent similarly.
21.5 Telephony takeover execution
21.5.1 Phase 1 transfer mechanism summary
When a device wins acceptance, the backend initiates a telephony transfer that connects the caller to the user’s real phone number.
Constraints:
                                                                                                            * The AI conversation stream cannot remain active while the call is being dialed to the user.
                                                                                                            * If transfer fails, the call must return to AI screening immediately and continue.
21.5.2 Transfer pre-checks
Before starting transfer, the backend must verify:
                                                                                                            * Call is still in progress (not completed)
                                                                                                            * Offer status is accepted
                                                                                                            * handoff_attempt_count is 0 (Phase 1 single attempt)
                                                                                                            * User has a valid verified destination phone number stored
                                                                                                            * Loop suppression is not active (handoff_loop_detected false)
If any check fails:
                                                                                                            * Set offer status transfer_cancelled or transfer_failed with reason
                                                                                                            * Notify devices
                                                                                                            * AI continues and call record is created normally
21.5.3 Increment attempt counter and set transfer_starting
When the transfer starts:
                                                                                                            * handoff_attempt_count += 1
                                                                                                            * handoff_status = transfer_starting
                                                                                                            * event handoff_transfer_starting
This counter ensures no second transfer attempt occurs later in the same call.
21.5.4 Transfer TwiML behavior
Transfer uses a dedicated handoff TwiML response that:
                                                                                                            * Plays a short connecting message
                                                                                                            * Dials the user destination number with a timeout
                                                                                                            * Enables answering machine detection so voicemail does not capture the caller
                                                                                                            * Routes dial results to an action URL that decides whether to return to AI
Transfer TwiML requirements:
                                                                                                            * Caller ID for the user leg must use a platform-owned handoff caller ID number that is not the AI number.
                                                                                                            * Dial timeout must be bounded (Phase 1 default 20 seconds).
                                                                                                            * If voicemail or machine is detected, the system must abort and return to AI.
                                                                                                            * If the user answers as a human, connect the caller to the user.
21.5.5 Returning to AI after transfer failure
If transfer fails for any reason:
                                                                                                            * The system must reconnect the caller back into AI screening without requiring the caller to redial.
                                                                                                            * The AI should immediately continue with message-taking or resume screening from its current state, and it must avoid repeating too many questions.
Call record requirements:
                                                                                                            * handoff_offered: yes
                                                                                                            * handoff_accepted: yes
                                                                                                            * handoff_result: failed
                                                                                                            * failure reason code stored
AI behavior requirement:
                                                                                                            * No dead air. The caller should hear either ringing during the dial attempt, or an immediate message then continued screening after returning.
21.5.6 Transfer success behavior
If the user answers as a human:
                                                                                                            * The caller is bridged to the user
                                                                                                            * AI stops participating
                                                                                                            * The call record is finalized with:
                                                                                                            * handoff_accepted: yes
                                                                                                            * handoff_result: connected
                                                                                                            * transfer_connected_at timestamp
Post-call summary rules:
                                                                                                            * Summary must note that the user took over
                                                                                                            * Screening fields captured before takeover remain stored
                                                                                                            * Transcript handling depends on availability of speech-to-text around the transfer boundary. If transcript is partial, mark as partial.
21.6 Loop prevention strategy
21.6.1 Why loops happen
If the user has call forwarding configured and is still busy or unreachable, an attempted dial to the user can be forwarded back to the AI number. Without loop prevention, this can create repeated inbound calls and uncontrolled cost.
21.6.2 Core loop prevention rules
Phase 1 loop prevention is implemented through layered rules:
Rule A: Single handoff attempt per call
                                                                                                            * Enforced by handoff_attempt_count max 1
Rule B: Dedicated handoff caller ID
                                                                                                            * The dial to the user uses a platform-owned caller ID that is not the AI number.
                                                                                                            * This increases the chance the user recognizes it and reduces misrouting confusion.
Rule C: Loop signature detection on inbound calls
If a forwarded loop returns to the AI number, the inbound webhook will often show:
                                                                                                            * To equals the AI number
                                                                                                            * From equals the platform handoff caller ID
When this pattern is detected within a short time window after a transfer attempt:
                                                                                                            * Mark handoff_loop_detected = true on the original call
                                                                                                            * Reject the looping inbound call immediately
                                                                                                            * Record event handoff_transfer_returned_to_ai
                                                                                                            * Do not attempt any further handoff for the original call
Rule D: Temporary suppression window per user destination
Maintain an in-memory or database suppression record:
                                                                                                            * destination_user_phone_hash
                                                                                                            * suppression_expires_at (short TTL, for example 60 to 120 seconds)
                                                                                                            * reason: loop_detected or repeated failures
If suppression active, do not initiate transfer. Continue AI screening only.
21.6.3 Interaction with forwarding misconfiguration detection
If loop detection occurs or if many calls are answered by AI when the user is likely available, the system must:
                                                                                                            * Flag the account for “forwarding may be unconditional” warning in-app
                                                                                                            * Provide a guided checklist to fix forwarding settings
                                                                                                            * In Phase 1, the system must not auto-change forwarding. It only detects and warns.
21.6.4 Suppression of repeated offers within the same call
Even if conditions remain eligible (VIP or urgent) after a failed transfer, do not re-offer takeover.
This avoids:
                                                                                                            * Spammy user experience
                                                                                                            * Repeat transfer costs
                                                                                                            * Increased loop risk
21.7 Timeout and decline behavior
21.7.1 Offer timeout (20 seconds)
Offer window is 20 seconds from creation.
If timeout occurs:
                                                                                                            * status becomes expired
                                                                                                            * AI continues screening as normal
                                                                                                            * call record marks handoff offered yes, accepted no
21.7.2 Decline behavior
If the user declines:
                                                                                                            * status becomes declined
                                                                                                            * AI continues screening as normal
                                                                                                            * no further offers for this call
21.7.3 Failure behavior after acceptance
If the user accepts but transfer fails:
                                                                                                            * AI returns and continues screening immediately
                                                                                                            * call record marks offered yes, accepted yes, result failed
Failure reasons (bounded enum examples):
                                                                                                            * user_no_answer
                                                                                                            * voicemail_detected
                                                                                                            * user_line_busy
                                                                                                            * telephony_error
                                                                                                            * loop_detected
                                                                                                            * call_ended_before_transfer
21.8 Sequence diagrams (logical)
21.8.1 Success path (offer, accept, connect)
                                                                                                            1. AI screening starts
                                                                                                            2. Eligibility satisfied (VIP or urgent confirmed)
                                                                                                            3. Minimum data satisfied (name and reason)
                                                                                                            4. Offer created, push sent to all devices
                                                                                                            5. One device accepts and wins arbitration
                                                                                                            6. Transfer starts
                                                                                                            7. User answers phone, caller bridged to user
                                                                                                            8. Call ends, call record finalized
21.8.2 Ignore path (offer, no response)
                                                                                                            1. Offer created, push sent
                                                                                                            2. No device responds within 20 seconds
                                                                                                            3. Offer expires
                                                                                                            4. AI continues screening, ends normally
                                                                                                            5. Call record finalized with offered yes, accepted no
21.8.3 Decline path (offer, decline)
                                                                                                            1. Offer created, push sent
                                                                                                            2. Any device declines
                                                                                                            3. Offer becomes declined globally
                                                                                                            4. AI continues screening, ends normally
                                                                                                            5. Call record finalized with offered yes, accepted no
21.8.4 Failure path (accept, transfer fails, return to AI)
                                                                                                            1. Offer accepted
                                                                                                            2. Transfer starts and caller hears connecting and ringback
                                                                                                            3. Transfer fails due to no answer or voicemail detection
                                                                                                            4. System reconnects to AI
                                                                                                            5. AI continues, ends normally
                                                                                                            6. Call record finalized with offered yes, accepted yes, result failed
21.8.5 Multi-device race (two accepts)
                                                                                                            1. Offer created
                                                                                                            2. Device A accept and Device B accept arrive nearly simultaneously
                                                                                                            3. Database conditional update allows only one to set selected_device_id
                                                                                                            4. Winner receives won response
                                                                                                            5. Loser receives lost response
                                                                                                            6. Loser is notified offer taken
21.9 APIs and realtime events for the mobile app
21.9.1 Required mobile endpoints
                                                                                                            * POST /handoff/offers/{offer_id}/accept
                                                                                                            * POST /handoff/offers/{offer_id}/decline
                                                                                                            * Optional: POST /handoff/offers/{offer_id}/ack
                                                                                                            * Optional: GET /calls/{call_id}/handoff (current status for UI recovery)
21.9.2 Realtime events (recommended)
To keep UI consistent across devices, the backend should emit realtime events:
                                                                                                            * handoff_offer_created
                                                                                                            * handoff_offer_taken (selected_device_id masked to other devices)
                                                                                                            * handoff_offer_expired
                                                                                                            * handoff_transfer_starting
                                                                                                            * handoff_transfer_connected
                                                                                                            * handoff_transfer_failed
If realtime events are not available, push notifications plus periodic polling must be used, but that increases latency and complexity.
21.9.3 UI state requirements (Phase 1)
When takeover is available, the app must show:
                                                                                                            * caller display (masked depending on privacy)
                                                                                                            * one-line reason
                                                                                                            * urgency badge
                                                                                                            * Accept and Decline buttons
                                                                                                            * Countdown timer reflecting expires_at
After accept:
                                                                                                            * Show “Connecting” state
                                                                                                            * If transfer succeeds: show “Connected on phone” and keep call record accessible
                                                                                                            * If transfer fails: show “Not available, AI continued” and open call record
21.10 Safety, security, and abuse protections
21.10.1 Authorization requirements
Accept and decline endpoints require:
                                                                                                            * Valid authenticated session
                                                                                                            * Device must be active and not revoked
                                                                                                            * Offer must belong to the authenticated user
21.10.2 Replay prevention
                                                                                                            * Accept and decline require idempotency_key
                                                                                                            * Requests are rejected if timestamp skew is unreasonable (optional)
                                                                                                            * Offers cannot be reused after expiry or after status changes
21.10.3 Cost control
Handoff can trigger telephony costs. Controls:
                                                                                                            * Max one handoff attempt per call
                                                                                                            * Rate limit handoff accept attempts per user per time window
                                                                                                            * Optional global kill switch to disable handoff if costs spike or incidents occur
21.11 QA and test plan for live handoff
21.11.1 Functional test cases
                                                                                                            * VIP call triggers offer after name and reason captured
                                                                                                            * Urgent call triggers confirmation question, then offer only if confirmed
                                                                                                            * Offer expires after 20 seconds with AI continuing
                                                                                                            * Decline cancels offer globally and AI continues
                                                                                                            * Accept triggers transfer and connects to user when answered
                                                                                                            * Accept triggers transfer and returns to AI on no answer
                                                                                                            * Accept triggers transfer and returns to AI on voicemail detection
                                                                                                            * Call ends before accept, accept returns cancelled outcome
                                                                                                            * Offer created but push delayed, accept after expiry returns expired outcome
21.11.2 Multi-device tests
                                                                                                            * Two devices accept simultaneously, only one wins
                                                                                                            * Device A declines while Device B accepts, deterministic outcome enforced
                                                                                                            * Revoked device cannot accept
                                                                                                            * Device without push token is not included in delivery list
                                                                                                            * Device time skew does not break server-side expiry
21.11.3 Loop prevention tests
                                                                                                            * Simulate user forwarding that routes the handoff dial back to AI number
                                                                                                            * Verify loop signature detection rejects inbound loop call
                                                                                                            * Verify original call marks loop_detected and does not retry handoff
                                                                                                            * Verify suppression window prevents immediate repeat transfer attempts
21.11.4 Reliability and degradation tests
                                                                                                            * Telephony provider dial error returns to AI
                                                                                                            * AI stream reconnection succeeds after failed transfer
                                                                                                            * Database temporary failure during accept does not allow double acceptance
                                                                                                            * Background sweeper expires offers correctly even if no device responds
21.12 Completion checklist for this chapter
This chapter is complete when:
                                                                                                            * Eligibility rules are implemented exactly as settings specify (off, VIP only, urgent only, VIP plus urgent)
                                                                                                            * Urgency confirmation question rule is enforced and measurable
                                                                                                            * Minimum screening requirement is enforced, with an urgency bypass for highly urgent calls
                                                                                                            * Offer creation, preview payload, and privacy-mode notification content are implemented
                                                                                                            * Offer TTL is 20 seconds with correct expired behavior
                                                                                                            * Multi-device arbitration guarantees a single winner using atomic server-side locking
                                                                                                            * Transfer attempts are limited to one per call and failure returns to AI with no dead air
                                                                                                            * Loop prevention includes caller ID strategy, inbound loop signature detection, and suppression window
                                                                                                            * Call record stores offered, accepted, and transfer results reliably
                                                                                                            * QA plan passes across success, decline, ignore, failure, multi-device races, and loop scenarios
________________


CHAPTER 22: MESSAGING AND APPROVAL WORKFLOW
22.0 Purpose and scope
This chapter defines how Phase 1 supports texting back as a follow-up action from a call record, using an approval-first workflow. It covers:
                                                                                                            * Outbound SMS sending through Twilio
                                                                                                            * The approval-first enforcement model, including how the backend guarantees “no send without approval”
                                                                                                            * Template library and AI draft generation rules
                                                                                                            * Message state tracking (drafted, approved, sent, delivered, failed)
                                                                                                            * Retry behavior and idempotency to prevent duplicate sends
                                                                                                            * Logging, auditing, and data minimization for outbound messages
                                                                                                            * Retention and deletion behavior for message content as part of call records
Phase 1 intent: texting is an action attached to a call record, not a full messaging inbox.
22.0.1 Core Phase 1 requirements this chapter enforces
                                                                                                            * Provider for outbound texting: Twilio
                                                                                                            * Approval-first is required by default for all outbound texts
                                                                                                            * AI drafts a suggested text after every call
                                                                                                            * No attachments in Phase 1
                                                                                                            * Message send result and status must be stored on the call record
                                                                                                            * Failures must be visible and retryable without losing the draft
22.1 Architecture and responsibility boundaries
22.1.1 Component responsibilities
Mobile app
                                                                                                            * Displays “Text back” action on call detail
                                                                                                            * Shows draft preview screen, allows editing, template selection
                                                                                                            * Submits approval (and final message body) to backend
                                                                                                            * Displays status and retry options
                                                                                                            * Applies privacy behavior for notifications and UI previews
Backend API (FastAPI)
                                                                                                            * Owns approval-first enforcement and action state machine
                                                                                                            * Stores draft, approval, and send attempt records
                                                                                                            * Validates recipient number and user permissions
                                                                                                            * Enqueues outbound send jobs
                                                                                                            * Receives Twilio delivery callbacks and updates status
                                                                                                            * Provides templates and draft generation endpoints
Background workers (SQS-driven)
                                                                                                            * Executes outbound send to Twilio
                                                                                                            * Retries transient failures with safe idempotency
                                                                                                            * Updates action state based on send result
                                                                                                            * Emits user notification events for send completion or failure
Twilio
                                                                                                            * Accepts outbound SMS send requests
                                                                                                            * Returns provider message identifiers
                                                                                                            * Emits status callbacks for delivery outcomes if enabled
22.1.2 The unified action and approval pipeline principle
Text back must be implemented as an action pipeline, not a one-off endpoint. Phase 1 establishes a reusable pattern:
                                                                                                            * Action object with a lifecycle and explicit approval step
                                                                                                            * Deterministic server-side checks at each state transition
                                                                                                            * Provider connector execution in background jobs
                                                                                                            * Auditable state and minimal logging
This pattern is designed to extend later to other outbound connectors (email and messaging) without changing the approval model.
22.2 Messaging identity and number selection
22.2.1 Outbound “from” number selection rules
Every outbound SMS must have a valid platform-managed sending number. Phase 1 supports two user situations:
A) User has a dedicated AI number
                                                                                                            * If the user’s AI number is SMS-capable, use it as the SMS “from” number.
                                                                                                            * This keeps the experience consistent: caller sees the same number that handled the call.
B) User does not have a dedicated AI number (forwarding-only users)
                                                                                                            * Assign the user a platform messaging number from a shared pool (SMS-capable Twilio numbers) and store it as the user’s messaging identity.
                                                                                                            * This number is used only for outbound follow-ups and is not advertised as a dedicated AI line unless product UX explicitly exposes it.
22.2.2 Capability tracking
The system must track number capabilities so sending does not fail due to a voice-only number. For each number we store:
                                                                                                            * e164
                                                                                                            * provider_number_sid
                                                                                                            * capabilities: voice_inbound, sms_outbound
                                                                                                            * status: active, pending, suspended
If a user attempts to text back and no SMS-capable number is assigned, the backend must:
                                                                                                            * Block send
                                                                                                            * Create a visible failure state in the UI: “Texting not available yet”
                                                                                                            * Trigger a provisioning or assignment repair job if applicable
22.2.3 Blocking and user intent constraints
If the caller is blocked by the user, Phase 1 should treat texting as not applicable for that call:
                                                                                                            * Hide or disable “Text back” for blocked callers
                                                                                                            * Prevent send server-side even if the client tries
22.3 Data model for messaging actions
22.3.1 Core objects
Phase 1 stores outbound texting as an action attached to a call record.
Action object (text_back_action)
                                                                                                            * action_id (public id)
                                                                                                            * owner_user_id
                                                                                                            * call_id
                                                                                                            * action_type = text_back
                                                                                                            * status (see 22.3.2)
                                                                                                            * from_number_e164 (or from_number_id)
                                                                                                            * to_number_e164 (or to_number_hash plus last4 plus optional encrypted full)
                                                                                                            * draft_body_ciphertext
                                                                                                            * final_body_ciphertext (nullable, set when user edits or approves)
                                                                                                            * template_id_used (nullable)
                                                                                                            * prompt_pack_version (nullable, if AI drafted)
                                                                                                            * created_at, updated_at
                                                                                                            * approved_at (nullable)
                                                                                                            * approved_by_device_id (nullable)
                                                                                                            * last_error_code (nullable, bounded)
                                                                                                            * last_error_message_short (nullable, bounded)
                                                                                                            * retention_expires_at (derived from call retention policy)
Send attempt object (text_send_attempt)
                                                                                                            * attempt_id
                                                                                                            * action_id
                                                                                                            * attempt_number (1, 2, 3...)
                                                                                                            * idempotency_key
                                                                                                            * provider = twilio
                                                                                                            * provider_message_sid (nullable until accepted)
                                                                                                            * provider_status (queued, accepted, delivered, failed, undelivered, unknown)
                                                                                                            * provider_error_code (nullable)
                                                                                                            * provider_error_message_short (nullable)
                                                                                                            * started_at, finished_at
22.3.2 Message status model
For a clear user experience, Phase 1 uses a small set of user-visible states, while internal states may be more granular.
User-visible status
                                                                                                            * Draft ready (not approved)
                                                                                                            * Sending
                                                                                                            * Sent
                                                                                                            * Delivered (optional if delivery callbacks are enabled)
                                                                                                            * Failed
Internal status enum for action lifecycle
                                                                                                            * drafted
                                                                                                            * awaiting_approval
                                                                                                            * approved
                                                                                                            * sending
                                                                                                            * sent
                                                                                                            * delivered
                                                                                                            * failed
                                                                                                            * cancelled
Mapping rules
                                                                                                            * drafted and awaiting_approval render as “Draft ready”
                                                                                                            * approved and sending render as “Sending”
                                                                                                            * sent renders as “Sent”
                                                                                                            * delivered renders as “Delivered”
                                                                                                            * failed renders as “Failed”
                                                                                                            * cancelled is not shown as success and should revert UI to “Draft ready” with the user’s last draft preserved
22.3.3 Encryption and sensitive fields
Message bodies are sensitive and must be encrypted at rest. Store:
                                                                                                            * draft_body_ciphertext and final_body_ciphertext encrypted
                                                                                                            * to_number stored as:
                                                                                                            * to_number_hash (for lookup and dedupe)
                                                                                                            * to_number_last4 (for UI)
                                                                                                            * encrypted full to_number_e164 if needed for send execution
Logs must never include plaintext message bodies or full phone numbers.
22.4 SMS sending via Twilio
22.4.1 Outbound send flow (high level)
                                                                                                            1. Draft exists (AI-generated or user-selected template)
                                                                                                            2. User reviews and edits draft
                                                                                                            3. User taps Send
                                                                                                            4. Backend validates approval and creates an approved action record
                                                                                                            5. Backend enqueues a send job with an idempotency key
                                                                                                            6. Worker sends SMS via Twilio
                                                                                                            7. Worker stores provider message SID and marks sent
                                                                                                            8. Status callbacks update delivered or failed if enabled
                                                                                                            9. Call record UI shows final state and keeps sent content accessible under app privacy controls
22.4.2 Server-side send contract
The backend must be the only component allowed to call Twilio APIs. The mobile app never sends SMS directly.
Reason
                                                                                                            * Ensures approval-first enforcement is not bypassed
                                                                                                            * Centralizes rate limiting, auditing, retries, and idempotency
                                                                                                            * Keeps Twilio credentials off the device
22.4.3 Idempotency to prevent duplicate billing
SMS send must be idempotent because mobile networks and background workers can retry.
Idempotency rules
                                                                                                            * Each send attempt uses an idempotency_key generated at approval time.
                                                                                                            * The worker checks if an attempt already exists with that idempotency_key and a provider_message_sid.
                                                                                                            * If yes, do not resend. Return the existing result.
The system must never send the same action twice due to a retry unless the user explicitly presses “Retry” after a confirmed failure.
22.4.4 Message length and content constraints
Phase 1 should keep outbound texts short, predictable, and low-cost.
                                                                                                            * Enforce a maximum character limit for the final message body.
                                                                                                            * If the user exceeds the limit, the client must block send and show a clear error.
                                                                                                            * If the user uses unicode characters, treat length conservatively, since segmentation behavior can differ.
Phase 1 default policy
                                                                                                            * Keep drafts under a short limit suitable for a single or limited number of segments.
                                                                                                            * Prefer clarity and brevity over long explanations.
22.4.5 Recipient number validation rules
Before approval and send:
                                                                                                            * Normalize to E.164 when possible
                                                                                                            * Reject empty or obviously invalid numbers
                                                                                                            * If the call record has two numbers (caller number and provided callback number), the UI must show both and allow selection, but backend must validate the chosen one
Failure behavior
                                                                                                            * If number cannot be normalized or is invalid, mark the action as failed with a clear reason and keep the draft for editing.
22.4.6 Failure classification
Worker failures are classified as:
Transient (retry in worker)
                                                                                                            * provider timeouts
                                                                                                            * temporary provider errors
                                                                                                            * internal network errors
                                                                                                            * rate-limit responses that suggest waiting
Permanent (do not auto retry, require user action)
                                                                                                            * invalid destination number
                                                                                                            * destination cannot receive SMS
                                                                                                            * account or number not authorized for SMS
                                                                                                            * blocked by opt-out state (see 22.6.3)
Transient failures can be retried automatically within a bounded limit. Permanent failures must surface to the user immediately with a “Fix and retry” path.
22.5 Approval-first enforcement
22.5.1 Enforcement principle
Texting must not occur without explicit user approval by default. This must be enforced server-side, not as a UI convention.
22.5.2 Approval transaction requirements
When the user presses Send, the backend performs an atomic approval transition:
Preconditions
                                                                                                            * action belongs to the authenticated user
                                                                                                            * action status is drafted or awaiting_approval
                                                                                                            * call is owned by the user
                                                                                                            * caller is not blocked
                                                                                                            * user policy requires approval (default true)
                                                                                                            * message body passes validation rules
Atomic update
                                                                                                            * set status = approved
                                                                                                            * set approved_at = now
                                                                                                            * set approved_by_device_id = device_id
                                                                                                            * store final_body_ciphertext (the exact message approved)
                                                                                                            * create send attempt with idempotency_key
                                                                                                            * enqueue send job
If any part fails, the action must remain in a safe state where no SMS is sent.
22.5.3 Preventing bypass through API misuse
The backend must not expose an endpoint that sends a message without an approval step. Even if an API merges “approve and send” into one call, the server must still write approval state first, then enqueue send.
22.5.4 Limited exceptions policy
Phase 1 default is strict approval-first. If exceptions are supported at all, they must be limited and explicitly configured, such as:
                                                                                                            * Auto-allow for VIP callers only
If Phase 1 includes VIP auto-allow, it must still store the sent message and show it in the call record, and the user must be able to disable the exception at any time. If Phase 1 does not include exceptions, store the policy fields anyway so it can be added later without redesign.
22.6 Templates and drafts
22.6.1 Template library model
Phase 1 includes a built-in template library intended to reduce unpredictability and improve trust.
Template object
                                                                                                            * template_id
                                                                                                            * category (busy, callback_request, request_details, reschedule, wrong_number, sales_decline, etc.)
                                                                                                            * title (short)
                                                                                                            * body (parameterized)
                                                                                                            * tone_tag (neutral, warm, formal, concise)
                                                                                                            * enabled_by_default (true for built-ins)
Templates are global defaults. Optional per-user templates can be supported later. If Phase 1 supports user-saved templates, they must be stored per user and sync across devices.
22.6.2 Template parameterization
Templates may include placeholders populated from call extraction:
                                                                                                            * caller_name
                                                                                                            * user_display_name or assistant_name
                                                                                                            * callback_window
                                                                                                            * main_reason short phrase
Rules
                                                                                                            * If a placeholder value is missing, the system must remove it cleanly instead of inventing a value.
                                                                                                            * No template should insert sensitive data by default.
22.6.3 AI draft generation rules
AI drafts are generated after every call as a suggestion. They are not sent automatically.
Draft input sources
                                                                                                            * Call summary
                                                                                                            * Extracted fields (reason, urgency, callback number presence, best time window)
                                                                                                            * Label and label reason (only to choose tone, not to include sensitive content)
Draft constraints
                                                                                                            * Must not claim actions are already approved
                                                                                                            * Must not promise a specific callback time unless the user explicitly set a policy that allows it
                                                                                                            * Must not include sensitive user information
                                                                                                            * Must not include links or attachments in Phase 1
                                                                                                            * Must be short and editable
Draft selection logic
                                                                                                            * Choose an initial template category based on extracted intent:
                                                                                                            * callback request if caller wants follow-up
                                                                                                            * request details if reason unclear
                                                                                                            * busy acknowledgment default if reason exists but callback details missing
                                                                                                            * sales decline if labeled sales and user prefers to decline by text
                                                                                                            * Fill placeholders from extracted fields when present
                                                                                                            * Provide the result as draft_body
22.6.4 Draft editing and finalization
The user can edit the draft before approval.
The system must preserve:
                                                                                                            * original draft (for reference)
                                                                                                            * final approved message body
This supports user trust and debugging without leaking content in logs.
22.7 APIs and backend workflows
22.7.1 Template retrieval
GET /templates/text-back
                                                                                                            * Returns the built-in template list and any user templates if enabled
                                                                                                            * Supports filtering by category and tone_tag
22.7.2 Create or retrieve a draft for a call
POST /calls/{call_id}/text-back/draft
Creates an action in drafted state if one does not already exist, otherwise returns the existing draft action.
Rules
                                                                                                            * One active draft action per call by default
                                                                                                            * If user wants multiple texts, Phase 1 can allow creating another draft after the first is sent, but keep it explicit.
Response includes
                                                                                                            * action_id
                                                                                                            * recipient options (caller number, callback number if different)
                                                                                                            * draft_body
                                                                                                            * template suggestions metadata
                                                                                                            * current status
22.7.3 Update draft body
PATCH /actions/{action_id}/text-back
                                                                                                            * Allows updating recipient selection and draft body
                                                                                                            * Validates length and content rules
                                                                                                            * Stores updated draft_body_ciphertext
22.7.4 Approve and send
POST /actions/{action_id}/approve
Body
                                                                                                            * final_body (plaintext over TLS, stored encrypted)
                                                                                                            * to_number selection or explicit to_number
                                                                                                            * idempotency_key
                                                                                                            * device_id (derived from session)
Response
                                                                                                            * status: sending
                                                                                                            * attempt_id
                                                                                                            * server timestamps
22.7.5 Retry send after failure
POST /actions/{action_id}/retry
Allowed only if current status is failed.
Rules
                                                                                                            * Requires a new idempotency_key
                                                                                                            * Must not send if a prior attempt is still pending or if the action is already sent
22.7.6 Cancel draft
POST /actions/{action_id}/cancel
                                                                                                            * Allowed only if status is drafted or awaiting_approval
                                                                                                            * Does not delete the action record immediately, it marks cancelled so history is consistent
                                                                                                            * User can create a new draft afterward
22.8 Delivery status updates and synchronization
22.8.1 Status callback handling
If Twilio status callbacks are enabled, the backend receives provider updates and maps them to our internal status.
Update rules
                                                                                                            * If provider indicates accepted or queued: keep action in sent state if already marked sent, otherwise transition from sending to sent
                                                                                                            * If provider indicates delivered: transition to delivered
                                                                                                            * If provider indicates failed or undelivered: transition to failed and store provider error code and short message
