22.8.2 UI synchronization across devices
Message action updates must sync across devices.
Mechanisms
                                                                                                            * Realtime event feed if available
                                                                                                            * Otherwise, client polls call detail and action endpoints when it is open
Minimum requirement
                                                                                                            * When one device sends a text, all devices see status and final message content in the call record.
22.9 Logging and audit for outbound messages
22.9.1 Data minimization rules
Never log
                                                                                                            * message body plaintext
                                                                                                            * full recipient phone number
                                                                                                            * full sender phone number
Allowed logs (examples)
                                                                                                            * action_id, call_id, owner_user_id
                                                                                                            * message length
                                                                                                            * recipient_hash and last4
                                                                                                            * status transitions
                                                                                                            * provider_message_sid
                                                                                                            * provider error code
                                                                                                            * attempt_number
                                                                                                            * timestamps
22.9.2 Audit events required
Audit events for outbound messages must be written for:
                                                                                                            * text_draft_created
                                                                                                            * text_draft_edited
                                                                                                            * text_approved
                                                                                                            * text_send_started
                                                                                                            * text_send_succeeded
                                                                                                            * text_send_failed
                                                                                                            * text_retry_requested
                                                                                                            * text_cancelled
Audit event payload must be metadata-only, not the message body. If it must reference content, store only content hash and length.
22.9.3 Access controls for viewing message content
Message content is visible only inside the app and only to the authenticated user who owns the call record.
If biometric protection is enabled
                                                                                                            * Viewing message content follows the same gating as transcript and summary viewing
                                                                                                            * Sending does not require biometric gating, but the content preview screen should respect the app’s privacy rules
22.10 Edge cases and failure behavior
22.10.1 Caller cannot receive SMS
If Twilio returns a destination not reachable by SMS or similar permanent error:
                                                                                                            * Mark action failed
                                                                                                            * Show “Text not supported for this number”
                                                                                                            * Keep draft saved
                                                                                                            * Offer alternative: call back action (no automatic fallback send)
22.10.2 Multiple possible recipient numbers
If caller number differs from a provided callback number:
                                                                                                            * UI shows both with labels
                                                                                                            * Default selection uses the explicitly provided callback number when present
                                                                                                            * Backend validates chosen number and stores it
22.10.3 Duplicate taps and flaky networks
If the user taps Send multiple times:
                                                                                                            * Client should disable the Send button immediately after press
                                                                                                            * Server idempotency_key prevents duplicate sends
                                                                                                            * If the server receives multiple approvals for the same action, it returns the current state instead of creating another attempt
22.10.4 Retention and deletion behavior
Outbound message content is part of call data and must follow retention and deletion rules.
When a call record is deleted
                                                                                                            * delete all linked message actions and send attempts
                                                                                                            * delete encrypted message bodies
                                                                                                            * keep minimal audit metadata if required by system audit policy, without content
22.10.5 Rate limiting and abuse controls
Even though texting is user-initiated, the backend must enforce:
                                                                                                            * per-user daily send caps (Phase 1 conservative defaults)
                                                                                                            * per-call send caps (prevents spamming the same caller repeatedly)
                                                                                                            * burst limiting to prevent accidental rapid sends from UI issues
When limits trigger
                                                                                                            * block send
                                                                                                            * show a clear error
                                                                                                            * log an audit event: text_send_rate_limited
22.11 Completion checklist for this chapter
This chapter is complete when:
                                                                                                            * Outbound SMS uses Twilio and is executed only by backend workers
                                                                                                            * Approval-first is enforced server-side and cannot be bypassed
                                                                                                            * Each call record can generate a draft text and show it for editing
                                                                                                            * A template library exists and templates can be selected and edited before sending
                                                                                                            * Message actions have clear statuses and sync across devices
                                                                                                            * Sending is idempotent and retries do not duplicate messages
                                                                                                            * Delivery status is stored and visible, with failure reasons and retry support
                                                                                                            * Logs never contain message bodies or full phone numbers
                                                                                                            * Audit events exist for draft, approval, send, failure, and retry
                                                                                                            * Message content follows retention and deletion rules with encrypted storage
________________


CHAPTER 23: NOTIFICATIONS AND REMINDER SYSTEM
23.0 Purpose and scope
This chapter defines the Phase 1 notification system and in-app reminder engine, including:
                                                                                                            * Push delivery architecture for iOS and Android
                                                                                                            * Notification privacy defaults and preview rules
                                                                                                            * Quiet hours behavior and bypass rules
                                                                                                            * Reliability requirements including retries and delivery tracking
                                                                                                            * Reminder creation, scheduling, triggering, deduplication, and delivery
                                                                                                            * Multi-device behavior and per-device notification controls
Phase 1 constraints:
                                                                                                            * Push notifications must work across iOS and Android
                                                                                                            * Notifications are delivered to all signed-in devices by default
                                                                                                            * Notification privacy is private by default (lock screen safe)
                                                                                                            * Quiet hours are required in Phase 1
                                                                                                            * Reminders are in-app only in Phase 1 (no calendar integration)
                                                                                                            * Notification delivery must have retry and delivery tracking, not best-effort only
23.1 Push architecture
23.1.1 Provider selection and routing
Phase 1 uses:
                                                                                                            * Android: Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM)
                                                                                                            * iOS: Apple Push Notification service (APNS)
A single internal Notification Service abstracts provider differences and exposes a consistent backend API:
                                                                                                            * enqueue_notification(event)
                                                                                                            * send_to_device(notification_id, device_id)
                                                                                                            * record_provider_result(notification_delivery_id, result)
                                                                                                            * reconcile_invalid_tokens(device_id, provider_feedback)
The Notification Service runs in the backend and uses background workers for delivery, so call processing is not blocked by push sending.
23.1.2 Device registration and token lifecycle
Each device must have a server-side record. A device is any mobile device signed into the app and eligible to receive notifications.
Device record fields required for notification delivery:
                                                                                                            * device_id
                                                                                                            * owner_user_id
                                                                                                            * platform: ios or android
                                                                                                            * push_provider: apns or fcm
                                                                                                            * push_token (encrypted at rest)
                                                                                                            * push_token_hash (for lookup and dedupe)
                                                                                                            * notifications_enabled (per device)
                                                                                                            * last_token_update_at
                                                                                                            * last_push_success_at
                                                                                                            * last_push_failure_at
                                                                                                            * push_health_status: unknown, healthy, failing, disabled
                                                                                                            * app_version, os_version (for debugging and rollout control)
Token registration flow:
                                                                                                            1. App requests OS notification permission
                                                                                                            2. If granted, app obtains a push token
                                                                                                            3. App calls backend: POST /devices/register_push
                                                                                                            * device_id
                                                                                                            * platform
                                                                                                            * push_token
                                                                                                            * notifications_enabled true
                                                                                                            * timezone hint if available (for quiet hours accuracy)
                                                                                                            4. Backend stores token encrypted, updates hashes, sets push_health_status to unknown
                                                                                                            5. Backend optionally triggers a test push (onboarding step)
If permission is denied:
                                                                                                            * App still registers the device without a token
                                                                                                            * notifications_enabled remains false or push_health_status is disabled
                                                                                                            * Backend does not attempt push sends to that device
Token refresh behavior:
                                                                                                            * App must call /devices/register_push whenever the token changes
                                                                                                            * Backend overwrites token safely and invalidates pending deliveries that referenced the old token if needed
23.1.3 Per-device notification preferences
Phase 1 supports per-device notification enabled state:
                                                                                                            * notifications_enabled true means the device is eligible to receive pushes
                                                                                                            * notifications_enabled false means the backend must not send push notifications to that device
This is separate from OS-level permission. If OS permission is off, the device will have no token and effectively cannot receive pushes even if notifications_enabled is true. The UI should reflect both.
23.1.4 Notification service data model
Phase 1 requires explicit tracking records for auditability and reliability.
A) notifications (one row per logical notification event)
                                                                                                            * notification_id
                                                                                                            * owner_user_id
                                                                                                            * type (see 23.2.1)
                                                                                                            * priority (low, normal, high, critical)
                                                                                                            * created_at
                                                                                                            * source_entity_type (call, handoff_offer, reminder, system_error)
                                                                                                            * source_entity_id
                                                                                                            * privacy_mode_applied (private or preview)
                                                                                                            * quiet_hours_applied (none, silent, suppressed)
                                                                                                            * payload_version
                                                                                                            * content_hash (hash of the rendered payload, not the full content)
B) notification_deliveries (one row per device delivery attempt set)
                                                                                                            * notification_delivery_id
                                                                                                            * notification_id
                                                                                                            * device_id
                                                                                                            * provider (apns or fcm)
                                                                                                            * status (queued, sending, provider_accepted, provider_rejected, delivered_ack, expired, cancelled)
                                                                                                            * attempt_count
                                                                                                            * next_attempt_at
                                                                                                            * last_attempt_at
                                                                                                            * provider_message_id (if available)
                                                                                                            * last_error_code
                                                                                                            * last_error_message_short
C) notification_receipts (optional but recommended for delivery tracking)
                                                                                                            * receipt_id
                                                                                                            * notification_id
                                                                                                            * device_id
                                                                                                            * received_at (device reported)
                                                                                                            * opened_at (device reported, optional)
                                                                                                            * receipt_method (push_received, user_opened)
Key rule:
Provider acceptance is not the same as delivery to the device. Phase 1 tracks both provider results and device receipts when available.
23.1.5 Provider payload types and message categories
Two payload categories are required:
A) Alert notification
                                                                                                            * Displays a visible notification to the user
                                                                                                            * Used for: important call, handoff available, reminder due (depending on quiet hours rules)
B) Silent data notification
                                                                                                            * No visible alert
                                                                                                            * Used to: update badges, sync new call record availability, and support quiet hours suppression while still keeping the app state accurate
                                                                                                            * Silent notifications must never include sensitive content
23.1.6 Notification send pipeline
End-to-end pipeline:
                                                                                                            1. Product event occurs (call completed, call marked important, handoff offer created, reminder due, processing error)
                                                                                                            2. Backend creates notifications row
                                                                                                            3. Backend expands to deliveries for all eligible devices
                                                                                                            * devices where notifications_enabled true and token exists
                                                                                                            4. Backend enqueues delivery jobs to SQS
                                                                                                            5. Worker sends to APNS or FCM
                                                                                                            6. Worker records provider response in notification_deliveries
                                                                                                            7. If app supports receipts, app posts receipt back to backend
                                                                                                            8. Backend updates device push health status and delivery metrics
23.1.7 Token invalidation and device push health
When provider indicates an invalid token:
                                                                                                            * Mark device push_health_status as disabled
                                                                                                            * Clear stored token fields (token ciphertext, token hash)
                                                                                                            * Set notifications_enabled to false only if the product wants to treat this as a user-visible disable. Otherwise keep notifications_enabled true but token empty, and show “notifications not configured” state.
Common invalidation behaviors:
                                                                                                            * iOS: token invalid or unregistered
                                                                                                            * Android: not registered, invalid registration, mismatch sender
All invalidation events must be recorded in audit logs and in device status fields so the device list can show notification status clearly.
23.1.8 Onboarding test push
Phase 1 onboarding includes a test push so the user can confirm notifications work.
Backend endpoint: POST /notifications/test
Behavior:
                                                                                                            * Create a test notification
                                                                                                            * Deliver to the requesting device only (default)
                                                                                                            * Optionally deliver to all devices if the user is already multi-device
Success criteria:
                                                                                                            * Provider accepted result recorded
                                                                                                            * If receipts are implemented, a receipt arrives within a short window
                                                                                                            * App shows a clear pass or fail and basic troubleshooting guidance
23.2 Notification privacy mode
23.2.1 Notification types
Phase 1 supports at least these notification types:
                                                                                                            * call_screened_created
                                                                                                            * call_important_detected
                                                                                                            * handoff_available
                                                                                                            * call_processing_error
                                                                                                            * reminder_due
Each type has:
                                                                                                            * default priority
                                                                                                            * default privacy rendering
                                                                                                            * quiet hours policy
                                                                                                            * required deep link target (internal app route)
23.2.2 Privacy modes
Users choose one of these privacy modes:
A) Private mode (default)
                                                                                                            * Lock screen and notification body show no caller identity and no message content
                                                                                                            * Safe default content examples:
                                                                                                            * “New call screened”
                                                                                                            * “Important call detected”
                                                                                                            * “Take over call”
                                                                                                            * “Reminder”
B) Preview mode
                                                                                                            * Notification may include limited preview content:
                                                                                                            * Caller display name if provided, otherwise masked number
                                                                                                            * One line reason snippet (strictly length-bounded)
                                                                                                            * Urgency badge (optional)
                                                                                                            * No transcript content in notifications, ever
Privacy mode is enforced server-side during payload rendering. The client cannot request a less private payload than the user setting.
23.2.3 Content safety rules
These rules apply to all notifications regardless of privacy mode:
                                                                                                            * Never include full transcript text
                                                                                                            * Never include full phone numbers
                                                                                                            * Never include recording links
                                                                                                            * Never include OTP codes or other sensitive secrets in call notifications
                                                                                                            * Reason snippets must be short, bounded, and derived from structured extraction, not raw transcript
Masking rules:
                                                                                                            * Phone numbers displayed as last 2 to 4 digits only
                                                                                                            * Caller display name trimmed to a safe maximum length
                                                                                                            * If caller name appears suspicious or overly long, fall back to “Unknown caller”
23.2.4 Deep link rules
Each notification must include a deep link target that opens the app to a safe screen:
                                                                                                            * call_screened_created and call_important_detected link to call detail, but the detail view must still enforce biometric gating for transcript and sensitive fields
                                                                                                            * handoff_available links to the handoff UI for the live call
                                                                                                            * call_processing_error links to the call detail with a visible “processing incomplete” banner
                                                                                                            * reminder_due links to the call detail and highlights the reminder item
Deep links must use internal identifiers only (call_id, reminder_id). Never embed sensitive content in the link.
23.2.5 Multi-device privacy consistency
Privacy mode is per user account, not per device, in Phase 1. This ensures consistent behavior across all devices and reduces confusion.
If per-device privacy is added later, the backend must apply the more private option among user-level and device-level settings.
23.3 Quiet hours
23.3.1 Quiet hours model
Quiet hours define when alert notifications should be suppressed or converted to silent notifications.
Quiet hours are configured per user and use the user’s timezone. Phase 1 requires:
                                                                                                            * enabled flag
                                                                                                            * start time (local)
                                                                                                            * end time (local)
                                                                                                            * days of week selection
                                                                                                            * timezone identifier (auto-detected, user-editable if needed)
Quiet hours evaluation must handle intervals that cross midnight.
23.3.2 Quiet hours application modes
Quiet hours can apply in two ways:
                                                                                                            * Suppress: do not send an alert notification
                                                                                                            * Silent: send a silent data notification only (for badge updates and sync)
Phase 1 default:
                                                                                                            * Standard call notifications during quiet hours use Silent mode
                                                                                                            * This keeps the app state accurate without interrupting the user
23.3.3 Exceptions and bypass rules
Some notifications are time sensitive or explicitly user-scheduled. Phase 1 supports bypass rules by notification type:
A) handoff_available
                                                                                                            * Bypasses quiet hours by default
                                                                                                            * Reason: it is a 20 second window event and has no value if delayed
                                                                                                            * Still obeys privacy mode and uses private content by default
B) call_important_detected
                                                                                                            * Default behavior: bypass quiet hours only if the call is urgent or VIP
                                                                                                            * If not urgent or VIP, treat as standard and use Silent mode during quiet hours
C) reminder_due
                                                                                                            * Default behavior: bypass quiet hours because the user scheduled it
                                                                                                            * Optional setting: “Reminders respect quiet hours”
                                                                                                            * If enabled, reminders use Silent mode during quiet hours and alert at the end of quiet hours
D) call_processing_error
                                                                                                            * Default behavior: Silent during quiet hours
                                                                                                            * Error details are not urgent enough to wake the user by default
23.3.4 Deferred delivery option
If a notification is suppressed by quiet hours and is configured to deliver later, the system must schedule a deferred notification at the end of quiet hours.
Phase 1 keeps this limited:
                                                                                                            * Only reminders may use deferred delivery when the user chooses “Reminders respect quiet hours”
                                                                                                            * Other suppressed notifications are not deferred by default, because the call list and badge count are sufficient
Deferred notifications must deduplicate to avoid flooding:
                                                                                                            * At most one deferred reminder alert per reminder item
                                                                                                            * At most one “reminders pending” alert per quiet hours window if multiple reminders became due
23.3.5 Quiet hours and multi-device
Quiet hours apply to all devices under the user account. This prevents one device from alerting while another is silent.
If a device is in a different timezone, the system still uses the user account timezone in Phase 1 to keep behavior consistent. The app may display a note if a device timezone differs significantly.
23.4 Reliability
23.4.1 Reliability goals
Phase 1 push reliability must satisfy:
                                                                                                            * Notifications are generated for every eligible event
                                                                                                            * Each device delivery is attempted with retries on transient errors
                                                                                                            * Delivery outcomes are stored per device
                                                                                                            * Tokens are cleaned up automatically when invalid
                                                                                                            * Users can verify notifications during onboarding via a test push
                                                                                                            * The system produces metrics that show success rates and failure causes
23.4.2 Retry policy
Retry is managed at the notification_delivery level.
Transient failure examples:
                                                                                                            * provider timeout
                                                                                                            * network error
                                                                                                            * temporary provider unavailable
                                                                                                            * internal worker failure
Permanent failure examples:
                                                                                                            * invalid token
                                                                                                            * unregistered device token
                                                                                                            * unauthorized provider credentials (system incident)
Retry rules:
                                                                                                            * Max attempts per delivery: bounded (example: 5)
                                                                                                            * Exponential backoff with jitter
                                                                                                            * Stop retrying when:
                                                                                                            * delivery reaches provider_accepted, or
                                                                                                            * permanent failure is detected, or
                                                                                                            * notification is expired (handoff and other time sensitive notifications)
Expiration rules:
                                                                                                            * handoff_available deliveries expire at offer_expires_at and must not be retried after expiry
                                                                                                            * call notifications expire after a longer window (example: 24 hours) but do not need continuous retries beyond the max attempt count
                                                                                                            * reminder_due expires only when the reminder is marked completed or dismissed, but alert retry is still bounded
23.4.3 Delivery tracking and device receipts
Provider acceptance tracking:
                                                                                                            * Store provider response for each delivery attempt
                                                                                                            * Mark provider_accepted when APNS or FCM accepts the message
Device receipt tracking (recommended):
                                                                                                            * App posts receipt when it receives a notification
                                                                                                            * POST /notifications/{notification_id}/receipt
                                                                                                            * Includes device_id and received_at
                                                                                                            * App posts opened signal when user taps the notification
                                                                                                            * POST /notifications/{notification_id}/opened
                                                                                                            * Includes device_id and opened_at
Receipt tracking supports:
                                                                                                            * Showing “notification status” in the device list
                                                                                                            * Diagnosing silent failures where provider accepted but device never received
                                                                                                            * Measuring end-to-end notification reliability
Receipts are best-effort and must not block the user experience. If receipts are unavailable due to platform limitations, provider acceptance remains the primary metric.
23.4.4 Deduplication rules
Duplicates are common in distributed systems. Phase 1 deduplicates at two levels:
A) Notification event deduplication
A logical event should create at most one notification row:
                                                                                                            * call_screened_created: dedupe key = call_id + type
                                                                                                            * call_important_detected: dedupe key = call_id + type
                                                                                                            * handoff_available: dedupe key = offer_id + type
                                                                                                            * call_processing_error: dedupe key = call_id + error_code + type
                                                                                                            * reminder_due: dedupe key = reminder_id + due_at + type
B) Delivery deduplication
A notification should create at most one delivery row per device:
                                                                                                            * dedupe key = notification_id + device_id
Workers must be idempotent. If a delivery job runs twice, it must not create duplicate delivery rows or send duplicates beyond the retry policy.
23.4.5 Priority handling and queue separation
Phase 1 uses separate SQS queues or separate priority handling within one queue:
                                                                                                            * High priority: handoff_available, reminder_due
                                                                                                            * Normal priority: call_screened_created, call_important_detected
                                                                                                            * Low priority: call_processing_error, silent sync pushes
This prevents slow or high-volume events from delaying time sensitive ones.
23.4.6 Observability requirements
For notifications, Phase 1 must emit metrics and logs that exclude sensitive content:
Metrics per type:
                                                                                                            * notification_created_count
                                                                                                            * delivery_attempt_count
                                                                                                            * provider_accepted_rate
                                                                                                            * permanent_failure_rate
                                                                                                            * invalid_token_rate
                                                                                                            * receipt_rate (if receipts enabled)
                                                                                                            * time_to_provider_accept_ms
                                                                                                            * time_to_receipt_ms (if receipts enabled)
Logs must never include:
                                                                                                            * caller name
                                                                                                            * message content
                                                                                                            * transcript
                                                                                                            * full phone numbers
Logs may include:
                                                                                                            * notification_id
                                                                                                            * device_id
                                                                                                            * type
                                                                                                            * provider error codes
                                                                                                            * attempt counts
                                                                                                            * timestamps
23.5 Reminder engine
23.5.1 Reminder requirements
Reminders allow the user to schedule follow-up from a call record.
Phase 1 reminder properties:
                                                                                                            * Title or reason
                                                                                                            * Date and time
                                                                                                            * Link back to the call record
                                                                                                            * In-app only (no calendar integration)
                                                                                                            * Sync across all user devices
                                                                                                            * Must trigger a notification at the scheduled time
23.5.2 Reminder data model
A) reminders
                                                                                                            * reminder_id
                                                                                                            * owner_user_id
                                                                                                            * call_id (required)
                                                                                                            * title (bounded length)
                                                                                                            * due_at (UTC)
                                                                                                            * timezone_at_creation (for display)
                                                                                                            * status: scheduled, triggered, completed, cancelled
                                                                                                            * created_at, updated_at
                                                                                                            * created_by_device_id
                                                                                                            * completed_at (nullable)
B) reminder_triggers (optional but recommended for reliability)
                                                                                                            * reminder_trigger_id
                                                                                                            * reminder_id
                                                                                                            * scheduled_due_at
                                                                                                            * triggered_at
                                                                                                            * trigger_status: pending, enqueued, sent, failed
                                                                                                            * dedupe_key = reminder_id + scheduled_due_at
This separation makes scheduling and deduplication easier when reminders are rescheduled.
23.5.3 Reminder creation and editing flows
Create reminder:
                                                                                                            * POST /calls/{call_id}/reminders
                                                                                                            * title
                                                                                                            * due_at_local + timezone, or due_at_utc
Backend behavior:
                                                                                                               * Validate due_at is in the future
                                                                                                               * Convert to UTC for storage
                                                                                                               * Create reminder with status scheduled
                                                                                                               * Create reminder_trigger with pending state
                                                                                                               * Return reminder_id and normalized due_at
Edit reminder:
                                                                                                               * PATCH /reminders/{reminder_id}
                                                                                                               * title and or due_at
Rules:
                                                                                                                  * Only scheduled reminders can be edited
                                                                                                                  * Editing due_at creates a new reminder_trigger and cancels the old pending trigger
                                                                                                                  * If the reminder is already triggered, editing becomes a new reminder item (Phase 1 can block this to keep it simple)
Complete reminder:
                                                                                                                  * POST /reminders/{reminder_id}/complete
Rules:
                                                                                                                  * Marks status completed
                                                                                                                  * Cancels any pending triggers
                                                                                                                  * Updates UI across devices
Cancel reminder:
                                                                                                                     * POST /reminders/{reminder_id}/cancel
Rules:
                                                                                                                     * Marks status cancelled
                                                                                                                     * Cancels any pending triggers
23.5.4 Scheduling mechanism
SQS cannot schedule long delays reliably for reminders (delay limits are short). Phase 1 uses a database-driven scheduler.
Reminder scheduler job:
                                                                                                                        * Runs on a fixed interval (example: every 30 to 60 seconds)
                                                                                                                        * Queries for reminder_triggers where:
                                                                                                                        * trigger_status = pending
                                                                                                                        * scheduled_due_at <= now
                                                                                                                        * Marks them as enqueued in a transaction to prevent double-enqueue
                                                                                                                        * Enqueues a reminder_due job to SQS with reminder_id and trigger_id
This design supports:
                                                                                                                        * Deduplication with transactional state transitions
                                                                                                                        * Recovery after downtime, because pending triggers remain in the database
                                                                                                                        * Horizontal scaling by ensuring only one worker claims a trigger row at a time
23.5.5 Reminder due processing
Reminder due worker steps:
                                                                                                                        1. Load reminder and trigger by IDs
                                                                                                                        2. Verify reminder status is still scheduled
                                                                                                                        3. Create a reminder_due notification event (type reminder_due)
                                                                                                                        4. Apply privacy mode and quiet hours behavior:
                                                                                                                        * Title and body should not include sensitive call details in private mode
                                                                                                                        * If preview mode is enabled, include only bounded title and a safe call reference
                                                                                                                        5. Deliver to all eligible devices using the standard notification pipeline
                                                                                                                        6. Mark reminder as triggered if the system wants one-time semantics
                                                                                                                        * Phase 1 one-time reminders: set reminder.status to triggered after successful provider acceptance, or always after enqueue depending on policy
                                                                                                                        7. Update reminder_trigger state to sent or failed
23.5.6 Reminder deduplication and exactly-once behavior
Exact once delivery is approximated through idempotency:
                                                                                                                        * reminder_trigger row claim is atomic
                                                                                                                        * reminder_due worker is idempotent using dedupe key (reminder_id + due_at)
                                                                                                                        * notification creation uses the reminder_due dedupe key so duplicate worker runs do not create duplicate notifications
                                                                                                                        * delivery pipeline prevents duplicate deliveries per device for the same notification_id
If the worker crashes after sending to some devices:
                                                                                                                        * Remaining device deliveries can still be retried because delivery rows exist per device and the retry policy can re-attempt only the failed ones
23.5.7 Reminder presentation in the app
Call detail view must show reminders linked to that call:
                                                                                                                        * Upcoming reminder time
                                                                                                                        * Title
                                                                                                                        * Status (scheduled, triggered, completed, cancelled)
                                                                                                                        * Actions: edit, complete, cancel
Reminder list view (optional in Phase 1 but recommended if reminders exist):
                                                                                                                        * All upcoming reminders
                                                                                                                        * Filter by day and status
                                                                                                                        * Link back to call record
23.5.8 Reminder notifications and privacy
Private mode default:
                                                                                                                        * Notification shows “Reminder” and does not reveal caller identity or reason
Preview mode:
                                                                                                                        * May show reminder title (bounded) and a safe call reference
                                                                                                                        * Must still avoid caller number exposure beyond masked digits
23.5.9 Failure handling
If reminder notification delivery fails:
                                                                                                                           * reminder_trigger.trigger_status becomes failed
                                                                                                                           * System retries delivery using notification delivery retries
                                                                                                                           * If all deliveries fail due to invalid tokens, the reminder remains in triggered state but the user will not be alerted. The app will still show reminder status when opened.
If the scheduler is down:
                                                                                                                           * Pending triggers remain in the database
                                                                                                                           * When scheduler resumes, it will enqueue overdue triggers immediately
23.5.10 Completion checklist for this chapter
This chapter is complete when:
                                                                                                                           * iOS uses APNS and Android uses FCM through a unified Notification Service
                                                                                                                           * Devices can register and refresh push tokens, and token invalidation is handled automatically
                                                                                                                           * Notifications are sent to all eligible devices by default, with per-device disable supported
                                                                                                                           * Notification privacy is private by default and enforced server-side
                                                                                                                           * Quiet hours suppress or convert to silent notifications, with defined bypass rules for handoff and important calls
                                                                                                                           * Notification sending is reliable with retries, per-device delivery status, and deduplication
                                                                                                                           * A test push exists and can confirm notification setup during onboarding
                                                                                                                           * Reminders can be created, edited, completed, and cancelled, and are linked to call records
                                                                                                                           * Reminders are scheduled server-side using a database-driven scheduler and triggered reliably with deduplication
________________


CHAPTER 24: SETTINGS, PERSONALIZATION, AND CONFIGURATION
24.0 Purpose and scope
This chapter defines the Phase 1 settings system, including:
                                                                                                                           * The full catalog of user facing settings and what each one changes in product behavior
                                                                                                                           * How settings are stored, versioned, migrated, and synchronized across multiple devices
                                                                                                                           * How presets work (voice, temperament, call objective, call length, greeting templates)
                                                                                                                           * Which settings are enforced server side and cannot be bypassed by client behavior
                                                                                                                           * Validation, auditing, and safety rules for sensitive settings changes
Phase 1 requirement: settings must be simple for users, but technically structured and versioned so Phase B and Phase C can reuse the same framework without redesign.
24.1 Settings catalog
24.1.1 Settings organization (user experience groups)
Settings are organized into these groups in the app:
A) Account
                                                                                                                           * Profile basics
                                                                                                                           * Sign out
                                                                                                                           * Sign out all devices
                                                                                                                           * Delete account
B) Devices
                                                                                                                           * Signed in devices list
                                                                                                                           * Remove device (revoke session)
                                                                                                                           * Per device notifications toggle
C) Assistant personalization
                                                                                                                           * Assistant name
                                                                                                                           * Voice selection
                                                                                                                           * Temperament preset
                                                                                                                           * Swearing rule
                                                                                                                           * Accent preference (best effort)
D) Call handling
                                                                                                                           * Mode toggles (Dedicated AI number, Forwarding fallback)
                                                                                                                           * Dedicated number access control (everyone, contacts only, VIP only)
                                                                                                                           * Call objective (screen and summarize, message only)
                                                                                                                           * Max call length (general)
                                                                                                                           * VIP call length override
                                                                                                                           * Business hours and after hours behavior
                                                                                                                           * Live handoff enable and rule selection
E) Lists and rules
                                                                                                                           * VIP list management
                                                                                                                           * VIP handling preferences
                                                                                                                           * Block list management
                                                                                                                           * Spam control toggles and thresholds
F) Messaging follow up
                                                                                                                           * Text back enabled
                                                                                                                           * Approval policy (approval first enforced)
                                                                                                                           * Template preferences
G) Privacy and security
                                                                                                                           * Biometric unlock toggle
                                                                                                                           * Notification privacy mode (private, preview)
                                                                                                                           * Quiet hours schedule and rules
                                                                                                                           * Recording enabled toggle
                                                                                                                           * Data retention window
                                                                                                                           * Delete all call data
                                                                                                                           * Memory on off and deletion controls
24.1.2 Canonical settings keys and behaviors
Phase 1 stores settings as structured fields. The keys below are the canonical internal names used by backend and mobile apps.
24.1.2.1 Account and identity settings
assistant_name
                                                                                                                           * Type: string
                                                                                                                           * Default: a safe platform default (for example a neutral name)
                                                                                                                           * Constraints: short length, no profanity filter bypass, no impersonation phrasing
                                                                                                                           * Used in: call greeting and transcript header
                                                                                                                           * Server rule: must never imply it is the user
primary_language
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum
                                                                                                                           * Phase 1 value: English only (stored as English)
                                                                                                                           * Purpose: stored for future phases, but Phase 1 call handling remains English
secondary_language
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum or null
                                                                                                                           * Phase 1: stored only, not used
timezone
                                                                                                                           * Type: IANA timezone string
                                                                                                                           * Default: auto detected
                                                                                                                           * Used in: business hours, quiet hours, reminder display and scheduling logic
                                                                                                                           * Server rule: if invalid, fall back to auto detected
24.1.2.2 Device and session settings
Per device: notifications_enabled
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: true if OS permission granted and token exists, otherwise false
                                                                                                                           * Used in: push delivery targeting
                                                                                                                           * Server rule: no push sends to a device with notifications_enabled false or missing token
Sign out all devices
                                                                                                                           * Action, not a stored setting
                                                                                                                           * Behavior: revoke all device sessions except the current device if explicitly chosen, or revoke all including current device if user confirms
24.1.2.3 Assistant voice and temperament settings
voice_profile_id
                                                                                                                           * Type: provider voice ID
                                                                                                                           * Default: platform default voice
                                                                                                                           * Used in: real time voice selection
                                                                                                                           * Server rule: fallback hierarchy must apply if provider voice not available
voice_fallback_profile_id
                                                                                                                           * Type: provider voice ID
                                                                                                                           * Default: platform default fallback
                                                                                                                           * Used in: real time voice fallback
temperament_preset_id
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum or preset ID
                                                                                                                           * Default: professional_and_polite
                                                                                                                           * Used in: conversation style parameters and prompt pack selection
swearing_level
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum { none, mild, allow }
                                                                                                                           * Default: none
                                                                                                                           * Server rule: even if allow, hateful or abusive language is forbidden
accent_preference
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum or string, best effort
                                                                                                                           * Default: none
                                                                                                                           * Server rule: if not supported by selected voice, ignore without failing calls
response_length_preference
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum { short, balanced }
                                                                                                                           * Default: short
                                                                                                                           * Used in: turn length limits and summary style guidance
24.1.2.4 Call handling mode toggles
dedicated_ai_number_enabled
