                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: false until provisioned and activated
                                                                                                                           * Used in: inbound routing and number display in app
forwarding_fallback_enabled
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: true after onboarding if user completes forwarding setup, otherwise false
                                                                                                                           * Used in: inbound routing eligibility for forwarded calls
dedicated_number_access_policy
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum { everyone, contacts_only, vip_only }
                                                                                                                           * Default: everyone
                                                                                                                           * Used in: whether AI answers direct calls to the dedicated AI number
                                                                                                                           * Server rule: evaluated before answering, block list always overrides
24.1.2.5 Call objective and call length controls
call_objective_mode
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum { screen_and_summarize, message_only }
                                                                                                                           * Default: screen_and_summarize
                                                                                                                           * Behavior:
                                                                                                                           * screen_and_summarize asks a short sequence of screening questions
                                                                                                                           * message_only minimizes questions and captures a single message plus callback details when possible
max_call_length_seconds
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum derived value
                                                                                                                           * Allowed values: 120, 180, 300
                                                                                                                           * Default: 180
                                                                                                                           * Server enforcement:
                                                                                                                           * hard stop at limit with a polite close
                                                                                                                           * record outcome as ended_by_policy
vip_max_call_length_seconds
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum derived value
                                                                                                                           * Allowed values: 180, 300
                                                                                                                           * Default: 300
                                                                                                                           * Behavior: overrides max_call_length_seconds only when caller is VIP
greeting_template_id
                                                                                                                           * Type: preset ID
                                                                                                                           * Default: standard greeting
                                                                                                                           * Used in: opening line structure
                                                                                                                           * Server rule: greeting templates are controlled, not fully free text
24.1.2.6 Business hours and after hours behavior
business_hours_enabled
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: true after onboarding sets schedule, otherwise false
business_hours_schedule
                                                                                                                           * Type: structured schedule object
                                                                                                                           * Fields:
                                                                                                                           * days_of_week: set of 0 to 6
                                                                                                                           * start_time_local: HH:MM
                                                                                                                           * end_time_local: HH:MM
                                                                                                                           * timezone: IANA timezone string
                                                                                                                           * Validation: schedule must be valid, supports crossing midnight
after_hours_behavior
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum { screen_normally, message_only, screen_low_priority }
                                                                                                                           * Default: screen_low_priority
                                                                                                                           * Behavior:
                                                                                                                           * screen_normally: same screening flow as in hours
                                                                                                                           * message_only: switches to message taking mode
                                                                                                                           * screen_low_priority: screens normally but biases labels to low priority unless VIP or urgent
Server rule:
                                                                                                                           * Business hours never change whether routing happens. They change how the assistant behaves after answering.
24.1.2.7 Live handoff settings
live_handoff_enabled
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: false
live_handoff_rule
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum { vip_only, urgent_only, vip_and_urgent }
                                                                                                                           * Default: vip_only if enabled, otherwise not applicable
handoff_prompt_timing
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum
                                                                                                                           * Phase 1 value: after_minimum_screening
                                                                                                                           * Minimum screening definition:
                                                                                                                           * caller name or identifier captured
                                                                                                                           * one line reason captured
handoff_timeout_seconds
                                                                                                                           * Type: integer
                                                                                                                           * Default: 20
                                                                                                                           * Server enforcement: offers expire at timeout and are not retried
live_handoff_notification_privacy
                                                                                                                           * Derived from notification_privacy_mode
                                                                                                                           * Behavior:
                                                                                                                           * private mode uses generic lock screen prompt
                                                                                                                           * preview mode may include bounded caller display and one line reason snippet
24.1.2.8 VIP settings
vip_list_enabled
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: true
vip_calls_marked_important
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: true
                                                                                                                           * Behavior: VIP label contributes to important notification routing and UI badges
vip_notification_intensity
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum { normal, strong }
                                                                                                                           * Default: normal
                                                                                                                           * Behavior:
                                                                                                                           * normal follows standard notification rules
                                                                                                                           * strong increases prominence but still respects privacy mode
vip_live_handoff_override
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum or null
                                                                                                                           * Default: null
                                                                                                                           * Behavior: optional override for VIP handling if product chooses to expose it, otherwise derived from live_handoff_rule
24.1.2.9 Block list settings
block_list_enabled
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: true
blocked_caller_behavior
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum { end_immediately, short_neutral_message }
                                                                                                                           * Default: short_neutral_message
                                                                                                                           * Behavior:
                                                                                                                           * end_immediately: hang up with no conversation
                                                                                                                           * short_neutral_message: brief refusal then end
log_blocked_attempts
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: true
                                                                                                                           * Behavior: creates a call record stub for blocked attempts without transcript content
notify_on_blocked_attempts
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: false
                                                                                                                           * Behavior: sends a private notification that a blocked attempt occurred
24.1.2.10 Spam control settings
spam_labeling_enabled
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: true
block_suggestions_enabled
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: true
repeat_caller_threshold
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum { 2, 3, 5 }
                                                                                                                           * Default: 3
                                                                                                                           * Meaning: number of calls within a short system defined window that increases spam suspicion
Server rule:
                                                                                                                           * Spam labeling must be conservative. If confidence is not high, prefer normal or unknown.
24.1.2.11 Messaging follow up settings
text_back_enabled
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: true
                                                                                                                           * Behavior: enables the Text back action on call records when a valid destination exists
text_approval_policy
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum
                                                                                                                           * Phase 1 value: approval_first_only
                                                                                                                           * Server enforcement: sending is blocked unless explicitly approved
vip_auto_allow_texts
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: false
                                                                                                                           * Phase 1: stored for future, not active unless explicitly enabled later
default_text_template_tone
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum aligned to temperament presets
                                                                                                                           * Default: derived from temperament_preset_id
24.1.2.12 Notification and quiet hours settings
notification_privacy_mode
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum { private, preview }
                                                                                                                           * Default: private
                                                                                                                           * Server enforcement:
                                                                                                                           * private mode shows no caller identity or message content on lock screen
                                                                                                                           * preview mode still never includes transcripts and never includes full phone numbers
quiet_hours_enabled
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: true after onboarding sets schedule, otherwise false
quiet_hours_schedule
                                                                                                                           * Type: schedule object (same shape as business hours)
                                                                                                                           * Fields:
                                                                                                                           * days_of_week
                                                                                                                           * start_time_local
                                                                                                                           * end_time_local
                                                                                                                           * timezone
quiet_hours_behavior
                                                                                                                           * Type: fixed rule set in Phase 1
                                                                                                                           * Behavior:
                                                                                                                           * silent for normal calls
                                                                                                                           * alert for important calls only
                                                                                                                           * handoff prompts bypass quiet hours by default
reminders_respect_quiet_hours
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: false
                                                                                                                           * Behavior:
                                                                                                                           * if true, reminder alerts are deferred or silent during quiet hours
                                                                                                                           * if false, reminders alert at due time
24.1.2.13 Recording, retention, and memory settings
recording_enabled
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: false
                                                                                                                           * Server enforcement:
                                                                                                                           * if enabled, recording disclosure must be spoken at call start
                                                                                                                           * playback requires biometric unlock when enabled
biometric_unlock_enabled
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: true
                                                                                                                           * Behavior:
                                                                                                                           * gates viewing transcripts, summaries, label reasons, recordings, and message content inside app
data_retention_days
                                                                                                                           * Type: enum { 30, 90, 180 }
                                                                                                                           * Default: 90
                                                                                                                           * Behavior: controls automated deletion windows for call artifacts and related objects
delete_all_call_data
                                                                                                                           * Action, not a stored setting
                                                                                                                           * Behavior: deletes all call records and derived artifacts under the account, respects audit requirements
memory_enabled
                                                                                                                           * Type: boolean
                                                                                                                           * Default: true
                                                                                                                           * Behavior: allows creation of memory items from calls within a strict allowlist
memory_delete_all
                                                                                                                           * Action
                                                                                                                           * Behavior: deletes all stored memory items for the user
24.1.3 Settings change propagation rules
                                                                                                                           * Settings are account wide. Any change applies to all devices immediately after server acceptance.
                                                                                                                           * Devices must sync changes quickly:
                                                                                                                           * The updating device receives the new settings revision in the response
                                                                                                                           * Other devices refresh via silent sync notification when possible, otherwise next app open and periodic refresh
24.1.4 Settings snapshot rules for reproducibility
Because settings can change over time, each call record stores a settings snapshot captured at call start:
                                                                                                                           * settings_schema_version
                                                                                                                           * settings_revision
                                                                                                                           * effective values used for that call:
                                                                                                                           * voice_profile_id used
                                                                                                                           * temperament_preset_id
                                                                                                                           * call_objective_mode
                                                                                                                           * max_call_length_seconds and vip override if applicable
                                                                                                                           * business hours evaluation result and after hours behavior
                                                                                                                           * live_handoff_enabled and rule
                                                                                                                           * notification privacy mode applied for events
Snapshots prevent confusing situations where a user changes settings later and historical call behavior becomes hard to explain.
24.2 Versioned settings storage
24.2.1 Design goals
                                                                                                                           * Structured and versioned
                                                                                                                           * Backward compatible with older app versions
                                                                                                                           * Safe defaults when fields are missing
                                                                                                                           * Auditable for sensitive changes
                                                                                                                           * Efficient to read during call handling and notification decisions
24.2.2 Storage model overview
Phase 1 uses a hybrid model:
A) user_settings (single row per user)
                                                                                                                           * Stores all scalar and nested settings fields
                                                                                                                           * Stores schema_version and revision for concurrency control
B) List tables for high change items
                                                                                                                           * vip_list entries
                                                                                                                           * block_list entries
These are stored as separate tables to support efficient matching and management without rewriting the entire settings document.
C) device table for per device preferences
                                                                                                                              * Stores notifications_enabled and push token state
24.2.3 user_settings schema requirements
user_settings fields:
                                                                                                                              * user_id (primary key)
                                                                                                                              * schema_version (integer)
                                                                                                                              * revision (monotonic integer, increments on every update)
                                                                                                                              * settings_json (structured object, validated)
                                                                                                                              * created_at
                                                                                                                              * updated_at
                                                                                                                              * last_migrated_at
Validation rules:
                                                                                                                              * Server validates the full settings_json against the schema for the stored schema_version
                                                                                                                              * Unknown fields are allowed only if they are explicitly within a forward compatible extension object, otherwise rejected to avoid unbounded growth
24.2.4 Optimistic concurrency control
To prevent overwriting settings changes from another device:
                                                                                                                              * All update requests include expected_revision
                                                                                                                              * Server updates only if expected_revision matches current revision
                                                                                                                              * If mismatch, server returns conflict and the latest settings so the device can merge and retry
24.2.5 Partial update behavior
The API supports patch semantics:
                                                                                                                              * Client sends only the fields being changed
                                                                                                                              * Server merges changes into the existing settings_json
                                                                                                                              * Server revalidates the full settings_json
                                                                                                                              * Server increments revision and returns the full updated settings document
24.2.6 Default resolution and effective settings computation
Effective settings are computed as:
                                                                                                                              1. System defaults (hardcoded safe defaults)
                                                                                                                              2. User settings overrides (user_settings)
                                                                                                                              3. Per device overrides where applicable (notifications_enabled)
                                                                                                                              4. Server enforced constraints that cap or override unsafe values
Example:
                                                                                                                              * Even if a client attempts to set text_approval_policy to bypass approval, server clamps it to approval_first_only in Phase 1.
24.2.7 Settings migrations
When new settings are added in later releases:
                                                                                                                              * Server increments schema_version
                                                                                                                              * On read, server can migrate older settings_json to the latest schema by applying deterministic defaults and transformations
                                                                                                                              * Migration must be idempotent
                                                                                                                              * Migration can be lazy:
                                                                                                                              * migrate on first read after deployment
                                                                                                                              * persist migrated settings_json back to user_settings to avoid repeating work
Compatibility requirement:
                                                                                                                              * Older app versions must still function. The server must:
                                                                                                                              * return only fields the older app understands if needed, or
                                                                                                                              * allow older apps to ignore unknown fields safely
24.2.8 Auditing of settings changes
Sensitive settings changes must be audit logged with metadata:
Audit required for:
                                                                                                                              * notification_privacy_mode changes
                                                                                                                              * biometric_unlock_enabled changes
                                                                                                                              * recording_enabled changes
                                                                                                                              * data_retention_days changes
                                                                                                                              * live_handoff_enabled and rule changes
                                                                                                                              * sign out all devices
                                                                                                                              * delete account
                                                                                                                              * delete all call data
                                                                                                                              * block list and VIP list changes
Audit payload rules:
                                                                                                                              * Do not store plaintext sensitive values
                                                                                                                              * Store:
                                                                                                                              * user_id
                                                                                                                              * device_id
                                                                                                                              * change type
                                                                                                                              * timestamps
                                                                                                                              * before and after hashes or minimal diffs for non sensitive fields
24.3 Presets system
24.3.1 Preset goals
                                                                                                                              * Keep user controls simple and explainable
                                                                                                                              * Reduce unsafe or unpredictable free text configuration
                                                                                                                              * Make behavior consistent across calls
                                                                                                                              * Allow future phases to reuse the same preset framework
24.3.2 Preset types in Phase 1
A) Voice presets
                                                                                                                              * Each option maps to a provider voice_profile_id
                                                                                                                              * Each option includes a short preview sample in the app
                                                                                                                              * Fallback voice_profile_id is always defined
B) Temperament presets
Required Phase 1 presets:
                                                                                                                              * professional_and_polite
                                                                                                                              * friendly_and_casual
                                                                                                                              * short_and_direct
                                                                                                                              * warm_and_supportive
Each preset expands into concrete parameters used by the AI pipeline:
                                                                                                                              * formality level
                                                                                                                              * friendliness level
                                                                                                                              * verbosity target
                                                                                                                              * humor allowance
                                                                                                                              * default swearing_level
                                                                                                                              * default response_length_preference
C) Call objective presets
                                                                                                                              * screen_and_summarize
                                                                                                                              * message_only
D) Call length presets
                                                                                                                              * 2 minutes
                                                                                                                              * 3 minutes
                                                                                                                              * 5 minutes
VIP override presets:
                                                                                                                              * 3 minutes
                                                                                                                              * 5 minutes
E) Greeting templates
                                                                                                                                 * standard greeting
                                                                                                                                 * after hours greeting
                                                                                                                                 * VIP greeting
F) After hours behavior presets
                                                                                                                                 * screen_normally
                                                                                                                                 * message_only
                                                                                                                                 * screen_low_priority
24.3.3 Preset storage strategy
Presets are not stored as arbitrary text. They are stored as stable IDs that map to a preset definition.
Preset definition storage options for Phase 1:
                                                                                                                                 * Defined in backend code as a versioned registry, or
                                                                                                                                 * Stored in a presets table with:
                                                                                                                                 * preset_id
                                                                                                                                 * preset_type
                                                                                                                                 * preset_version
                                                                                                                                 * display_name
                                                                                                                                 * description
                                                                                                                                 * payload_json
Phase 1 requirement:
                                                                                                                                 * preset_id must be stable so a user selection does not change meaning unexpectedly.
24.3.4 Preset versioning rules
                                                                                                                                 * If a preset behavior must change materially, create a new preset_id rather than silently changing the old preset.
                                                                                                                                 * Old preset_ids can be marked deprecated for new users but must remain supported for existing users.
                                                                                                                                 * The user_settings stores selected preset_ids, not raw payloads, to keep data small and consistent.
24.3.5 Presets and AI prompt packs
Temperament presets and greeting templates influence which prompt pack parameters are applied.
Rules:
                                                                                                                                 * A prompt pack version is selected server side.
                                                                                                                                 * Presets provide parameters into the prompt pack, not raw instructions.
                                                                                                                                 * The call record stores the prompt pack version and key preset IDs used for that call as part of the snapshot.
24.4 Server enforced policies
24.4.1 Why server enforcement is required
Settings affect cost, privacy, and safety. Server side enforcement ensures:
                                                                                                                                 * Approval first cannot be bypassed by a modified client
                                                                                                                                 * Notification privacy rules are consistent across devices
                                                                                                                                 * Recording disclosures happen when recording is enabled
                                                                                                                                 * Call length limits are respected to control costs
                                                                                                                                 * Block list enforcement is reliable and not dependent on client behavior
24.4.2 Enforcement matrix (what is enforced where)
Telephony layer enforcement:
                                                                                                                                 * blocked callers handling
                                                                                                                                 * dedicated number access policy
                                                                                                                                 * max call length hard stop
                                                                                                                                 * one handoff attempt per call
AI runtime enforcement:
                                                                                                                                 * assistant identity boundary (assistant for the user, not the user)
                                                                                                                                 * temperament and swearing limits
                                                                                                                                 * refusal fallback behavior
                                                                                                                                 * urgency confirmation question rule impacts handoff eligibility signals
Messaging enforcement:
                                                                                                                                 * approval first required before any Twilio send
                                                                                                                                 * no attachments in Phase 1
                                                                                                                                 * recipient number validation and normalization
Notification enforcement:
                                                                                                                                 * private notification default
                                                                                                                                 * no transcript content in any notification
                                                                                                                                 * quiet hours behavior and bypass rules for handoff
Data enforcement:
                                                                                                                                 * biometric gating requirements for sensitive content display inside app
                                                                                                                                 * retention deletion scheduling based on data_retention_days
                                                                                                                                 * memory creation only within allowlisted categories
24.4.3 Settings validation rules
The server validates all settings updates with strict rules:
                                                                                                                                 * Assistant name: bounded length, safe character set, no impersonation phrasing
                                                                                                                                 * Schedules: valid day sets and times, supports crossing midnight, timezone must be valid
                                                                                                                                 * Call lengths: only allowed preset values
                                                                                                                                 * Handoff rule: must be one of the allowed enums
                                                                                                                                 * Notification privacy mode: only private or preview
                                                                                                                                 * Recording: if enabled, require disclosure flag true and biometric gating enabled for playback
                                                                                                                                 * Retention: must be one of allowed windows
                                                                                                                                 * Lists: VIP and block list entries must have normalized numbers, stored hashes, and safe display names
24.4.4 Hard locked Phase 1 constraints reflected as settings
Some constraints are not user configurable in Phase 1, but are stored as configuration fields so future phases can expand without schema churn:
                                                                                                                                 * Language: English only enforced, but language preference fields exist
                                                                                                                                 * Region: US only enforced, but region field can exist for future provisioning logic
                                                                                                                                 * Text approval: approval first only enforced, optional exception fields exist but are inactive by default
24.4.5 Safe default initialization during onboarding
Onboarding must set a minimal set of settings so the system behaves predictably immediately:
                                                                                                                                 * Select voice preset
                                                                                                                                 * Select temperament preset
                                                                                                                                 * Set business hours schedule and after hours behavior
                                                                                                                                 * Optionally add at least one VIP entry
                                                                                                                                 * Confirm texting is enabled and remains approval first
                                                                                                                                 * Quiet hours schedule is set or left disabled with a clear default
24.4.6 Completion checklist for this chapter
This chapter is complete when:
                                                                                                                                 * The settings catalog is fully implemented with defined keys, allowed values, defaults, and behavior effects
                                                                                                                                 * Settings are stored as a structured, versioned model with schema_version and revision
                                                                                                                                 * Multi device concurrency is handled with optimistic locking and conflict responses
                                                                                                                                 * Presets exist for voice, temperament, call objective, call length, greetings, and after hours behavior
                                                                                                                                 * Settings changes synchronize across devices reliably
                                                                                                                                 * Sensitive settings changes are audit logged without storing sensitive content
                                                                                                                                 * Server enforcement guarantees privacy, safety, and approval first behavior regardless of client implementation
________________


CHAPTER 25: DATA LIFECYCLE, RETENTION, AND DELETION
25.0 Purpose and scope
This chapter defines how Phase 1 manages data from creation to deletion. It covers:
                                                                                                                                 * The retention policy model and how it applies across all stored call content
                                                                                                                                 * Per-call deletion behavior, including exactly what is deleted and how deletion is verified
                                                                                                                                 * Delete all call data and account deletion flows
                                                                                                                                 * Memory management rules and user controls
                                                                                                                                 * Local device cache handling, encryption, and clearing rules
Phase 1 goal: user control is a product requirement. Users must be able to understand what exists, how long it stays, and how to remove it.
________________


25.0.1 Data categories handled in Phase 1
Phase 1 data is separated into these categories so retention and deletion rules are consistent:
A) Identity and account data
                                                                                                                                 * User account profile
                                                                                                                                 * Auth identities and sessions
                                                                                                                                 * Device records and push tokens
                                                                                                                                 * User settings and preferences
                                                                                                                                 * Phone number mappings and provisioning records
B) Call record data (per call)
                                                                                                                                 * Call metadata: timestamps, duration, routing mode, outcome
                                                                                                                                 * Caller identity fields: masked number, optional name, normalized callback number if provided
                                                                                                                                 * AI artifacts: summary, extracted fields, labels, label reasons
                                                                                                                                 * Follow-up actions: text drafts, approvals, send statuses
                                                                                                                                 * Reminders linked to calls
                                                                                                                                 * Optional recording references if recording is enabled
                                                                                                                                 * Transcript availability references (provider IDs and statuses)
C) Memory data (cross-call)
                                                                                                                                 * Memory items that persist beyond a single call (preference-style only)
                                                                                                                                 * Memory items are visible and deletable in-app
                                                                                                                                 * Memory on-off flag and per-item deletion controls
D) Operational metadata
                                                                                                                                 * Audit events (metadata-only, no call content)
                                                                                                                                 * System logs and metrics (strictly minimized content)
                                                                                                                                 * Background job state and dedupe keys
________________


25.0.2 Storage model assumption for Phase 1 call content
Phase 1 prioritizes data minimization:
                                                                                                                                 * Call audio files are not stored in MattBot storage.
                                                                                                                                 * Transcripts and media artifacts are treated as provider-owned artifacts when available, referenced by provider IDs.
                                                                                                                                 * MattBot stores derived, user-facing artifacts needed for workflows (summary, extracted fields, labels, label reasons, actions, reminders), plus minimal provider references needed to retrieve or delete provider artifacts.
Retention and deletion must still behave as if “call content” is fully governed, meaning: when a call is deleted in MattBot, all locally stored artifacts are removed and provider deletion is attempted where supported.
________________


25.1 Retention policy model
25.1.1 Retention options
Phase 1 supports simple retention options:
                                                                                                                                 * 7 days
                                                                                                                                 * 30 days
                                                                                                                                 * 90 days
These are account-wide settings. They apply uniformly to all call records.
25.1.2 Default retention
Default retention for Phase 1 is 30 days.
Rationale in product terms: 30 days balances usefulness (reviewing recent calls, follow-ups) with privacy risk. The UI must explain that longer retention increases privacy risk.
25.1.3 What retention applies to
Retention must apply to all call record content consistently. Retention is not allowed to delete only part of a call record because partial retention breaks trust and creates confusion.
Retention applies to:
                                                                                                                                 * Call record row and all per-call metadata
                                                                                                                                 * Summary and extracted fields
                                                                                                                                 * Labels and label reasons
                                                                                                                                 * Notes if implemented
                                                                                                                                 * Follow-up action history (text drafts, approvals, send results)
                                                                                                                                 * Call-linked reminders
                                                                                                                                 * Provider references for transcript and recording, and any locally cached transcript snippets if stored
                                                                                                                                 * Any call-linked “pending memory suggestions” that are not confirmed as separate memory items
Retention does not automatically delete cross-call memory items that the user intentionally saved as independent memory entries. Those are controlled by memory deletion controls (see 25.4).
25.1.4 Retention anchor timestamp
Retention countdown is based on call completion time:
                                                                                                                                 * retention_anchor = call_ended_at (UTC)
If call_ended_at is missing due to abnormal termination, fall back to:
                                                                                                                                 * retention_anchor = max(call_started_at, created_at)
25.1.5 Retention scheduler design
Retention expiration is enforced by a server-side sweeper and purge workers.
A) Sweeper job
                                                                                                                                 * Runs on a fixed cadence (example: hourly)
                                                                                                                                 * Queries for call records where:
                                                                                                                                 * call_ended_at <= now - retention_window
                                                                                                                                 * deleted_at is null
                                                                                                                                 * Marks them as deletion_requested with reason retention_expired
                                                                                                                                 * Enqueues purge jobs for each call_id (or batches if needed)
B) Purge worker
                                                                                                                                 * Executes the actual deletion (hard delete of content tables)
                                                                                                                                 * Attempts provider deletion where applicable
                                                                                                                                 * Is idempotent and safe to retry
25.1.6 Separation between “hide” and “purge”
To deliver instant UI results without blocking on external providers or heavy deletes, Phase 1 uses a two-step model:
                                                                                                                                 1. Soft delete flag (hide immediately)
                                                                                                                                 * deleted_at set
                                                                                                                                 * deletion_reason set (retention_expired, user_single_call_delete, delete_all_call_data, account_deletion)
                                                                                                                                 * Call list queries exclude deleted rows
                                                                                                                                 2. Purge (hard delete)
                                                                                                                                 * Physical deletes across dependent tables
                                                                                                                                 * Provider deletion attempts
                                                                                                                                 * Cleanup of dangling references and caches
The system must ensure soft deleted call rows are not accessible through any API, even if their dependent data still exists for a short time before purge.
25.1.7 Provider artifact deletion policy under retention
When retention expires, MattBot must attempt to delete provider-side artifacts that were created for the call, when provider APIs support deletion.
                                                                                                                                 * For recordings stored in a telephony provider: attempt delete using provider recording ID.
                                                                                                                                 * For transcripts stored in an AI provider: attempt delete using provider conversation or transcript ID.
If provider deletion is not available or fails permanently, MattBot must still:
                                                                                                                                 * Remove all local references that allow retrieval
                                                                                                                                 * Record provider_deletion_status = not_supported or failed
                                                                                                                                 * Ensure the user cannot access the provider artifact through MattBot after deletion
25.1.8 Backups and retention reality
Database backups and infrastructure snapshots can temporarily contain deleted data until backup retention expires. Phase 1 must:
                                                                                                                                 * Configure short backup retention suitable for production stability (example: 7 days for automated backups)
                                                                                                                                 * Document in internal operational policy that deleted call data may persist in backups until those backups expire
                                                                                                                                 * Ensure backups are encrypted and access-controlled
                                                                                                                                 * Ensure restores are treated as a security event with restricted access
This prevents “silent retention growth” through operational artifacts.
________________


25.2 Per-call deletion
25.2.1 User experience trigger
The user can delete a single call record from the call detail view.
Deletion must:
                                                                                                                                 * Require confirmation because it is irreversible in normal operation
                                                                                                                                 * Update UI immediately to remove the call record from lists
                                                                                                                                 * Be safe under poor network conditions and retries
25.2.2 Exactly what gets deleted for a single call
Deleting a single call record removes:
                                                                                                                                 * Call summary
                                                                                                                                 * Extracted fields
                                                                                                                                 * Labels and label reasons
                                                                                                                                 * Transcript reference and any locally stored transcript snippets
                                                                                                                                 * Recording reference if any
                                                                                                                                 * Notes linked to the call
                                                                                                                                 * Follow-up action history linked to the call:
                                                                                                                                 * drafts, approvals, send attempts, provider message IDs
                                                                                                                                 * Reminders attached to that call
                                                                                                                                 * Any call-linked pending memory suggestions (not confirmed memory)
Confirmed memory items are not deleted automatically, but their source_call_id reference is handled safely:
                                                                                                                                 * Either retain the memory item and set source_call_id to null, or keep it but accept that the source call is missing
                                                                                                                                 * The memory UI must not break if the source call no longer exists
25.2.3 Deletion API contract
DELETE /calls/{call_id}
Server-side steps:
                                                                                                                                 1. Authorization
                                                                                                                                 * Verify the call belongs to the authenticated user
                                                                                                                                 * Deny if not owner
                                                                                                                                 2. Soft delete transaction
                                                                                                                                 * Set calls.deleted_at = now
                                                                                                                                 * Set calls.deletion_reason = user_single_call_delete
                                                                                                                                 * Create audit event call_deleted with metadata only
                                                                                                                                 * Enqueue purge job with call_id and deletion_reason
                                                                                                                                 3. Response
                                                                                                                                 * Return success immediately with:
                                                                                                                                 * call_id
                                                                                                                                 * deleted_at
                                                                                                                                 * purge_status = queued
25.2.4 Purge worker behavior for per-call deletion
The purge worker:
                                                                                                                                 * Hard deletes dependent rows in a deterministic order to satisfy foreign keys
                                                                                                                                 * Attempts provider deletions
                                                                                                                                 * Deletes encrypted blobs for summaries, label reasons, and message bodies
                                                                                                                                 * Records purge completion in a deletion_jobs table (optional but recommended)
Recommended deletion order:
                                                                                                                                 1. Dependent child tables
                                                                                                                                 * labels, label_reasons
                                                                                                                                 * summaries and extracted fields
                                                                                                                                 * reminders
                                                                                                                                 * actions and send attempts
                                                                                                                                 * call notes
                                                                                                                                 * call-to-provider reference rows
                                                                                                                                 2. The call row (or keep a tombstone row briefly)
                                                                                                                                 * If a tombstone is used:
                                                                                                                                 * keep minimal metadata only (call_id, deleted_at, deletion_reason)
                                                                                                                                 * expire tombstone within a short window
25.2.5 Verification and user-visible proof
Phase 1 includes two verification signals:
A) Immediate verification
                                                                                                                                 * Call disappears from lists and is not retrievable by call_id
B) Completion verification
