                                                                                                                                 * Call deletion job status is visible in the call detail if the user navigates immediately after deletion via deep link, or in a lightweight “recently deleted” activity log if implemented
                                                                                                                                 * If purge failed due to provider errors, the system still considers the call deleted locally, but records provider deletion failure internally
25.2.6 Idempotency and retries
If the user repeats the delete action:
                                                                                                                                 * If calls.deleted_at is already set, return success with current deleted_at and current purge status
                                                                                                                                 * Never recreate call records or re-expose content
                                                                                                                                 * Purge job enqueue can be deduped by (call_id, deletion_reason)
________________


25.3 Account deletion
25.3.1 User intent and scope
Account deletion is a destructive operation. It must delete:
                                                                                                                                 * All call data
                                                                                                                                 * All memory items
                                                                                                                                 * All settings
                                                                                                                                 * All devices and sessions
                                                                                                                                 * Phone number mappings and provisioning references
Account deletion must be explicit, confirmed, and not reversible in normal operation.
25.3.2 Account deletion flow (two-stage)
Phase 1 uses a staged account deletion flow for safety and reliability:
Stage 1: Immediate account lock and revoke
                                                                                                                                 * Set user.status = pending_deletion
                                                                                                                                 * Revoke all sessions and device tokens
                                                                                                                                 * Disable inbound call handling for the user (no answering, no handoff, no texting)
                                                                                                                                 * Hide all call records and personal data from APIs
Stage 2: Background purge
                                                                                                                                 * Enqueue a purge_account job that deletes all user-owned data with bounded retries
                                                                                                                                 * When complete, hard delete or tombstone the user record
25.3.3 Account deletion API contract
POST /account/delete
Server-side steps:
                                                                                                                                 1. Strong confirmation
                                                                                                                                 * Require a confirmation step in the UI
                                                                                                                                 * Require re-authentication or biometric gating depending on product security policy
                                                                                                                                 2. State change
                                                                                                                                 * status pending_deletion
                                                                                                                                 * revoke sessions and devices
                                                                                                                                 * audit event account_deletion_requested
                                                                                                                                 3. Enqueue purge job
                                                                                                                                 * purge_account(user_id)
                                                                                                                                 4. Response
                                                                                                                                 * Success with status pending_deletion
25.3.4 Purge job responsibilities
The purge_account worker deletes in this order:
                                                                                                                                 1. Sessions, device tokens, push tokens
                                                                                                                                 2. Call data and all dependent artifacts
                                                                                                                                 3. Memory items
                                                                                                                                 4. Settings documents and list tables (VIP list, block list)
                                                                                                                                 5. Phone number mappings and provisioning references
                                                                                                                                 6. Remaining user profile row, or convert to tombstone
25.3.5 Provider cleanup during account deletion
Account deletion must also attempt provider cleanup:
                                                                                                                                 * Release or unassign dedicated numbers if they are owned by the platform account and mapped to the user
                                                                                                                                 * Delete recordings and other provider artifacts where supported
                                                                                                                                 * Ensure inbound routing no longer recognizes the user mapping
Provider cleanup is best-effort but must never block local deletion completion. Failures are logged internally as operational issues.
25.3.6 Account deletion completion guarantees
When deletion is complete:
                                                                                                                                 * User cannot authenticate
                                                                                                                                 * No call records exist in primary databases
                                                                                                                                 * No memory items exist
                                                                                                                                 * No settings exist
                                                                                                                                 * No active device sessions exist
                                                                                                                                 * Routing does not map any inbound call to the deleted user
________________


25.4 Memory management
25.4.1 Memory goals in Phase 1
Memory is intentionally small and controlled. It exists to store preference-style information that improves future interactions, not to build a complex personal profile.
Memory requirements:
                                                                                                                                 * Memory is visible in-app
                                                                                                                                 * Memory is deletable per item
                                                                                                                                 * Memory can be turned on or off
                                                                                                                                 * Memory deletion is audited (metadata-only)
25.4.2 Memory page behavior
The app must provide a Memory page that shows:
                                                                                                                                 * Each memory item
                                                                                                                                 * The category or type
                                                                                                                                 * The value
                                                                                                                                 * When it was created
                                                                                                                                 * Optional source call reference (if the call still exists)
The memory page must include:
                                                                                                                                 * Delete single memory item
                                                                                                                                 * Delete all memory items
                                                                                                                                 * Memory enabled toggle
25.4.3 Memory retention rule
Memory items persist until the user deletes them or deletes the account.
Memory is not governed by call retention windows because it is explicitly “beyond a single call.” This keeps retention semantics understandable:
                                                                                                                                 * Call retention controls call records
                                                                                                                                 * Memory controls memory items
If memory is turned off:
                                                                                                                                 * No new memory items are created
                                                                                                                                 * Existing memory items remain until deleted by the user (unless product explicitly chooses to hide them; deletion behavior remains available)
25.4.4 Delete single memory item
Deleting a memory item:
                                                                                                                                 * Removes the memory row permanently (hard delete or soft delete plus purge)
                                                                                                                                 * Removes any embeddings or indexes derived from it if implemented later
                                                                                                                                 * Logs an audit event memory_deleted
25.4.5 Delete all memory items
Delete all memory items is separate from delete all call data.
This action:
                                                                                                                                 * Requires confirmation
                                                                                                                                 * Deletes all memory items for the user
                                                                                                                                 * Logs an audit event memory_delete_all
25.4.6 Memory items and call deletion interaction
When a call is deleted:
                                                                                                                                 * Pending, call-scoped memory suggestions are deleted with the call
                                                                                                                                 * Confirmed memory items remain by default
                                                                                                                                 * If the memory item stores source_call_id, the system must tolerate missing source calls without breaking the UI
This avoids surprise data loss when users delete calls for cleanup purposes.
________________


25.5 Cache handling on devices
25.5.1 Device-local data rules
Phase 1 must define what the mobile app stores locally and how it is protected.
Allowed local storage (minimal and purpose-driven):
                                                                                                                                 * Session tokens or refresh tokens (secure storage only)
                                                                                                                                 * Non-sensitive UI preferences (theme, minor UI state)
                                                                                                                                 * Cached call list metadata (minimal, no full phone numbers)
                                                                                                                                 * Cached call detail artifacts only when necessary for offline viewing:
                                                                                                                                 * summary
                                                                                                                                 * extracted fields
                                                                                                                                 * labels
                                                                                                                                 * transcripts only if the user explicitly opens and loads them
Forbidden local storage:
                                                                                                                                 * Raw audio recordings
                                                                                                                                 * Full phone numbers in plaintext storage
                                                                                                                                 * Full transcript content in unencrypted storage
                                                                                                                                 * Plaintext SMS draft bodies outside secure storage
25.5.2 Encryption and secure storage requirements
All sensitive cached data must be encrypted at rest on the device.
iOS
                                                                                                                                 * Use Keychain for secrets and encryption keys
                                                                                                                                 * Use encrypted local storage for cached call artifacts
Android
                                                                                                                                 * Use Keystore for secrets and encryption keys
                                                                                                                                 * Use encrypted local storage for cached call artifacts
Encryption requirements:
                                                                                                                                 * Per-user key material, scoped so one user’s cache is not readable under another account on the same device
                                                                                                                                 * Automatic cache invalidation on sign out and on account deletion
                                                                                                                                 * Defense against screenshots is UX-level, not storage-level, but sensitive screens should use platform protections where possible
25.5.3 Cache invalidation rules
Cache must be cleared on:
                                                                                                                                 * Sign out
                                                                                                                                 * Sign out all devices (on next app open and by remote push if available)
                                                                                                                                 * Delete all call data
                                                                                                                                 * Account deletion
                                                                                                                                 * Device revocation
Cache should also be pruned automatically:
                                                                                                                                 * Based on retention window
                                                                                                                                 * Based on local cache size caps (to prevent unlimited growth)
25.5.4 Remote cache clearing across devices
When call data is deleted on one device, other devices must clear their cached copies.
Mechanism:
                                                                                                                                 * Backend emits a silent sync notification containing:
                                                                                                                                 * a deletion sync token or revision number
                                                                                                                                 * optionally a small list of deleted call IDs (bounded), or a “refresh required” signal
                                                                                                                                 * Each device, upon receiving the sync signal:
                                                                                                                                 * refreshes the call list
                                                                                                                                 * removes local cached artifacts for calls that are no longer present
                                                                                                                                 * clears any offline transcript cache for deleted calls
If silent notifications are unavailable:
                                                                                                                                 * The app performs the same cleanup on next foreground open using a server-provided revision marker.
25.5.5 Preventing silent retention growth on devices
The device must never become a hidden retention store.
Controls:
                                                                                                                                 * Local cache TTL should be equal to or shorter than server retention
                                                                                                                                 * Local cache pruning runs periodically
                                                                                                                                 * Local cache is cleared on any delete-all action
                                                                                                                                 * The app must not keep unlimited transcript history offline by default
________________


25.6 Completion checklist for this chapter
This chapter is complete when:
                                                                                                                                 * Retention options exist (7, 30, 90 days) with default 30 days
                                                                                                                                 * Retention applies consistently to all call record content and does not partially delete calls
                                                                                                                                 * Retention expiration is enforced by a sweeper plus purge workers, with reliable monitoring
                                                                                                                                 * Users can delete a single call record, delete all call data, and delete their account
                                                                                                                                 * Per-call deletion removes all call-linked artifacts, actions, and reminders, and blocks access immediately
                                                                                                                                 * Account deletion revokes sessions immediately and purges all user data in the background
                                                                                                                                 * Memory is visible, deletable per item, deletable in bulk, and can be turned on or off
                                                                                                                                 * Device caches store only minimal data, encrypted at rest, and are cleared correctly on delete actions and sign out
                                                                                                                                 * Cross-device cache invalidation is implemented so deleted content is removed from all devices quickly
________________


CHAPTER 26: SECURITY ARCHITECTURE
26.0 Purpose and scope
This chapter defines the Phase 1 security architecture across network, data, webhooks, secrets, mobile, and abuse prevention. The system handles personal communications and derived content (summaries, extracted fields, reminders, drafts), so security is treated as mandatory MVP scope, not a later enhancement.
Primary goals:
                                                                                                                                 * Prevent cross-user data exposure through strict authorization and isolation
                                                                                                                                 * Protect personal data in transit and at rest
                                                                                                                                 * Minimize stored data and prevent sensitive content leakage via logs or notifications
                                                                                                                                 * Ensure webhook endpoints cannot be spoofed or replayed to trigger actions
                                                                                                                                 * Ensure mobile devices store data safely and require biometrics for sensitive content
                                                                                                                                 * Prevent abuse patterns that can create cost spikes or service instability
Threat model focus:
                                                                                                                                 * Unauthorized access to call content (summary, transcript references, recordings references)
                                                                                                                                 * Account takeover leading to message sending or data extraction
                                                                                                                                 * Forged or replayed webhooks that create fake calls or send texts
                                                                                                                                 * Sensitive content leakage through notifications, logs, or device caches
                                                                                                                                 * Abuse by high volume calls, repeated handoff attempts, and SMS spam
Security principles:
                                                                                                                                 * Defense in depth: multiple independent controls for the same risk
                                                                                                                                 * Least privilege: minimum access required for each component and user role
                                                                                                                                 * Fail closed: if a security check cannot be performed, reject the action
                                                                                                                                 * Data minimization: do not store what is not needed for product behavior
                                                                                                                                 * Deterministic audit: record sensitive actions with metadata only
________________


26.1 Network security
26.1.1 VPC topology (production)
Phase 1 uses a single VPC with clear public and private separation.
Subnets:
                                                                                                                                 * Public subnets:
                                                                                                                                 * Application Load Balancer (ALB)
                                                                                                                                 * NAT Gateway (if private instances require outbound internet)
                                                                                                                                 * Private subnets:
                                                                                                                                 * Application compute instances (EC2 running Docker services)
                                                                                                                                 * RDS PostgreSQL (no public access)
Routing:
                                                                                                                                 * Public subnets route 0.0.0.0/0 to an Internet Gateway
                                                                                                                                 * Private subnets route 0.0.0.0/0 to a NAT Gateway for outbound calls to providers (Twilio, ElevenLabs, push providers, OAuth, etc.)
                                                                                                                                 * RDS remains in private subnets with no internet route and no public IP
26.1.2 Security group rules
Security Groups must be restrictive and explicit.
ALB security group:
                                                                                                                                 * Inbound:
                                                                                                                                 * TCP 443 from 0.0.0.0/0
                                                                                                                                 * TCP 80 optional only for HTTP to HTTPS redirect, otherwise block
                                                                                                                                 * Outbound:
                                                                                                                                 * TCP to application instances on the app listener port only
Application instance security group:
                                                                                                                                 * Inbound:
                                                                                                                                 * Only from ALB security group on the application port (for example 443 or 8080 depending on container design)
                                                                                                                                 * SSH not open to the internet
                                                                                                                                 * Outbound:
                                                                                                                                 * Allow outbound to internet via NAT for provider calls
                                                                                                                                 * Prefer explicit egress controls where practical, but do not break provider connectivity
RDS security group:
                                                                                                                                 * Inbound:
                                                                                                                                 * PostgreSQL port from application instance security group only
                                                                                                                                 * Outbound:
                                                                                                                                 * Default outbound is acceptable but not required for RDS operations
26.1.3 No public database policy
RDS must be configured with:
                                                                                                                                 * Public accessibility disabled
                                                                                                                                 * Private subnets only
                                                                                                                                 * Security group inbound limited to app security group
                                                                                                                                 * Database credentials not shared across environments
                                                                                                                                 * Separate DB users for application and admin operations (least privilege)
26.1.4 TLS termination and HTTP hardening
ALB is the public entrypoint.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                 * TLS required on all external traffic
                                                                                                                                 * Redirect HTTP to HTTPS if HTTP is enabled for redirect
                                                                                                                                 * Enforce modern TLS policy (TLS 1.2+ minimum)
                                                                                                                                 * Enable HSTS response headers at the application layer:
                                                                                                                                 * Strict-Transport-Security with a safe max-age and includeSubDomains where appropriate
                                                                                                                                 * Disable weak ciphers by selecting a strict ALB security policy
26.1.5 AWS WAF at the edge
Attach AWS WAF to the ALB.
WAF rules (Phase 1 baseline):
                                                                                                                                 * Managed rules for common web exploits (SQL injection patterns, known bad inputs)
                                                                                                                                 * Rate-based rule for API flood protection:
                                                                                                                                 * Apply to public endpoints such as auth, webhook endpoints, and any unauthenticated routes
                                                                                                                                 * Block requests with abnormal payload sizes or suspicious content types
                                                                                                                                 * Optional IP allow list for internal admin portal routes if the admin portal exists in Phase 1
26.1.6 Service exposure rules
External exposure must be limited to:
                                                                                                                                 * ALB public endpoints for API and webhook ingestion
                                                                                                                                 * No direct public access to:
                                                                                                                                 * compute instances
                                                                                                                                 * RDS
                                                                                                                                 * internal worker endpoints
Any internal service-to-service communication must be inside the VPC.
26.1.7 Admin portal network controls (if present)
If an operator admin portal exists:
                                                                                                                                 * Place it behind the same ALB but on a separate host or path namespace
                                                                                                                                 * Require strong authentication and MFA for admin users
                                                                                                                                 * Prefer IP allow listing if admin usage is restricted to known locations
                                                                                                                                 * Apply stricter WAF rules to admin routes than to general mobile API routes
26.1.8 Network logging and anomaly detection
Enable network-level signals without storing sensitive content.
Required:
                                                                                                                                 * ALB access logs stored in an encrypted bucket with retention limits
                                                                                                                                 * VPC flow logs enabled for troubleshooting and security reviews
                                                                                                                                 * Alerts on:
                                                                                                                                 * sudden spikes in request rate
                                                                                                                                 * repeated 401/403 patterns
                                                                                                                                 * unusual webhook traffic volume
________________


26.2 Data security
26.2.1 Data classification
Classify data so controls are consistent.
High sensitivity:
                                                                                                                                 * Transcripts and transcript-derived evidence snippets
                                                                                                                                 * Recording references and playback identifiers
                                                                                                                                 * Summary content and extracted call details
                                                                                                                                 * Outbound message bodies (draft and final)
                                                                                                                                 * Caller phone number full E.164 (where stored for actions)
Medium sensitivity:
                                                                                                                                 * Caller masked number and last4
                                                                                                                                 * Labels and label reasons (can imply private topics)
                                                                                                                                 * Reminder titles (can be private)
Low sensitivity:
                                                                                                                                 * Non-PII operational metrics and status fields
                                                                                                                                 * Aggregated counts without identifiers
26.2.2 Encryption in transit
All data in transit must be encrypted.
External:
                                                                                                                                 * TLS between mobile apps and backend (HTTPS only)
                                                                                                                                 * TLS between backend and providers (Twilio, ElevenLabs, push providers, OAuth)
Internal:
                                                                                                                                 * TLS between app containers and RDS must be enabled (client requires SSL)
                                                                                                                                 * If multiple internal services communicate over HTTP inside the VPC, they must either:
                                                                                                                                 * use mTLS, or
                                                                                                                                 * remain private and still enforce authentication and allow lists between services
Phase 1 allows private HTTP inside the VPC if strict security groups and service authentication are implemented, but DB connections must use TLS.
26.2.3 Encryption at rest: database
RDS PostgreSQL must be encrypted at rest.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Storage encryption enabled at DB creation
                                                                                                                                    * Automated backups encrypted
                                                                                                                                    * Snapshots encrypted
                                                                                                                                    * Access to snapshots restricted to operator roles only
26.2.4 Encryption at rest: object storage
Any S3 usage (logs, artifacts, exports if later added) must be encrypted.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Default bucket encryption enabled
                                                                                                                                    * Bucket policies deny unencrypted uploads
                                                                                                                                    * Block public access enabled
                                                                                                                                    * Object access restricted by least-privilege IAM roles
26.2.5 Encryption at rest: compute and disks
Compute storage must be protected.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * EBS volumes encrypted
                                                                                                                                    * No sensitive data written to ephemeral disk unless required and then deleted promptly
                                                                                                                                    * Container logs must avoid sensitive content so disk persistence does not become a leakage path
26.2.6 Application-level encryption for sensitive fields
In addition to platform encryption, Phase 1 must encrypt selected fields at the application layer, because these fields are directly user-private and may appear in DB queries, logs, or exports if mishandled.
Encrypt at application level:
                                                                                                                                    * Summary text
                                                                                                                                    * Label reasons evidence snippets
                                                                                                                                    * Outbound message bodies
                                                                                                                                    * Any stored full phone numbers (if stored, otherwise store hashes + last4 only)
Implementation requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Use envelope encryption:
                                                                                                                                    * A single master secret is stored only in secure server environment configuration
                                                                                                                                    * Per-record or per-field data keys derived and rotated through a key version strategy
                                                                                                                                    * Store key_version alongside ciphertext to support rotation
                                                                                                                                    * Decryption allowed only in the API layer after authorization checks and only for endpoints that require content
26.2.7 Access control and authorization enforcement
Backend must enforce per-user data isolation on every read and write.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Every call record query includes owner_user_id filter
                                                                                                                                    * All actions (handoff accept, texting approval, reminder creation, deletion) require ownership verification
                                                                                                                                    * Avoid using client-provided user identifiers for authorization decisions
                                                                                                                                    * Use server-side session identity as the only authority
Admin access:
                                                                                                                                    * If an internal admin portal exists, it must implement:
                                                                                                                                    * role-based access control
                                                                                                                                    * strict audit logging
                                                                                                                                    * no default ability to view sensitive call content unless explicitly required for support, and then gated by additional approval mechanisms
26.2.8 Logging and sensitive data minimization
Logs are a common leakage path. Phase 1 rules:
Never log:
                                                                                                                                    * full transcript text
                                                                                                                                    * summary text
                                                                                                                                    * outbound message bodies
                                                                                                                                    * full phone numbers
                                                                                                                                    * provider secrets or signed URLs
Allowed logs (examples):
                                                                                                                                    * call_id, user_id (internal), device_id
                                                                                                                                    * artifact status transitions
                                                                                                                                    * error codes and short bounded messages
                                                                                                                                    * masked phone last4 or hashes only
                                                                                                                                    * provider identifiers (CallSid, MessageSid) without content
Implement centralized log redaction:
                                                                                                                                    * Before emitting logs, pass fields through a redactor that:
                                                                                                                                    * masks phone number patterns
                                                                                                                                    * truncates free-form text fields
                                                                                                                                    * strips headers that can include secrets
26.2.9 Backups, restores, and retention reality
Backups are necessary for stability but affect deletion semantics.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Database backups encrypted and retention limited
                                                                                                                                    * Access to backups restricted to operators with explicit need
                                                                                                                                    * Restores treated as a sensitive operation with auditing
                                                                                                                                    * Deletion in the primary database is enforced immediately, but backup data persists until backup retention expires. This is handled as an operational reality, not a product feature.
________________


26.3 Webhook security
26.3.1 Webhook endpoints covered
Phase 1 webhooks include:
                                                                                                                                    * Twilio voice webhooks (call start, call status changes, dial actions)
                                                                                                                                    * Twilio messaging status callbacks (if enabled)
                                                                                                                                    * ElevenLabs webhooks (post-call completion, transcript availability, or other provider events)
26.3.2 Twilio signature validation
All Twilio webhooks must validate the Twilio signature.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Validate X-Twilio-Signature against the exact request URL and parameters Twilio signed
                                                                                                                                    * Ensure the server reconstructs the correct public URL when behind ALB:
                                                                                                                                    * use Host header and X-Forwarded-Proto correctly
                                                                                                                                    * Reject the request if:
                                                                                                                                    * signature missing
                                                                                                                                    * signature invalid
                                                                                                                                    * Fail closed: if validation code errors, reject
26.3.3 Replay defense and idempotency for Twilio
Even with signatures, replay is possible. Enforce replay controls:
                                                                                                                                    * Store dedupe keys for recent webhook events:
                                                                                                                                    * voice: CallSid + event type + timestamp bucket
                                                                                                                                    * messaging: MessageSid + status
                                                                                                                                    * Reject duplicates within a bounded replay window
                                                                                                                                    * Ensure all webhook handlers are idempotent:
                                                                                                                                    * repeated callbacks do not create duplicate call records
                                                                                                                                    * repeated status events do not regress state incorrectly
26.3.4 ElevenLabs webhook authentication and replay defense
ElevenLabs webhook events must be authenticated and time-bounded.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Validate provider HMAC signature using the shared secret
                                                                                                                                    * Validate timestamp window (for example, within a bounded tolerance)
                                                                                                                                    * Reject events that:
                                                                                                                                    * have invalid signature
                                                                                                                                    * fall outside the allowed timestamp window
                                                                                                                                    * duplicate provider event IDs within the replay window
26.3.5 Webhook request hardening
To reduce exploit surface:
                                                                                                                                    * Enforce strict content type validation for JSON routes
                                                                                                                                    * Enforce maximum request body size per endpoint
                                                                                                                                    * Enforce short server timeouts
                                                                                                                                    * Reject unexpected HTTP methods
                                                                                                                                    * Validate required fields and ignore unknown fields by default
                                                                                                                                    * Never execute provider supplied URLs or commands
26.3.6 Webhook to action boundaries
Webhooks can create records and update statuses, but must not directly trigger irreversible user-impacting actions without internal policy checks.
Rules:
                                                                                                                                    * Webhooks can update:
                                                                                                                                    * call status
                                                                                                                                    * transcript ready status
                                                                                                                                    * message delivery status
                                                                                                                                    * Webhooks must not trigger:
                                                                                                                                    * outbound texts without approval
                                                                                                                                    * handoff acceptance (only authenticated device can accept)
                                                                                                                                    * list modifications (VIP or block list)
________________


26.4 Secrets handling strategy
26.4.1 Secrets inventory
Phase 1 secrets include:
                                                                                                                                    * Twilio:
                                                                                                                                    * Account SID
                                                                                                                                    * Auth token
                                                                                                                                    * API keys (if used)
                                                                                                                                    * ElevenLabs:
                                                                                                                                    * API key
                                                                                                                                    * webhook signing secret
                                                                                                                                    * Database:
                                                                                                                                    * master password or app user password
                                                                                                                                    * Auth:
                                                                                                                                    * JWT signing keys (with key IDs for rotation)
                                                                                                                                    * OAuth client secrets (Google, Apple if used)
                                                                                                                                    * Push:
                                                                                                                                    * APNS authentication key or certificates
                                                                                                                                    * FCM server key or service account credentials
                                                                                                                                    * Observability:
                                                                                                                                    * Sentry auth and ingestion keys (self-hosted environment)
Rule: no secrets embedded in the mobile app build.
26.4.2 Environment strategy
Development:
                                                                                                                                    * Use local .env files for developers
                                                                                                                                    * .env must be ignored by version control
                                                                                                                                    * Provide a .env.example with placeholders, never real values
Production:
                                                                                                                                    * Store secrets in GitHub Secrets
                                                                                                                                    * Deploy pipeline injects secrets at deploy time into the server environment:
                                                                                                                                    * write a root-owned environment file with strict permissions
                                                                                                                                    * load into Docker containers via environment variables at runtime
                                                                                                                                    * Prevent secrets from appearing in:
                                                                                                                                    * container build layers
                                                                                                                                    * image registries
                                                                                                                                    * logs
26.4.3 Access control for secrets
                                                                                                                                    * Restrict GitHub Secrets access to protected environments and approved maintainers
                                                                                                                                    * Use branch protection so production deploys can only run from approved branches
                                                                                                                                    * Limit who can trigger deployments
                                                                                                                                    * Ensure server filesystem permissions prevent non-root users from reading secret files
26.4.4 Secret rotation and key versioning
Rotation must be planned even if not frequent.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Support multiple active key versions for:
                                                                                                                                    * JWT signing keys (accept old kid for verification, use newest for signing)
                                                                                                                                    * webhook secrets (allow a short overlap period if rotation occurs)
                                                                                                                                    * Rotation procedure:
                                                                                                                                    * add new secret version
                                                                                                                                    * deploy services that accept both
                                                                                                                                    * switch signing to new
                                                                                                                                    * remove old after overlap period
                                                                                                                                    * Record a metadata-only audit event for rotation actions
26.4.5 Incident response for secret exposure
If any secret is suspected exposed:
                                                                                                                                    * Immediately rotate impacted secrets
                                                                                                                                    * Invalidate sessions if auth keys impacted
                                                                                                                                    * Disable affected endpoints if needed until confirmed safe
                                                                                                                                    * Review audit logs and deployment history
                                                                                                                                    * Document and apply preventive controls to avoid repeat exposure
________________


26.5 Mobile app security
26.5.1 Secure local storage
Sensitive data stored on device must be protected by platform secure storage and encryption.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Store auth tokens in:
                                                                                                                                    * iOS Keychain
                                                                                                                                    * Android Keystore-backed encrypted storage
                                                                                                                                    * Cache sensitive call artifacts only when necessary and always encrypted:
                                                                                                                                    * summaries
                                                                                                                                    * extracted fields
                                                                                                                                    * label reasons
                                                                                                                                    * message drafts
                                                                                                                                    * Avoid offline transcript caching by default; if cached, treat as highest sensitivity
26.5.2 Biometric gating and lock rules
Biometrics must protect access to sensitive content.
Protected content:
                                                                                                                                    * call transcripts (if displayed)
                                                                                                                                    * recording playback (if enabled)
                                                                                                                                    * summary and extracted sensitive fields
                                                                                                                                    * label reasons and evidence snippets
                                                                                                                                    * outbound message content history
Lock timing (Phase 1):
                                                                                                                                    * Support at least one simple policy:
                                                                                                                                    * lock immediately on app background, or
                                                                                                                                    * lock after X minutes of inactivity
                                                                                                                                    * Enforce lock policy consistently across devices
Server alignment:
                                                                                                                                    * Even if the client fails to enforce biometrics, the server must not include sensitive content in notifications and should limit sensitive content in APIs to authenticated sessions only.
26.5.3 Screenshot and screen recording hardening
Phase 1 should reduce casual leakage:
                                                                                                                                    * Mark sensitive screens as secure where supported so they do not appear in app switcher previews
                                                                                                                                    * For platforms that support secure flags, apply them on transcript and recording screens
                                                                                                                                    * Do not rely on this as primary protection, biometrics and encryption remain primary
26.5.4 Root and jailbreak considerations
Rooted or jailbroken devices increase risk of local data extraction.
Phase 1 best-effort controls:
                                                                                                                                    * Detect common root or jailbreak signals
                                                                                                                                    * On detection:
                                                                                                                                    * warn the user
                                                                                                                                    * disable offline caching of sensitive content
                                                                                                                                    * require re-authentication more often
                                                                                                                                    * optionally block access to recordings if the risk is high
26.5.5 API transport hardening
                                                                                                                                    * Enforce HTTPS only
                                                                                                                                    * Validate server certificates through standard OS trust store
                                                                                                                                    * Consider certificate pinning as an optional enhancement, but do not depend on it for correctness
26.5.6 Session revocation and device removal
When a device is revoked:
                                                                                                                                    * Server invalidates its session immediately
                                                                                                                                    * Push token is removed or marked inactive
                                                                                                                                    * App must clear local caches on next open or on receiving a sync signal
________________


26.6 Abuse prevention
26.6.1 Rate limiting strategy
Rate limiting is required because telephony and AI pipelines are cost-sensitive.
Enforce limits at multiple layers:
A) Edge limits (WAF)
                                                                                                                                    * Rate-based rules for abusive IPs
                                                                                                                                    * Block common scanner patterns
B) Application API limits
                                                                                                                                    * Per IP:
                                                                                                                                    * auth endpoints
                                                                                                                                    * webhook endpoints (still validate signature)
                                                                                                                                    * Per account:
                                                                                                                                    * login attempts
                                                                                                                                    * device registration updates
                                                                                                                                    * Per device:
                                                                                                                                    * handoff accept and decline
                                                                                                                                    * approve and send operations
                                                                                                                                    * Per call_id:
                                                                                                                                    * prevent repeated handoff attempts
                                                                                                                                    * prevent repeated messaging approval
C) Telephony workflow limits
                                                                                                                                    * Per user per day:
                                                                                                                                    * maximum answered calls
                                                                                                                                    * maximum AI talk time
                                                                                                                                    * Per caller number:
                                                                                                                                    * repeated calls within a short time window triggers spam suspicion
                                                                                                                                    * optional temporary suppression behavior
26.6.2 Cost caps and circuit breakers
To prevent runaway billing and cascading failures, implement circuit breakers:
                                                                                                                                    * Call minutes cap:
                                                                                                                                    * if exceeded for a user in a period, system switches to message-only mode or declines calls safely based on policy
                                                                                                                                    * SMS cap:
                                                                                                                                    * limit texts sent per user per day
                                                                                                                                    * limit texts to the same recipient per day
                                                                                                                                    * Provider error circuit:
                                                                                                                                    * if Twilio or ElevenLabs error rate spikes, reduce concurrency and enter degraded mode
                                                                                                                                    * Queue backlog circuit:
                                                                                                                                    * if background queues exceed thresholds, temporarily disable non-critical jobs and preserve core call record creation
All circuit breaker triggers must be auditable and visible internally for operations.
26.6.3 Spam and solicitation controls
Phase 1 includes:
                                                                                                                                    * Block list enforcement at call answer time
                                                                                                                                    * Conservative spam labeling rules so false positives are minimized
                                                                                                                                    * “Suggest block” recommendations based on repeated patterns, requiring explicit user confirmation
