                                                                                                                                    * Prevent texting blocked callers and prevent auto-sending without approval
26.6.4 Account creation and authentication abuse controls
Controls for account takeover and fraud:
                                                                                                                                    * MFA required in Phase 1:
                                                                                                                                    * allow TOTP authenticator
                                                                                                                                    * allow email OTP as a weaker fallback
                                                                                                                                    * Rate limit OTP requests and verification attempts
                                                                                                                                    * Rate limit new account creation per IP and per device fingerprint signal (best effort)
                                                                                                                                    * Session limits:
                                                                                                                                    * set maximum session age and idle timeouts
                                                                                                                                    * allow user to revoke devices from device list
                                                                                                                                    * Alerting for suspicious patterns:
                                                                                                                                    * repeated failed logins
                                                                                                                                    * repeated MFA failures
                                                                                                                                    * sudden new device sign-ins
26.6.5 Webhook abuse controls
Even valid webhooks can be abused if endpoints are expensive.
Controls:
                                                                                                                                    * Strict signature validation before any heavy processing
                                                                                                                                    * Early rejection on invalid payload
                                                                                                                                    * Request body size limits
                                                                                                                                    * Idempotency keys to prevent repeated processing
                                                                                                                                    * Queue-based processing for heavier work so webhook handlers remain fast and do not time out
________________


26.7 Completion checklist for this chapter
This chapter is complete when:
                                                                                                                                    * Production network is segmented with VPC public and private subnets, ALB fronting the API, and RDS not publicly accessible
                                                                                                                                    * Security groups restrict traffic so only ALB can reach the app and only the app can reach RDS
                                                                                                                                    * TLS is enforced for external traffic and DB connections, and HTTP is redirected or disabled
                                                                                                                                    * WAF is enabled with managed protections and rate-based rules
                                                                                                                                    * Database, object storage, and disks are encrypted at rest, with application-level encryption for highly sensitive fields
                                                                                                                                    * Authorization is enforced server-side on every endpoint and prevents cross-user access
                                                                                                                                    * Logs and notifications are designed to avoid sensitive content leakage by default
                                                                                                                                    * Twilio and ElevenLabs webhooks are authenticated, replay-protected, and idempotent
                                                                                                                                    * Secrets are never embedded in mobile apps, are injected at deploy time in production, and rotation is supported via key versioning
                                                                                                                                    * Mobile app uses secure storage, encrypts cached sensitive content, enforces biometric gating, and clears caches on revoke and logout
                                                                                                                                    * Rate limits, cost caps, and circuit breakers exist to prevent abuse and billing spikes
________________


CHAPTER 27: COMPLIANCE AND LEGAL REQUIREMENTS
27.0 Purpose and scope
This chapter defines the compliance and legal requirements that Phase 1 must meet as a US-only, English-only phone concierge product. It covers:
                                                                                                                                    * What Phase 1 assumes for US compliance and how those assumptions map into product behavior
                                                                                                                                    * What must be included in the Privacy Policy and Terms to accurately describe Phase 1 behavior
                                                                                                                                    * The Phase 1 data residency decision and how to keep data location consistent with a US launch
Phase 1 design constraints that drive this chapter:
                                                                                                                                    * Recording is off by default and only enabled by the user
                                                                                                                                    * If recording is enabled, the assistant must announce recording at the start of the call
                                                                                                                                    * Calls are transcribed and summarized for the user, and the product must disclose this behavior
                                                                                                                                    * Outbound text follow-up exists but is approval-first and user-initiated
                                                                                                                                    * Strong privacy defaults exist (private notifications by default, biometrics gating for sensitive content, retention and deletion controls)
________________


27.1 US market compliance assumptions
27.1.1 Market and product scope assumptions
Phase 1 compliance is scoped to:
                                                                                                                                    * Country: United States only
                                                                                                                                    * Language: English only
                                                                                                                                    * Target user type: personal users
                                                                                                                                    * Primary channel: inbound calls (dedicated number and conditional forwarding fallback)
                                                                                                                                    * Secondary action: user-approved outbound SMS for follow-up
These constraints matter because the compliance surface is different for calls vs messaging and different by jurisdiction. Phase 1 stays narrow and adopts conservative disclosures to reduce legal risk.
27.1.2 Call recording consent announcement requirements
Recording is optional. When recording is disabled, the system must not store or expose audio recording artifacts. When recording is enabled, the product must behave as follows.
Recording disclosure rule (Phase 1 hard requirement):
                                                                                                                                    * At the beginning of any call that will be recorded, the assistant must announce that the call may be recorded.
Implementation requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * The recording announcement is spoken before any substantive screening questions.
                                                                                                                                    * The announcement must be short and clear.
                                                                                                                                    * The call record must store a boolean indicating recording_enabled_for_call and recording_disclosure_spoken.
Behavioral requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Recording is off by default for all users.
                                                                                                                                    * Recording can be enabled in settings.
                                                                                                                                    * Recording playback in the app must be protected behind biometrics.
Recommended announcement content shape (example structure, not exact script):
                                                                                                                                    * Identify the assistant
                                                                                                                                    * State recording may occur
                                                                                                                                    * Continue immediately into the normal call flow
27.1.3 Transcript and summarization disclosure requirements
Phase 1 produces a transcript and a summary as part of the call record. The product must disclose that call content is transcribed and summarized.
Disclosure mechanism decision for Phase 1:
Phase 1 must implement both of the following:
A) In-call disclosure
                                                                                                                                    * The assistant states early in the call that the conversation may be transcribed and summarized to help the user.
B) Legal text disclosure
                                                                                                                                    * Terms and Privacy Policy must explicitly state that calls are processed to produce transcripts and summaries.
Implementation requirements for in-call disclosure:
                                                                                                                                    * Must occur near the start of the call, after the initial greeting and before deep questioning.
                                                                                                                                    * Must not overwhelm the caller with legal language.
                                                                                                                                    * Must not include sensitive details in the disclosure itself.
                                                                                                                                    * Must set a flag in the call record indicating transcript_disclosure_spoken.
Rationale: Phase 1 is US-only and must be conservative. Relying only on a Terms or Privacy Policy disclosure is higher risk for user trust and caller expectations. In-call disclosure increases transparency.
27.1.4 Automated assistant identity and deception avoidance
Phase 1 must avoid implying the assistant is the user.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * The assistant must introduce itself as an assistant for the user.
                                                                                                                                    * The assistant must not claim to be the user, the user’s spouse, the user’s employee, or any other human identity.
                                                                                                                                    * Any caller question like “Are you Matt” must be answered with a truthful assistant identity statement.
This is both a trust requirement and a legal risk reduction requirement, especially for sensitive calls.
27.1.5 Emergency and safety limitations
Phase 1 is not an emergency service.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * The assistant must not encourage callers to rely on it for emergencies.
                                                                                                                                    * If a caller indicates an emergency, the assistant must instruct the caller to contact emergency services and should end the call or transition to a minimal safe close depending on policy.
The Terms must clearly state that the service is not designed for emergencies.
27.1.6 SMS compliance assumptions for Phase 1 follow-up texts
Phase 1 includes user-approved outbound SMS as a follow-up action from a call record.
Phase 1 assumptions to keep SMS risk low:
                                                                                                                                    * SMS sending is user-initiated and approval-first by default.
                                                                                                                                    * The system does not automatically send marketing campaigns.
                                                                                                                                    * Messages are transactional and tied to a specific call workflow (callback coordination, clarification, acknowledgment).
                                                                                                                                    * The system must support opt-out handling.
Opt-out requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * If an inbound SMS opt-out signal is received (for example, common STOP keywords), the system must mark the recipient number as opted_out_for_sms and block future sends to that number.
                                                                                                                                    * If a user attempts to send to an opted-out number, the backend must reject the send and surface a clear error.
Disclosure requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Terms and Privacy Policy must mention that outbound texting is possible and is initiated by the user through the app.
                                                                                                                                    * The product must not hide that a third-party provider is used to deliver SMS.
27.1.7 Consent logging and evidence flags
Phase 1 must store compliance-relevant flags per call and per message action. These are not transcripts. They are metadata that help verify the system behaved correctly.
Per call compliance flags:
                                                                                                                                    * recording_enabled_for_call
                                                                                                                                    * recording_disclosure_spoken
                                                                                                                                    * transcript_disclosure_spoken
                                                                                                                                    * assistant_identity_intro_spoken (optional but recommended)
                                                                                                                                    * emergency_detected (boolean) and emergency_guidance_given (boolean)
Per SMS action compliance flags:
                                                                                                                                    * approval_required (true in Phase 1)
                                                                                                                                    * approved_at and approved_by_device_id
                                                                                                                                    * recipient_opt_out_status at send time
                                                                                                                                    * provider_message_status tracking
27.1.8 App store disclosure readiness requirements
Phase 1 must prepare disclosure content consistent with actual behavior, because app store submissions require accurate data handling descriptions.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * The iOS and Android store submissions must accurately reflect:
                                                                                                                                    * what data is collected
                                                                                                                                    * whether data is linked to the user
                                                                                                                                    * retention and deletion controls
                                                                                                                                    * whether sensitive content is collected (call content)
                                                                                                                                    * whether data is shared with service providers
This includes ensuring that privacy mode defaults, biometrics gating, and deletion controls are implemented before claiming them in store disclosures.
________________


27.2 Privacy policy and terms requirements
27.2.1 General rule: describe real behavior
The Privacy Policy and Terms must match the real Phase 1 implementation. If the product does not do something, it must not claim it does. If the product does something sensitive, it must explicitly disclose it.
27.2.2 Privacy Policy required statements
The Privacy Policy must include, at minimum, clear statements for the following sections.
A) Data collected
List the categories Phase 1 collects:
                                                                                                                                    * Account data:
                                                                                                                                    * email or auth identifiers
                                                                                                                                    * device identifiers and push tokens
                                                                                                                                    * settings and preferences
                                                                                                                                    * Call metadata:
                                                                                                                                    * call timestamps, duration, routing mode, outcome
                                                                                                                                    * caller number handling (masked display, normalized storage where needed)
                                                                                                                                    * Call content derived artifacts:
                                                                                                                                    * summaries
                                                                                                                                    * extracted fields
                                                                                                                                    * labels and label reasons
                                                                                                                                    * Call content provider artifacts:
                                                                                                                                    * transcript references and transcript status
                                                                                                                                    * recording references only if recording is enabled
                                                                                                                                    * Follow-up actions:
                                                                                                                                    * message drafts and final approved messages (encrypted at rest)
                                                                                                                                    * send attempt metadata and delivery status
                                                                                                                                    * Reminders:
                                                                                                                                    * reminder title and due time, linked to a call
                                                                                                                                    * Security and operations data:
                                                                                                                                    * audit events for sensitive actions (metadata only)
                                                                                                                                    * abuse prevention and rate limit signals
B) How data is used
Phase 1 must state that data is used to:
                                                                                                                                    * Answer calls and perform screening
                                                                                                                                    * Create call records, summaries, and transcripts
                                                                                                                                    * Notify the user of call events and reminders
                                                                                                                                    * Execute user-approved follow-up actions (texts)
                                                                                                                                    * Maintain security, prevent abuse, and control costs
                                                                                                                                    * Operate and improve reliability (without exposing sensitive content in logs)
C) Service providers and sharing
Phase 1 must disclose sharing with service providers required to deliver the service, including:
                                                                                                                                    * telephony delivery and call routing
                                                                                                                                    * transcription and voice processing
                                                                                                                                    * push notification delivery
                                                                                                                                    * cloud hosting for backend and database
The disclosure must make it clear that these providers process data to deliver the service.
D) Retention and deletion
The Privacy Policy must match Phase 1 retention behavior:
                                                                                                                                    * Default retention window and available options
                                                                                                                                    * Per-call deletion support
                                                                                                                                    * Delete all call data support
                                                                                                                                    * Account deletion support
                                                                                                                                    * Memory behavior:
                                                                                                                                    * memory can be enabled or disabled
                                                                                                                                    * memory items are visible and deletable
E) Security measures
Describe controls that Phase 1 actually implements, such as:
                                                                                                                                    * encryption in transit
                                                                                                                                    * encryption at rest for sensitive fields
                                                                                                                                    * strict authorization and per-user isolation
                                                                                                                                    * biometrics gating for sensitive content in the app
                                                                                                                                    * private notifications by default
F) User controls and rights
Phase 1 must describe user-facing controls:
                                                                                                                                    * privacy mode for notifications
                                                                                                                                    * quiet hours
                                                                                                                                    * call data retention settings
                                                                                                                                    * deletion controls
                                                                                                                                    * device revoke and sign out all devices
                                                                                                                                    * SMS opt-out enforcement
G) Cross-border processing disclosure
Even with US hosting, Phase 1 must disclose that service providers may process data where they operate unless the system guarantees US-only processing for each provider. If the product configures US-only processing, the policy can state that. Otherwise it must be disclosed as possible cross-border processing.
H) Children’s privacy statement
Phase 1 should state that the service is not intended for children and does not knowingly collect children’s data.
27.2.3 Terms of Service required statements
The Terms must cover at least the following.
A) What the service does
A plain description of Phase 1 behavior:
                                                                                                                                    * answers calls when the user is busy or unreachable
                                                                                                                                    * screens callers with a short conversation
                                                                                                                                    * provides a summary and transcript to the user
                                                                                                                                    * supports optional recording if enabled by the user
                                                                                                                                    * supports user-approved follow-up actions (texts, reminders)
B) Disclosure and consent language
The Terms must align with in-call disclosures:
                                                                                                                                    * calls may be transcribed and summarized
                                                                                                                                    * calls may be recorded if enabled
                                                                                                                                    * users are responsible for ensuring their use is lawful and for using features responsibly
C) Limitations and no emergency use
The Terms must state:
                                                                                                                                    * not an emergency service
                                                                                                                                    * callers should contact emergency services for emergencies
                                                                                                                                    * the service may fail or be unavailable and should not be relied on for critical communications
D) Accuracy and reliance limitations
Phase 1 uses AI to summarize. The Terms must state:
                                                                                                                                    * summaries and labels can be incorrect
                                                                                                                                    * the user should verify important details
E) Prohibited use
At minimum prohibit:
                                                                                                                                    * harassment, illegal surveillance, or deceptive impersonation
                                                                                                                                    * attempts to bypass consent or recording rules
                                                                                                                                    * spamming via the texting feature
F) Messaging rules
Terms must include:
                                                                                                                                    * texting is user-initiated and approval-based
                                                                                                                                    * recipients can opt out and the system will block further messages
                                                                                                                                    * the user must not use texting for unlawful or abusive content
G) Account and device security responsibilities
Terms must state:
                                                                                                                                    * user responsibility to secure devices
                                                                                                                                    * device revoke is available
                                                                                                                                    * biometrics gating exists but user must keep device secure
H) Termination and deletion
Terms must describe:
                                                                                                                                    * account deletion behavior
                                                                                                                                    * call data deletion behavior
                                                                                                                                    * effects on phone number assignments (dedicated number release behavior if applicable)
27.2.4 Internal compliance checks before launch
Before Phase 1 release, the following must be verifiable through QA and logs:
                                                                                                                                    * Recording default is off for new accounts
                                                                                                                                    * When recording is enabled, the recording disclosure is spoken and logged per call
                                                                                                                                    * Transcript and summarization disclosure is spoken and logged per call
                                                                                                                                    * Private notifications are default and never include transcript content
                                                                                                                                    * SMS sending is blocked without approval
                                                                                                                                    * SMS sending is blocked when recipient is opted out
                                                                                                                                    * Retention sweeper runs and deletes expired call records correctly
                                                                                                                                    * Per-call deletion and account deletion remove access immediately
________________


27.3 Data residency decision
27.3.1 Phase 1 data residency objective
Phase 1 launches in the US only, so the data residency objective is:
                                                                                                                                    * Store and process MattBot-controlled data in a US-hosted environment
                                                                                                                                    * Minimize data stored in MattBot systems
                                                                                                                                    * Treat provider processing locations as a disclosed dependency unless explicitly constrained by configuration
27.3.2 Region selection decision for Phase 1 hosting
Phase 1 hosting decision:
                                                                                                                                    * All MattBot infrastructure for production and staging will run in a single US AWS region.
Rationale:
                                                                                                                                    * Aligns with US-only launch
                                                                                                                                    * Simplifies security posture, operations, and incident response
                                                                                                                                    * Reduces cross-region data movement risk
                                                                                                                                    * Minimizes complexity for backups, retention enforcement, and auditing
27.3.3 Separation of production and staging
Production and staging must be separate environments with separate databases and separate secrets.
Requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * No production data is copied into staging unless the user explicitly exports and imports and the system supports it (not required in Phase 1)
                                                                                                                                    * Staging data is treated as sensitive but not trusted for compliance reporting
                                                                                                                                    * Access controls are stricter for production than staging
27.3.4 Provider data residency and disclosure
Phase 1 uses external providers for telephony, voice processing, and push delivery. Data residency and processing location depends on provider capabilities and configuration.
Phase 1 requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Configure providers for US processing or storage where configuration exists and is compatible with Phase 1
                                                                                                                                    * If provider-level US-only guarantees are not configured or not available, disclose this in Privacy Policy as cross-border processing risk
                                                                                                                                    * Regardless of provider behavior, MattBot must:
                                                                                                                                    * store minimal local data
                                                                                                                                    * encrypt sensitive local fields
                                                                                                                                    * remove local references on deletion so the app cannot retrieve provider artifacts after deletion
27.3.5 Data residency and deletion alignment
Deletion must be enforced consistently with the data residency approach:
                                                                                                                                    * Local deletion removes all call record artifacts stored in MattBot databases
                                                                                                                                    * Provider deletion is attempted where supported
                                                                                                                                    * If provider deletion is not supported, MattBot must still:
                                                                                                                                    * remove local reference identifiers that enable access
                                                                                                                                    * prevent future retrieval through MattBot
27.3.6 Future expansion considerations
If the product expands beyond the US, the residency model must evolve to support:
                                                                                                                                    * multi-region deployment options
                                                                                                                                    * region-based user tenancy decisions
                                                                                                                                    * provider selection and configuration that matches regional policy
Phase 1 should not implement multi-region, but it must avoid design choices that make multi-region impossible later.
________________


CHAPTER 28: OBSERVABILITY, LOGGING, AND INCIDENT RESPONSE
28.0 Purpose and scope
This chapter defines how Phase 1 detects problems, explains what went wrong, and resolves incidents safely. Observability in Phase 1 must cover four high-risk zones:
                                                                                                                                    * Telephony routing and call answering correctness
                                                                                                                                    * AI pipeline reliability (transcript, summary, labels)
                                                                                                                                    * Notifications and reminders delivery
                                                                                                                                    * Privacy and security regressions (especially any content leakage)
Phase 1 must produce evidence, not guesses. Every critical user-facing failure must be traceable using a small set of identifiers (call_id, request_id, provider IDs) and measurable signals (rates, latency, queue backlog).
________________


28.1 Sentry self-host setup
28.1.1 Goals for Sentry in Phase 1
Sentry is the primary system for:
                                                                                                                                    * Crash and exception capture (backend, Node bridge, mobile apps)
                                                                                                                                    * Release tracking (which version introduced a regression)
                                                                                                                                    * Performance signals for key transactions (API latency, webhook latency, queue job latency)
                                                                                                                                    * Alert routing for high-severity failures
Sentry must not become a privacy leak. Configuration must prioritize PII control and content scrubbing.
28.1.2 Deployment topology (self-hosted)
Sentry self-host is deployed as a separate internal service stack, isolated from the product runtime.
Minimum requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Dedicated compute isolated from the API runtime, so observability does not degrade call handling during spikes
                                                                                                                                    * Private network access from services to Sentry ingestion endpoint
                                                                                                                                    * Restricted admin access (operator-only)
                                                                                                                                    * Encrypted storage volumes
                                                                                                                                    * Environment separation using tags (dev, prod)
Recommended deployment shape:
                                                                                                                                    * One Sentry host in the same AWS region as the backend
                                                                                                                                    * Sentry runs via Docker Compose with its required dependencies
                                                                                                                                    * Sentry data retention configured to a bounded window suitable for Phase 1 operations
28.1.3 Environment tagging and release tagging
All events must include:
                                                                                                                                    * environment: dev or prod
                                                                                                                                    * service_name: api, node_bridge, worker, admin_portal, mobile_ios, mobile_android
                                                                                                                                    * release: git commit SHA or semantic version (example: api@1.3.7)
                                                                                                                                    * build_time: ISO timestamp (for precise rollout correlation)
Release tagging rules:
                                                                                                                                    * Backend release is set at container start
                                                                                                                                    * Mobile release is set at app build time
                                                                                                                                    * Node bridge release is set at container start
                                                                                                                                    * Admin portal release is set at build and deploy time
28.1.4 PII and sensitive content controls (mandatory)
Sentry configuration must enforce:
                                                                                                                                    * Never store full transcripts in Sentry
                                                                                                                                    * Never store call summaries in Sentry
                                                                                                                                    * Never store outbound message bodies in Sentry
                                                                                                                                    * Never store full phone numbers in Sentry
                                                                                                                                    * Never store auth tokens, refresh tokens, or webhook secrets
Backend-side enforcement:
                                                                                                                                    * Disable request body capture for endpoints that can carry sensitive content
                                                                                                                                    * Scrub headers:
                                                                                                                                    * Authorization
                                                                                                                                    * Cookie
                                                                                                                                    * Any vendor signature headers
                                                                                                                                    * Scrub query strings for endpoints that could include identifiers
Identifier policy:
                                                                                                                                    * call_id is allowed (internal UUID)
                                                                                                                                    * provider IDs (example: Twilio CallSid, MessageSid) are allowed
                                                                                                                                    * user_id should be hashed before being attached as a tag
                                                                                                                                    * device_id is allowed (internal UUID) but must not contain hardware identifiers
Breadcrumb policy:
                                                                                                                                    * Keep breadcrumbs enabled for debugging but strip any message content and redact phone-like patterns.
                                                                                                                                    * Breadcrumbs for “text draft created” must never include the draft text.
28.1.5 Backend instrumentation (Python FastAPI)
Instrumentation requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Sentry SDK integrated at ASGI level
                                                                                                                                    * Capture:
                                                                                                                                    * unhandled exceptions
                                                                                                                                    * HTTP 5xx responses with error context
                                                                                                                                    * webhook validation failures (as structured events, not raw payload dumps)
                                                                                                                                    * Performance transactions:
                                                                                                                                    * /webhooks/twilio/voice inbound handler
                                                                                                                                    * /webhooks/elevenlabs events handler
                                                                                                                                    * call record creation endpoint
                                                                                                                                    * message approval endpoints
                                                                                                                                    * push notification dispatch
                                                                                                                                    * retention deletion job execution
Error enrichment fields (all safe):
                                                                                                                                    * request_id
                                                                                                                                    * call_id (if available)
                                                                                                                                    * provider_call_sid (if available)
                                                                                                                                    * job_type and job_id for workers
                                                                                                                                    * retry_count (for webhook and job retries)
                                                                                                                                    * safe failure code (example: TWILIO_SIGNATURE_INVALID, E11_TRANSCRIPT_TIMEOUT)
28.1.6 Node bridge instrumentation (realtime media service)
Node bridge is high-risk because it touches realtime call streams and vendor connectivity.
Instrumentation requirements:
                                                                                                                                    * Capture all unhandled promise rejections
                                                                                                                                    * Capture websocket lifecycle failures:
                                                                                                                                    * connect failures
                                                                                                                                    * unexpected close
                                                                                                                                    * reconnect loops
                                                                                                                                    * Track:
                                                                                                                                    * stream start latency
                                                                                                                                    * stream duration
                                                                                                                                    * vendor disconnect reason classification
                                                                                                                                    * Enrich events with:
                                                                                                                                    * call_id
                                                                                                                                    * provider_call_sid
                                                                                                                                    * stream_session_id
Strict logging rule:
                                                                                                                                    * Never log audio payloads, transcript text, or raw websocket message bodies.
28.1.7 Mobile app instrumentation (React Native)
Mobile instrumentation must cover:
                                                                                                                                    * Crash capture and ANR-like issues (app freezes) if supported
                                                                                                                                    * Screen-level performance for:
                                                                                                                                    * call list load
                                                                                                                                    * call detail load
                                                                                                                                    * transcript view unlock flow
                                                                                                                                    * approval flow
                                                                                                                                    * Network errors:
                                                                                                                                    * auth failures
                                                                                                                                    * sync conflicts (settings revision mismatches)
                                                                                                                                    * push token registration failures
PII rules on mobile:
                                                                                                                                    * Do not attach caller phone numbers to Sentry
                                                                                                                                    * Do not attach transcript content or summary content
                                                                                                                                    * If showing call_id, only show internal call_id
28.1.8 Sentry retention and volume control
Phase 1 must control event volume so Sentry remains usable and does not flood storage.
Controls:
                                                                                                                                    * Sampling:
                                                                                                                                    * 100 percent sampling for errors
                                                                                                                                    * limited sampling for performance events (example: 10 to 20 percent)
                                                                                                                                    * Rate limiting:
                                                                                                                                    * cap repeated identical errors per minute per service
                                                                                                                                    * Grouping:
                                                                                                                                    * group by normalized error code plus route, not by random message text
________________


28.2 Metrics catalog
28.2.1 Metrics goals
Metrics answer three questions:
                                                                                                                                    1. Is the system working right now
                                                                                                                                    2. Is it getting worse over time
                                                                                                                                    3. What changed when it got worse
Metrics must be defined with:
                                                                                                                                    * name and type (counter, gauge, histogram)
                                                                                                                                    * tags (environment, service, route, provider, job_type)
                                                                                                                                    * expected range and failure threshold
                                                                                                                                    * owner and alert policy (if critical)
28.2.2 Collection approach in Phase 1
Phase 1 uses a two-layer approach:
A) Infrastructure metrics (AWS-native)
                                                                                                                                    * ALB request count and 5xx rate
                                                                                                                                    * EC2 CPU, memory (via agent or host metrics), disk usage
                                                                                                                                    * RDS CPU, connections, storage, read latency, write latency
                                                                                                                                    * SQS queue depth and oldest message age
B) Application metrics (emitted by services)
                                                                                                                                    * Emit structured metrics events from:
                                                                                                                                    * API service
                                                                                                                                    * worker service
                                                                                                                                    * Node bridge
                                                                                                                                    * Aggregate into dashboards and alert conditions.
Key rule: application metrics must not include sensitive text fields.
28.2.3 Core product metrics (calls)
Counters:
                                                                                                                                    * calls_inbound_total
                                                                                                                                    * calls_answered_total
                                                                                                                                    * calls_completed_total
                                                                                                                                    * calls_failed_total
Rates:
                                                                                                                                    * call_answer_rate = calls_answered_total / calls_inbound_total
                                                                                                                                    * call_completion_rate = calls_completed_total / calls_answered_total
                                                                                                                                    * call_failure_rate = calls_failed_total / calls_answered_total
Dimensions (tags):
                                                                                                                                    * mode: dedicated_number or forwarded_fallback
                                                                                                                                    * end_reason:
                                                                                                                                    * completed_normal
                                                                                                                                    * ended_by_policy
                                                                                                                                    * ended_by_caller
                                                                                                                                    * vendor_failure
                                                                                                                                    * internal_error
                                                                                                                                    * blocked
                                                                                                                                    * caller_type:
                                                                                                                                    * vip
                                                                                                                                    * unknown
                                                                                                                                    * blocked
Histograms:
                                                                                                                                    * call_answer_latency_seconds (from inbound webhook to assistant speaking)
                                                                                                                                    * call_duration_seconds
28.2.4 AI pipeline metrics (transcript, summary, labels)
Counters:
                                                                                                                                    * transcript_requested_total
                                                                                                                                    * transcript_ready_total
                                                                                                                                    * transcript_failed_total
                                                                                                                                    * summary_requested_total
                                                                                                                                    * summary_ready_total
                                                                                                                                    * summary_failed_total
                                                                                                                                    * labeling_completed_total
                                                                                                                                    * labeling_failed_total
Rates:
                                                                                                                                    * transcript_success_rate = transcript_ready_total / transcript_requested_total
                                                                                                                                    * summary_success_rate = summary_ready_total / summary_requested_total
Histograms:
                                                                                                                                    * transcript_time_to_ready_seconds (call_end to transcript_ready)
                                                                                                                                    * summary_time_to_ready_seconds (call_end to summary_ready)
Failure reason classification (tag):
                                                                                                                                    * provider_timeout
                                                                                                                                    * provider_error
                                                                                                                                    * internal_processing_error
                                                                                                                                    * malformed_payload
                                                                                                                                    * retry_exhausted
28.2.5 Live handoff metrics
Counters:
                                                                                                                                    * handoff_offered_total
                                                                                                                                    * handoff_accepted_total
                                                                                                                                    * handoff_declined_total
                                                                                                                                    * handoff_timed_out_total
                                                                                                                                    * handoff_failed_total
                                                                                                                                    * handoff_loop_prevented_total
Rates:
                                                                                                                                    * handoff_accept_rate = handoff_accepted_total / handoff_offered_total
                                                                                                                                    * handoff_success_rate = (handoff_accepted_total - handoff_failed_total) / handoff_accepted_total
Histogram:
                                                                                                                                    * handoff_time_to_accept_seconds (offer to accept)
Arbitration metrics:
                                                                                                                                    * handoff_lock_contended_total
                                                                                                                                    * handoff_lock_acquired_total
28.2.6 Notifications and reminders metrics
Counters:
                                                                                                                                    * push_sent_total
                                                                                                                                    * push_failed_total
                                                                                                                                    * push_delivered_total (if delivery receipt is supported)
                                                                                                                                    * push_suppressed_quiet_hours_total
                                                                                                                                    * push_privacy_private_total
                                                                                                                                    * push_privacy_preview_total
Reminder metrics:
                                                                                                                                    * reminders_created_total
                                                                                                                                    * reminders_triggered_total
                                                                                                                                    * reminders_failed_total
                                                                                                                                    * reminder_deduped_total
Histogram:
                                                                                                                                    * push_dispatch_latency_seconds (event created to push send attempt)
28.2.7 Messaging and approval metrics
Counters:
                                                                                                                                    * message_draft_created_total
                                                                                                                                    * message_edited_total
                                                                                                                                    * message_approved_total
                                                                                                                                    * message_send_attempted_total
                                                                                                                                    * message_sent_total
                                                                                                                                    * message_failed_total
                                                                                                                                    * message_blocked_opt_out_total
                                                                                                                                    * message_blocked_policy_total
Rates:
                                                                                                                                    * approval_to_send_conversion = message_sent_total / message_approved_total
                                                                                                                                    * send_failure_rate = message_failed_total / message_send_attempted_total
28.2.8 Retention and deletion metrics
Counters:
                                                                                                                                    * retention_sweeper_runs_total
                                                                                                                                    * retention_candidates_found_total
                                                                                                                                    * deletion_jobs_enqueued_total
                                                                                                                                    * deletion_jobs_completed_total
                                                                                                                                    * deletion_jobs_failed_total
Histograms:
                                                                                                                                    * deletion_job_duration_seconds
                                                                                                                                    * time_from_deleted_at_to_purged_seconds
Critical correctness signal:
                                                                                                                                    * deleted_call_access_attempt_total (should be near zero; any spike indicates a possible authorization or caching issue)
28.2.9 Abuse and cost protection metrics
Counters:
                                                                                                                                    * rate_limit_triggered_total (tag by endpoint and dimension: ip, user, caller)
                                                                                                                                    * blocked_call_attempt_total
                                                                                                                                    * spam_suspected_total
                                                                                                                                    * circuit_breaker_triggered_total (tag by breaker type)
Cost driver tracking (per day, per user where possible):
                                                                                                                                    * total_call_minutes_handled
                                                                                                                                    * total_ai_processing_minutes
                                                                                                                                    * total_sms_sent
These metrics feed cost spike detection and rapid mitigation actions.
________________


28.3 Alerting rules
