28.3.1 Alerting goals and policy
Alerts must be:
                                                                                                                                    * Actionable: every alert includes what to check first
                                                                                                                                    * Owned: each alert has an assigned owner role
                                                                                                                                    * Low-noise: no success or informational alerts, only failures or risk thresholds
                                                                                                                                    * Fast: detect severe problems within minutes
                                                                                                                                    * Safe: include identifiers and counts, not sensitive content
28.3.2 Severity levels
Define four severities:
SEV0 Critical
                                                                                                                                    * Privacy leakage risk, cross-user access risk, system-wide call failure, or security breach indicators
SEV1 High
                                                                                                                                    * Major feature outage or widespread failures (transcripts down, summaries down, handoff broken)
SEV2 Medium
                                                                                                                                    * Partial degradation (elevated latency, increased failure rate, queue backlog growing)
SEV3 Low
                                                                                                                                    * Minor issues needing attention (sporadic provider errors, small regression)
28.3.3 Alert delivery targets
Phase 1 requires a clear destination:
                                                                                                                                    * SEV0 and SEV1 go to the on-call owner immediately (primary channel plus backup)
                                                                                                                                    * SEV2 goes to the engineering owner during working hours with escalation if it persists
                                                                                                                                    * SEV3 is aggregated into a daily review queue
The exact channels are implementation-defined, but they must be reliable and tested in dev before production rollout.
28.3.4 Core alert conditions (minimum set)
Call handling alerts:
                                                                                                                                    * Call failure spike:
                                                                                                                                    * call_failure_rate exceeds threshold over a short window
                                                                                                                                    * Calls not being answered:
                                                                                                                                    * calls_inbound_total rising while calls_answered_total flat
                                                                                                                                    * Answer latency regression:
                                                                                                                                    * call_answer_latency_seconds p95 above threshold
AI pipeline alerts:
                                                                                                                                    * Transcript failure spike:
                                                                                                                                    * transcript_success_rate below threshold
                                                                                                                                    * Summary failure spike:
                                                                                                                                    * summary_success_rate below threshold
                                                                                                                                    * Processing delay:
                                                                                                                                    * transcript_time_to_ready_seconds p95 above threshold
                                                                                                                                    * summary_time_to_ready_seconds p95 above threshold
Notification alerts:
                                                                                                                                    * Push failure spike:
                                                                                                                                    * push_failed_total / push_sent_total above threshold
                                                                                                                                    * Reminder trigger failures:
                                                                                                                                    * reminders_failed_total above threshold
Retention and deletion alerts:
                                                                                                                                    * Deletion job failures:
                                                                                                                                    * deletion_jobs_failed_total above threshold
                                                                                                                                    * Retention sweeper not running:
                                                                                                                                    * retention_sweeper_runs_total missing for expected interval
Security and privacy alerts (highest priority):
                                                                                                                                    * Any signal that private content could leak:
                                                                                                                                    * notification payload sanitizer error
                                                                                                                                    * forbidden field detected in push template build
                                                                                                                                    * Unauthorized access patterns:
                                                                                                                                    * repeated 403 spikes on call detail endpoints
                                                                                                                                    * deleted_call_access_attempt_total spike
Cost and abuse alerts:
                                                                                                                                    * Circuit breaker triggered:
                                                                                                                                    * circuit_breaker_triggered_total increases unexpectedly
                                                                                                                                    * Rate limit spikes:
                                                                                                                                    * rate_limit_triggered_total exceeds threshold
                                                                                                                                    * Cost driver spike:
                                                                                                                                    * daily minutes or SMS volume exceeds expected envelope
28.3.5 Alert payload requirements
Every alert message must include:
                                                                                                                                    * environment
                                                                                                                                    * service_name
                                                                                                                                    * time window and measured value
                                                                                                                                    * a short classification code (example: ALERT_CALL_FAILURE_SPIKE)
                                                                                                                                    * the top 3 likely causes
                                                                                                                                    * the first 3 checks to run
                                                                                                                                    * the mitigation switch recommended first (feature flag or throttle)
                                                                                                                                    * correlation pointers:
                                                                                                                                    * one or more request_ids
                                                                                                                                    * call_ids
                                                                                                                                    * provider IDs when relevant
No alert may include:
                                                                                                                                    * transcript text
                                                                                                                                    * summary text
                                                                                                                                    * full phone numbers
                                                                                                                                    * message bodies
________________


28.4 Runbooks
28.4.1 Incident response process (standard)
All incidents follow the same sequence:
                                                                                                                                    1. Confirm scope
                                                                                                                                    * how many users affected
                                                                                                                                    * which environment
                                                                                                                                    * which call modes (dedicated vs forwarded)
                                                                                                                                    2. Freeze change
                                                                                                                                    * pause onboarding new users if needed
                                                                                                                                    * stop deployments until stabilized
                                                                                                                                    3. Apply quickest safe mitigation
Examples of fast mitigations:
                                                                                                                                       * disable live handoff
                                                                                                                                       * force private notification mode
                                                                                                                                       * tighten rate limits
                                                                                                                                       * reduce max call length
                                                                                                                                       * disable high-cost paths temporarily
                                                                                                                                       4. Verify improvement
                                                                                                                                       * metrics returning toward normal ranges
                                                                                                                                       * error rate dropping
                                                                                                                                       * queue backlog shrinking
                                                                                                                                       5. Restore and monitor
                                                                                                                                       * re-enable features gradually
                                                                                                                                       * monitor for at least one full cycle of peak usage
                                                                                                                                       6. Document timeline and actions
                                                                                                                                       * start time, detection signal, changes made, recovery time
28.4.2 Runbook: Twilio outage or call routing failure
Symptoms:
                                                                                                                                       * calls_inbound_total increases but calls_answered_total stalls
                                                                                                                                       * webhook 5xx spikes
                                                                                                                                       * provider errors in call state transitions
                                                                                                                                       * sudden increases in vendor_failure end_reason
Immediate checks:
                                                                                                                                       1. Confirm webhook validation success rate (signature failures vs real provider outage)
                                                                                                                                       2. Check ALB 5xx and API error rate for webhook routes
                                                                                                                                       3. Check provider call status callbacks arriving or not
Mitigation steps:
                                                                                                                                       * If signature failures:
                                                                                                                                       * confirm correct public URL reconstruction behind ALB
                                                                                                                                       * confirm no proxy header regression
                                                                                                                                       * If provider outage suspected:
                                                                                                                                       * enable degraded call handling mode:
                                                                                                                                       * short “message-only” flow
                                                                                                                                       * reduce call length limits to reduce cost
                                                                                                                                       * disable live handoff to remove dependency risk
                                                                                                                                       * pause onboarding if impact is broad
Recovery:
                                                                                                                                       * Once provider stabilizes, re-enable normal screening and handoff gradually
                                                                                                                                       * Keep call length reduced until stable for a defined observation window
Post-incident:
                                                                                                                                       * Identify which portion failed:
                                                                                                                                       * inbound webhook
                                                                                                                                       * call connect
                                                                                                                                       * status callback
                                                                                                                                       * Add a targeted alert if it was not already covered
28.4.3 Runbook: ElevenLabs outage or transcript/summary collapse
Symptoms:
                                                                                                                                       * transcript_success_rate drops below threshold
                                                                                                                                       * summary_success_rate drops below threshold
                                                                                                                                       * time_to_ready p95 increases sharply
                                                                                                                                       * worker retries climbing
Immediate checks:
                                                                                                                                       1. Check provider webhook ingestion health and signature validation
                                                                                                                                       2. Check queue backlog for transcript and summary jobs
                                                                                                                                       3. Check if failures are timeouts vs hard errors
Mitigation steps:
                                                                                                                                       * Switch the AI pipeline into degraded mode:
                                                                                                                                       * still create call records immediately
                                                                                                                                       * mark transcript and summary as delayed
                                                                                                                                       * schedule controlled retries with capped concurrency
                                                                                                                                       * Reduce concurrency of AI jobs if provider is throttling
                                                                                                                                       * Disable optional labeling or advanced extraction if it depends on the failing component
User-facing behavior during mitigation:
                                                                                                                                       * Call detail shows “processing delayed” rather than blank content
                                                                                                                                       * Notifications do not claim summary is ready if it is not
Recovery:
                                                                                                                                       * Restore normal job concurrency gradually
                                                                                                                                       * Clear backlog in priority order:
                                                                                                                                       1. transcripts
                                                                                                                                       2. summaries
                                                                                                                                       3. labels
                                                                                                                                       * Keep alert thresholds temporarily stricter during recovery window
28.4.4 Runbook: Queue backlog and worker saturation
Symptoms:
                                                                                                                                       * SQS queue depth rising
                                                                                                                                       * oldest message age rising
                                                                                                                                       * job latency increasing
                                                                                                                                       * retries increasing
Immediate checks:
                                                                                                                                       1. Verify worker processes are alive and consuming
                                                                                                                                       2. Check EC2 CPU and memory of worker host
                                                                                                                                       3. Check DB connections and slow query signals
Mitigation steps:
                                                                                                                                       * Temporarily pause non-critical job types:
                                                                                                                                       * retention sweeper
                                                                                                                                       * low-priority labeling
                                                                                                                                       * Reduce expensive work per job:
                                                                                                                                       * shorten summary output targets
                                                                                                                                       * disable optional evidence snippets
                                                                                                                                       * Increase worker capacity:
                                                                                                                                       * increase worker concurrency if CPU allows
                                                                                                                                       * add an additional worker instance if needed
                                                                                                                                       * Ensure idempotency protections are not causing hot loops
Recovery:
                                                                                                                                       * Once backlog starts shrinking, restore paused jobs
                                                                                                                                       * Verify no duplicate call artifacts were created during retries
28.4.5 Runbook: Database pressure and degraded performance
Symptoms:
                                                                                                                                       * API latency rising across endpoints
                                                                                                                                       * RDS CPU high
                                                                                                                                       * connection count high
                                                                                                                                       * timeouts on call detail or list endpoints
Immediate checks:
                                                                                                                                       1. Identify top slow queries (by endpoint and timing)
                                                                                                                                       2. Check connection pool limits and leaks
                                                                                                                                       3. Check if a background job is causing high write load
Mitigation steps:
                                                                                                                                       * Apply feature-level load shedding:
                                                                                                                                       * reduce call detail payload size
                                                                                                                                       * delay non-critical writes
                                                                                                                                       * pause retention purge temporarily if DB is at risk
                                                                                                                                       * Reduce job concurrency for write-heavy workers
                                                                                                                                       * Restart unhealthy worker processes if they are leaking connections
Recovery:
                                                                                                                                       * Validate p95 API latency returns under target
                                                                                                                                       * Validate call record creation remains stable
                                                                                                                                       * Re-enable paused jobs with a controlled ramp
28.4.6 Runbook: Cost spike or spam flood
Symptoms:
                                                                                                                                       * total_call_minutes_handled spikes
                                                                                                                                       * rate_limit_triggered_total spikes
                                                                                                                                       * blocked_call_attempt_total rises
                                                                                                                                       * circuit_breaker_triggered_total activates
Immediate checks:
                                                                                                                                       1. Identify whether spike is:
                                                                                                                                       * few users with extreme volume
                                                                                                                                       * broad spam wave across many users
                                                                                                                                       2. Identify top caller patterns (hashed identifiers, not raw numbers)
Mitigation steps:
                                                                                                                                       * Tighten rate limits temporarily:
                                                                                                                                       * per caller
                                                                                                                                       * per user
                                                                                                                                       * per IP at edge
                                                                                                                                       * Reduce max call length globally for a period
                                                                                                                                       * Force message-only mode for non-VIP callers
                                                                                                                                       * Disable live handoff to avoid repeated routing loops under attack
Recovery:
                                                                                                                                       * Gradually relax limits when volume returns to normal
                                                                                                                                       * Review block suggestion rules and spam heuristics for tuning
________________


28.5 Completion checklist for this chapter
This chapter is complete when:
                                                                                                                                       * Sentry self-host is deployed, secured, and tagged by environment and release
                                                                                                                                       * PII scrubbing is enforced and verified with test events
                                                                                                                                       * Metrics catalog is implemented with dashboards for calls, AI pipeline, handoff, notifications, deletion, abuse, and cost drivers
                                                                                                                                       * Alerts exist for call failures, transcript/summary failures, notification failures, deletion failures, abuse, and cost spikes, with owners and severities
                                                                                                                                       * Runbooks exist and can be executed using only metrics, Sentry events, and safe identifiers (call_id, request_id, provider IDs)
                                                                                                                                       * An incident response process exists with clear steps for freeze, mitigate, verify, recover, and document
________________


CHAPTER 29: PERFORMANCE, CAPACITY, AND LOAD PLANNING
29.0 Purpose of this chapter
This chapter defines how Phase 1 will meet real-world performance expectations, what load the system is designed to handle at launch, and how we will prove it with testing. The design must remain reliable under bursty inbound calls, variable network conditions, and third-party provider latency.
Phase 1 constraints that shape performance planning:
                                                                                                                                       * Initial user base is small (early launch), but load is spiky and unpredictable
                                                                                                                                       * Real-time voice flows are latency-sensitive and must not be blocked by slow post-call processing
                                                                                                                                       * The platform depends on external providers for telephony, voice, and transcript generation
                                                                                                                                       * The system uses Docker on a VM and RDS PostgreSQL, with SQS for background processing
                                                                                                                                       * Strong privacy defaults mean we cannot rely on verbose logs for debugging, so metrics and trace IDs must be first-class
________________


29.1 Assumed load profile
29.1.1 User and device envelope
Phase 1 capacity planning is built around a small initial launch population, with multi-device enabled.
                                                                                                                                       * Active users: 50 to 100
                                                                                                                                       * Devices per user:
                                                                                                                                       * Typical: 1 to 2 devices signed in
                                                                                                                                       * Upper planning bound: 3 devices (phone plus tablet plus spare phone)
                                                                                                                                       * Push tokens per user: 1 to 3 (one per device)
This matters because notifications, settings sync, and call list refresh multiply by device count.
29.1.2 Call volume scenarios
Actual usage is uncertain at launch, so Phase 1 plans for an envelope of scenarios. The system must handle the high scenario without redesign.
Planning scenarios (per active user per day):
                                                                                                                                       * Low: 1 to 2 inbound calls answered by the assistant
                                                                                                                                       * Typical: 3 to 6 inbound calls answered by the assistant
                                                                                                                                       * High: 8 to 12 inbound calls answered by the assistant
Assumptions that apply across scenarios:
                                                                                                                                       * Calls are clustered around typical daily peaks (morning, lunch, late afternoon)
                                                                                                                                       * A subset of calls are forwarded calls, and a subset are to a dedicated AI number
                                                                                                                                       * A meaningful fraction of calls end quickly (caller hangs up, wrong number, spam), which increases webhook event rate even if minutes are low
29.1.3 Call duration assumptions
Duration impacts cost and compute.
                                                                                                                                       * Non-VIP calls:
                                                                                                                                       * Typical: 60 to 180 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * Upper planning bound: hard stop at configured max call length
                                                                                                                                       * VIP calls:
                                                                                                                                       * Typical: 120 to 300 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * Upper planning bound: hard stop at VIP max call length
Planning rule: capacity is based on concurrent calls, not total minutes, because real-time audio streaming and call orchestration are the limiting factors.
29.1.4 Concurrency assumptions and safety headroom
We plan for bursty concurrency, not averages.
Definitions:
                                                                                                                                       * Concurrent calls means calls simultaneously in active conversation (not just ringing).
                                                                                                                                       * Burst means a short window (1 to 5 minutes) where concurrency spikes.
Phase 1 concurrency envelope across all users:
                                                                                                                                       * Typical concurrency: 1 to 3 simultaneous calls
                                                                                                                                       * Burst concurrency: 4 to 8 simultaneous calls
                                                                                                                                       * Safety headroom target: handle 2x burst without total outage, by degrading gracefully
Degradation approach if concurrency exceeds safe limits:
                                                                                                                                       * Keep call answering and basic screening working
                                                                                                                                       * Temporarily disable or delay non-critical work:
                                                                                                                                       * advanced labeling
                                                                                                                                       * evidence snippet extraction
                                                                                                                                       * low-priority notification types
                                                                                                                                       * Reduce per-call compute cost:
                                                                                                                                       * shorten responses
                                                                                                                                       * enforce shorter call length policy for non-VIP calls during overload
                                                                                                                                       * Preserve the call record and mark post-call artifacts as processing delayed
29.1.5 Background job volume assumptions
Background jobs are driven by call completion and user actions.
Per call, expected jobs:
                                                                                                                                       * Call record finalize job (always)
                                                                                                                                       * Transcript and summary pipeline jobs (always, but may be delayed)
                                                                                                                                       * Notification dispatch jobs (1 to 3 notifications depending on important status and handoff events)
                                                                                                                                       * Optional text draft generation job (if enabled)
                                                                                                                                       * Reminder scheduling job only when user creates reminders
Envelope planning:
                                                                                                                                       * Typical: 3 to 6 background jobs per call
                                                                                                                                       * High: 8 to 12 jobs per call (if multiple artifacts, retries, and notifications occur)
Queue design requirement: a surge of post-call jobs must not delay real-time call handling.
29.1.6 Storage and database growth assumptions
Call record storage grows with call count.
Per call, Phase 1 stores:
                                                                                                                                       * Minimal metadata
                                                                                                                                       * Summary and extracted fields
                                                                                                                                       * Labels and label reasons
                                                                                                                                       * Action records (text back) and reminder records when used
                                                                                                                                       * Provider references for transcript and recording, without storing large media blobs in MattBot storage
Planning bounds:
                                                                                                                                       * Daily call records at high scenario:
                                                                                                                                       * 100 users x 12 calls = 1,200 calls/day
                                                                                                                                       * Monthly call records at high scenario:
                                                                                                                                       * 1,200 calls/day x 30 days = 36,000 calls/month
This is a manageable count for PostgreSQL on a small instance if queries are indexed and payloads are encrypted but bounded.
29.1.7 Performance-critical paths
These paths must remain fast under load:
                                                                                                                                       * Inbound call start and stream setup
                                                                                                                                       * Real-time conversational turns
                                                                                                                                       * Handoff offer creation and delivery
                                                                                                                                       * Handoff accept and call connect
                                                                                                                                       * Call list and call detail retrieval in the mobile app
All other work must yield to these paths.
________________


29.2 Latency targets
Latency targets are defined using percentiles, not averages, because user experience is driven by worst-case behavior.
29.2.1 Inbound answer and greeting targets
Goal: the caller should not experience long silence.
Definitions:
                                                                                                                                       * Time-to-answer: from inbound call event received by the system to the assistant speaking the first greeting line
                                                                                                                                       * Time-to-first-turn: from the caller finishing their first utterance to assistant starting its response
Targets:
                                                                                                                                       * Time-to-answer:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 2.5 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 5.0 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * hard ceiling: 8.0 seconds before fallback behavior is triggered
                                                                                                                                       * Time-to-first-turn:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 1.2 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 2.5 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * hard ceiling: 4.0 seconds before fallback behavior is triggered
Fallback behavior when ceilings are exceeded:
                                                                                                                                       * The assistant speaks a short “one moment” style line and continues
                                                                                                                                       * If streaming is unstable or provider connectivity is failing, the call ends with a polite fallback message rather than silence
29.2.2 Conversational turn latency targets
Conversational turns repeat many times per call, so stability matters more than occasional speed.
Targets per turn:
                                                                                                                                       * End-of-speech detection to assistant speech start:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 1.2 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 2.5 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * No more than 1 in 20 turns should exceed 3.0 seconds during normal operation
If turn latency repeatedly exceeds targets:
                                                                                                                                       * Reduce response verbosity dynamically
                                                                                                                                       * Reduce tool or extraction work during the call
                                                                                                                                       * Prefer short clarifying questions rather than multi-sentence replies
29.2.3 Live handoff latency targets
Handoff is time-sensitive, with a short accept window.
Definitions:
                                                                                                                                       * Offer-to-notification: time from handoff offer creation to push dispatched
                                                                                                                                       * Accept-to-connect: time from user tapping accept to call being bridged
Targets:
                                                                                                                                       * Offer-to-notification:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 1.0 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 3.0 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * Accept-to-connect:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 1.5 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 3.5 seconds
If these targets cannot be met due to provider errors:
                                                                                                                                       * Mark handoff as failed and continue conversation without silence
                                                                                                                                       * Do not retry the same handoff endlessly; one offer per call attempt only
29.2.4 Post-call processing latency targets
Post-call artifacts must appear quickly enough for user trust, but they are allowed to be delayed under load.
Definitions:
                                                                                                                                       * Transcript time-to-ready: from call end to transcript ready state
                                                                                                                                       * Summary time-to-ready: from call end to summary ready state
                                                                                                                                       * Labeling time-to-ready: from call end to labels ready state
Targets:
                                                                                                                                       * Transcript time-to-ready:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 60 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 4 minutes
                                                                                                                                       * Summary time-to-ready:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 90 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 5 minutes
                                                                                                                                       * Labels time-to-ready:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 2 minutes
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 6 minutes
Degradation rules:
                                                                                                                                       * If summary is not ready, the call record still appears immediately with “processing” state
                                                                                                                                       * Retries are controlled and bounded so backlogs do not grow without limit
29.2.5 Mobile app API latency targets
The app must feel responsive even on weak networks.
Targets for API responses (server-side time, excluding mobile network):
                                                                                                                                       * Call list endpoint:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 150 ms
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 400 ms
                                                                                                                                       * Call detail endpoint:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 200 ms
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 600 ms
                                                                                                                                       * Settings fetch:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 150 ms
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 400 ms
                                                                                                                                       * Approve and send text:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 250 ms
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 800 ms
API payload sizing constraints:
                                                                                                                                       * Paginate call list
                                                                                                                                       * Call detail returns only what is needed for the current screen
                                                                                                                                       * Transcript content is fetched only when user requests it and passes biometric gating
29.2.6 Notification latency targets
Notifications must be timely but privacy-safe.
Targets:
                                                                                                                                       * Important call notification dispatch:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 2 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 6 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * Reminder due dispatch:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: 2 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * p95: 6 seconds
                                                                                                                                       * Quiet hours suppression decision:
                                                                                                                                       * applied deterministically server-side, no more than 100 ms overhead
29.2.7 Database performance targets
Targets are defined to protect interactive flows.
                                                                                                                                       * RDS query latency:
                                                                                                                                       * p50: under 20 ms for indexed lookups
                                                                                                                                       * p95: under 80 ms for the call list and call detail queries
                                                                                                                                       * Connection stability:
                                                                                                                                       * no sustained connection saturation
                                                                                                                                       * connection pool must cap and backpressure rather than collapse
Database design requirements to meet targets:
                                                                                                                                       * Index on owner_user_id plus call_ended_at for call list
                                                                                                                                       * Index on call_id plus owner_user_id for call detail
                                                                                                                                       * Index on status fields used by background workers (pending, scheduled, processing)
                                                                                                                                       * Avoid unbounded text searches in Phase 1 interactive endpoints
29.2.8 Service-level reliability targets tied to performance
Performance and reliability are coupled.
Phase 1 service targets:
                                                                                                                                       * Answer success rate target: high and measurable
                                                                                                                                       * Transcript success rate target: measurable and alertable
                                                                                                                                       * Summary success rate target: measurable and alertable
                                                                                                                                       * Clear degraded mode behavior rather than silent failures
These targets are enforced through metrics and alert thresholds defined in the observability chapter.
________________


29.3 Load testing plan
29.3.1 Goals of load testing in Phase 1
Load testing is used to prove:
                                                                                                                                       * The system can sustain expected bursts without dropping calls
                                                                                                                                       * Latency targets are met for the critical paths
                                                                                                                                       * Background processing does not starve real-time call handling
                                                                                                                                       * Rate limits and circuit breakers behave correctly under abuse-like traffic
                                                                                                                                       * The system degrades gracefully and recovers automatically
29.3.2 Test environments
Phase 1 has dev and production only, so performance testing must use one of these approaches:
Option A: Dedicated temporary performance environment
                                                                                                                                       * Clone production-like infrastructure with separate secrets and a separate database
                                                                                                                                       * Run tests and destroy environment after validation
                                                                                                                                       * This is preferred for realistic results without risking production stability
Option B: Dev environment performance window
                                                                                                                                       * Run controlled tests in dev with strict time windows and cost caps
                                                                                                                                       * This is acceptable if a dedicated environment is not feasible
Rule: never run high-concurrency telephony tests in production.
29.3.3 Tooling strategy
Different components require different test tools.
API load testing:
                                                                                                                                       * Use an HTTP load testing tool to simulate:
                                                                                                                                       * onboarding and auth flows
                                                                                                                                       * call list and call detail refresh
                                                                                                                                       * settings reads and updates
                                                                                                                                       * reminder creation and completion
                                                                                                                                       * message draft creation and approval endpoints
                                                                                                                                       * Capture:
                                                                                                                                       * p50, p95, p99 latency
                                                                                                                                       * error rate by endpoint
                                                                                                                                       * throughput (requests per second)
Queue and worker testing:
                                                                                                                                       * Create synthetic batches of jobs that mimic post-call workload:
                                                                                                                                       * transcript-ready events
                                                                                                                                       * summary pipeline jobs
                                                                                                                                       * notification dispatch jobs
                                                                                                                                       * deletion jobs
                                                                                                                                       * Validate:
                                                                                                                                       * queue depth behavior
                                                                                                                                       * oldest message age
                                                                                                                                       * worker throughput and retry behavior
Real-time call path testing:
                                                                                                                                       * Use a call simulation harness that can trigger inbound call flows, including:
                                                                                                                                       * dedicated AI number path
                                                                                                                                       * forwarded fallback path (where feasible)
                                                                                                                                       * Where full audio simulation is expensive, use a controlled “mock audio” mode:
                                                                                                                                       * same orchestration and websocket lifecycle
                                                                                                                                       * but simplified audio payload generation
                                                                                                                                       * Validate:
                                                                                                                                       * time-to-answer
                                                                                                                                       * turn latency distribution
                                                                                                                                       * disconnect behavior
                                                                                                                                       * handoff offer and accept path
29.3.4 Scenario matrix
Phase 1 must test at least these scenarios.
A) Baseline functional performance
                                                                                                                                       * 1 concurrent call, normal network
                                                                                                                                       * Verify latency targets are met and call records created reliably
B) Burst calls
                                                                                                                                       * Burst to 4 concurrent calls for 2 minutes
                                                                                                                                       * Burst to 8 concurrent calls for 2 minutes
                                                                                                                                       * Validate:
                                                                                                                                       * call acceptance
                                                                                                                                       * no dead air
                                                                                                                                       * no call record loss
                                                                                                                                       * graceful degradation rules activate if needed
C) Sustained mixed workload
                                                                                                                                       * 3 concurrent calls sustained for 30 minutes
                                                                                                                                       * With background processing enabled
                                                                                                                                       * Validate:
                                                                                                                                       * no queue runaway
                                                                                                                                       * stable memory usage
                                                                                                                                       * stable DB connections
D) Handoff stress
                                                                                                                                       * Repeated handoff offers and accepts under load
                                                                                                                                       * Validate:
                                                                                                                                       * offer-to-notification latency
                                                                                                                                       * accept-to-connect latency
                                                                                                                                       * lock and dedupe logic prevents repeated offers
E) Notifications burst
                                                                                                                                       * Simulate many call events at once that trigger notifications
                                                                                                                                       * Validate:
                                                                                                                                       * dispatch throughput
                                                                                                                                       * quiet hours suppression logic
                                                                                                                                       * privacy mode enforcement
F) Deletion and retention load
                                                                                                                                       * Generate a large number of deletion jobs
                                                                                                                                       * Validate:
                                                                                                                                       * deletion job throughput
                                                                                                                                       * DB performance impact
                                                                                                                                       * deleted records cannot be accessed
G) Failure injection
Simulate:
                                                                                                                                       * provider timeout spikes
                                                                                                                                       * webhook replay attempts
                                                                                                                                       * queue worker restarts
                                                                                                                                       * DB slowdowns
Validate:
                                                                                                                                       * degraded mode triggers
                                                                                                                                       * retries are bounded
                                                                                                                                       * correctness is maintained
29.3.5 Measurements and dashboards
For every test run, record:
Critical path metrics:
                                                                                                                                          * time-to-answer p50 and p95
                                                                                                                                          * turn latency p50 and p95
                                                                                                                                          * handoff offer-to-notification p50 and p95
                                                                                                                                          * accept-to-connect p50 and p95
System health metrics:
                                                                                                                                          * API 5xx rate
                                                                                                                                          * webhook validation failure rate
                                                                                                                                          * worker job success rate
                                                                                                                                          * queue depth and oldest message age
                                                                                                                                          * EC2 CPU and memory
                                                                                                                                          * RDS CPU, connections, and query latency
                                                                                                                                          * rate limit and circuit breaker counters
Correctness checks:
                                                                                                                                          * Every simulated call produces a call record
                                                                                                                                          * Call records have consistent state transitions
                                                                                                                                          * No cross-user data access occurs during tests
                                                                                                                                          * No sensitive content appears in logs or notifications
29.3.6 Pass and fail gates
