A load test run passes only if all gates are met.
Core gates:
                                                                                                                                          * No more than a small fraction of calls fail due to internal errors during burst tests
                                                                                                                                          * Time-to-answer p95 stays under the defined ceiling under typical concurrency
                                                                                                                                          * Turn latency p95 stays within target under typical concurrency
                                                                                                                                          * Background jobs do not accumulate without recovery after the test ends
                                                                                                                                          * The system enters degraded mode rather than collapsing under overload
                                                                                                                                          * Critical privacy constraints hold:
                                                                                                                                          * notifications do not leak transcripts
                                                                                                                                          * logs do not contain message bodies or full phone numbers
Release gate for production readiness:
                                                                                                                                          * Baseline and burst scenarios pass twice on separate runs
                                                                                                                                          * One sustained workload test passes
                                                                                                                                          * One failure injection test passes
                                                                                                                                          * Alerts fire correctly when thresholds are intentionally violated
29.3.7 Capacity tuning and scaling triggers
Phase 1 runs on small instances, so it must include clear triggers for upgrades.
Scaling triggers for compute:
                                                                                                                                          * Consistent time-to-answer p95 regression under load
                                                                                                                                          * Sustained CPU above safe threshold during typical hours
                                                                                                                                          * Node bridge shows websocket instability due to resource pressure
                                                                                                                                          * Worker backlog grows and does not recover within a defined window
Scaling triggers for database:
                                                                                                                                          * RDS CPU sustained high during normal workload
                                                                                                                                          * Connection pool saturation events
                                                                                                                                          * Call list and call detail query p95 exceeds targets
                                                                                                                                          * Increased lock contention on hot tables (jobs, call state transitions)
Scaling triggers for queue workers:
                                                                                                                                          * Oldest message age rising steadily
                                                                                                                                          * Retry rates increasing
                                                                                                                                          * Throughput less than incoming job rate during peak windows
Scaling actions in Phase 1:
                                                                                                                                          * Increase instance size for the VM hosting Docker services
                                                                                                                                          * Increase RDS instance class one step
                                                                                                                                          * Split real-time Node bridge onto its own VM if co-location causes contention
                                                                                                                                          * Increase worker concurrency carefully, bounded by DB connection capacity
                                                                                                                                          * Adjust job prioritization to ensure call handling and handoff remain highest priority
29.3.8 Cost-aware load testing rules
Load tests can be expensive due to telephony minutes and AI processing.
Rules:
                                                                                                                                          * Use mock modes where possible for repeatable performance tests
                                                                                                                                          * Use real provider calls only for a small validation subset
                                                                                                                                          * Cap test duration and concurrency for real provider tests
                                                                                                                                          * Record test cost metrics alongside performance results
                                                                                                                                          * Maintain a clear stop condition if cost spikes or circuit breakers trigger
________________


29.4 Completion checklist for this chapter
This chapter is complete when:
                                                                                                                                          * A realistic load envelope is defined with concurrency headroom
                                                                                                                                          * Concrete latency targets exist for:
                                                                                                                                          * answer time
                                                                                                                                          * conversational turns
                                                                                                                                          * handoff
                                                                                                                                          * post-call artifacts
                                                                                                                                          * notifications
                                                                                                                                          * app endpoints
                                                                                                                                          * A load testing approach exists for:
                                                                                                                                          * APIs
                                                                                                                                          * queues and workers
                                                                                                                                          * real-time call flow
                                                                                                                                          * Scenario coverage includes burst, sustained, and failure injection tests
                                                                                                                                          * Pass and fail gates are explicit and measurable
                                                                                                                                          * Scaling triggers and actions are defined so Phase 1 can grow without redesign
________________


CHAPTER 30: COST MODEL AND COST CONTROLS
30.0 Purpose and scope
Phase 1 must include basic cost protection because inbound calls and AI processing create direct variable costs that can spike unexpectedly. This chapter defines:
                                                                                                                                          * How Phase 1 models cost drivers and calculates estimated cost per user and per day
                                                                                                                                          * How Phase 1 enforces caps using throttles and degraded modes without collapsing core functionality
                                                                                                                                          * How Phase 1 monitors spend signals and triggers alerts for unusual activity
Phase 1 does not require user billing or subscriptions. The purpose is internal cost protection and operational safety.
________________


30.1 Cost drivers list
30.1.1 Cost driver categories
Phase 1 cost drivers are split into two categories:
A) Variable costs (scale with usage)
                                                                                                                                          * Telephony: voice minutes, phone numbers, call forwarding legs, SMS
                                                                                                                                          * AI processing: speech processing and transcription related usage
                                                                                                                                          * Bandwidth and egress (if meaningful at scale)
B) Fixed baseline costs (exist even with low usage)
                                                                                                                                          * Compute: EC2 for API, workers, realtime bridge, and any internal services
                                                                                                                                          * Database: RDS PostgreSQL
                                                                                                                                          * Storage: S3 for logs and operational artifacts (and any optional file storage)
                                                                                                                                          * Observability: self-hosted Sentry compute and storage
Cost controls primarily target variable costs, because fixed costs are predictable.
________________


30.1.2 Telephony cost model (Twilio voice and SMS)
30.1.2.1 Units captured for every call
For each call, store the raw usage units needed to compute billed cost consistently:
                                                                                                                                          * call_duration_seconds
                                                                                                                                          * call_billing_units_seconds or minutes (depending on provider billing rules used)
                                                                                                                                          * call_direction (inbound)
                                                                                                                                          * call_mode (dedicated_number vs forwarded_fallback)
                                                                                                                                          * call_legs_count (forwarding can create additional legs)
                                                                                                                                          * caller_country (Phase 1 is US-only, still store for auditing)
                                                                                                                                          * provider_call_id (for reconciliation)
For SMS actions, store:
                                                                                                                                          * sms_segments_count (message length can split into segments)
                                                                                                                                          * sms_direction (outbound)
                                                                                                                                          * provider_message_id
                                                                                                                                          * delivery_status transitions
30.1.2.2 Telephony cost estimation formula
Use a config-driven rate table. Do not hardcode prices in code.
Voice estimated cost per call:
                                                                                                                                          * voice_cost_usd =
billable_units_voice * rate_voice_usd_per_unit
                                                                                                                                             * numbers_monthly_allocation
                                                                                                                                             * forwarding_leg_adjustment (if applicable)
Where:
                                                                                                                                             * billable_units_voice is derived from call_duration_seconds using the billing rounding rule configured for Phase 1
                                                                                                                                             * numbers_monthly_allocation is a per-user share of monthly number rental if a dedicated number exists
                                                                                                                                             * forwarding_leg_adjustment accounts for extra billed legs in forwarding mode, if the architecture creates a second billed voice path
SMS estimated cost per message:
                                                                                                                                             * sms_cost_usd = sms_segments_count * rate_sms_usd_per_segment
30.1.2.3 Number rental and per-user allocation
Dedicated numbers create a monthly fixed fee per user. For internal cost estimation, allocate number rental as:
                                                                                                                                             * number_rental_daily_usd = number_rental_monthly_usd / days_in_month
                                                                                                                                             * number_rental_allocated_to_user_day = number_rental_daily_usd if user_has_dedicated_number else 0
This keeps daily dashboards accurate and prevents variable-only dashboards from hiding a real cost base.
________________


30.1.3 AI processing cost model (ElevenLabs STT and voice)
30.1.3.1 AI usage units captured
Store AI usage units at the call level even if billing is aggregated by provider:
                                                                                                                                             * ai_conversation_seconds (duration during which AI is actively processing audio)
                                                                                                                                             * ai_stt_seconds (speech-to-text processing seconds, if billed separately)
                                                                                                                                             * ai_tts_seconds (text-to-speech generated seconds, if billed separately)
                                                                                                                                             * transcript_requested (boolean)
                                                                                                                                             * transcript_ready (boolean) and transcript_provider_id
                                                                                                                                             * summary_generated (boolean)
Phase 1 treats provider media and full transcripts as provider-held artifacts when possible. Cost estimation still requires usage units and provider reconciliation IDs.
30.1.3.2 AI cost estimation formula
Use a config-driven rate table for AI processing. Estimated cost per call:
                                                                                                                                             * ai_cost_usd =
ai_stt_billable_units * rate_ai_stt_usd_per_unit
                                                                                                                                                * ai_tts_billable_units * rate_ai_tts_usd_per_unit
                                                                                                                                                * optional_transcript_fee (if provider charges separately)
If provider cost is billed as a single combined unit, store it as:
                                                                                                                                                * ai_billable_units_total * rate_ai_usd_per_unit
Key requirement: the system must store raw usage units so rates can change without losing historical accuracy. Do not store only “final cost” without usage units.
________________


30.1.4 AWS infrastructure costs (EC2, RDS, S3, bandwidth)
30.1.4.1 Fixed cost components tracked monthly
Phase 1 has stable baseline infrastructure. Track these as monthly fixed costs:
                                                                                                                                                * EC2 baseline (Docker host for API and workers, plus any separate realtime host if used)
                                                                                                                                                * RDS baseline (PostgreSQL instance plus storage)
                                                                                                                                                * ALB baseline
                                                                                                                                                * NAT Gateway baseline (if used)
                                                                                                                                                * S3 baseline for logs and operational objects
                                                                                                                                                * CloudWatch logs and metrics baseline (if used)
These costs are not reliably attributable to a single user at low scale, but they must be visible to prevent underestimating the true cost of running Phase 1.
30.1.4.2 Optional allocation method (internal reporting only)
If internal reporting needs per-user allocation, allocate fixed costs by one of these models:
                                                                                                                                                * Per active user per day allocation
                                                                                                                                                * Proportional to call minutes (higher usage users consume more real-time capacity)
                                                                                                                                                * Hybrid: base per active user plus variable per call minute
Allocation is for reporting only. Caps enforcement should rely on variable costs.
________________


30.1.5 Sentry self-hosting cost drivers
Sentry self-host cost includes:
                                                                                                                                                * Sentry compute instance cost
                                                                                                                                                * Storage for Sentry data and attachments (should be minimized)
                                                                                                                                                * Backup retention for Sentry data (bounded)
Sentry must be configured to minimize sensitive payloads and high-volume performance noise, which also reduces storage and compute costs.
________________


30.1.6 Cost configuration registry (rates and plans)
All rates and cap thresholds must be centralized in configuration and versioned.
Required config objects:
A) pricing_rates
                                                                                                                                                * provider (telephony, ai, aws_estimated)
                                                                                                                                                * region (us)
                                                                                                                                                * unit_type (second, minute, segment, month)
                                                                                                                                                * rate_usd_per_unit
                                                                                                                                                * effective_from_timestamp
                                                                                                                                                * effective_to_timestamp (optional)
B) billing_rounding_rules
                                                                                                                                                * voice_rounding_mode (per_second, per_minute_round_up, etc.)
                                                                                                                                                * sms_segment_rules (assume segments based on length if needed)
C) usage_plans (internal, not user-facing in Phase 1)
                                                                                                                                                * plan_id (default)
                                                                                                                                                * monthly_soft_cap_usd_variable
                                                                                                                                                * monthly_hard_cap_usd_variable
                                                                                                                                                * daily_soft_cap_usd_variable
                                                                                                                                                * daily_hard_cap_usd_variable
                                                                                                                                                * max_calls_per_day
                                                                                                                                                * max_call_minutes_per_day
                                                                                                                                                * max_sms_per_day
                                                                                                                                                * default_max_call_length_seconds
                                                                                                                                                * degraded_max_call_length_seconds
                                                                                                                                                * degraded_features_mask (which features turn off in degraded mode)
D) user_overrides
                                                                                                                                                * user_id
                                                                                                                                                * plan_id override
                                                                                                                                                * special caps override
All of these must be editable only by trusted operators through internal tools or controlled config deployment.
________________


30.2 Cost caps policy
30.2.1 Objectives of cost caps in Phase 1
Cost caps in Phase 1 serve three objectives:
                                                                                                                                                * Prevent one user or a spam wave from creating uncontrolled spend
                                                                                                                                                * Keep the platform available by shedding load safely instead of collapsing
                                                                                                                                                * Preserve the core promise (call capture and call record creation) even in degraded mode
Caps must be predictable and enforced server-side. The client must not be able to bypass them.
________________


30.2.2 Cap types and enforcement hierarchy
Phase 1 enforces caps using multiple layers, from cheapest and fastest to heavier controls:
Layer 1: Per-caller and per-IP flood controls
                                                                                                                                                * Rate limiting per caller number
                                                                                                                                                * Basic flood detection and temporary suppression
                                                                                                                                                * These are cost controls because they reduce repeated call minutes and AI time
Layer 2: Per-user daily and monthly variable spend caps
                                                                                                                                                * Soft cap triggers degraded behavior
                                                                                                                                                * Hard cap blocks expensive actions and may stop answering non-VIP calls
Layer 3: Global circuit breakers
                                                                                                                                                * If provider costs spike or platform load is unstable, apply platform-wide degradation:
                                                                                                                                                * reduce max call length
                                                                                                                                                * disable live handoff
                                                                                                                                                * delay non-critical processing
________________


30.2.3 Budget reservation model (prevents race conditions)
Because calls are realtime and concurrency can happen, Phase 1 uses a reservation model to enforce caps safely.
At call start:
                                                                                                                                                1. Compute worst-case estimated variable cost for the call based on:
                                                                                                                                                * max_call_length_seconds (or degraded length if already in degraded mode)
                                                                                                                                                * telephony voice rate
                                                                                                                                                * AI rate for the same duration
                                                                                                                                                * expected post-call processing baseline (transcript and summary) if it adds separate billable cost
                                                                                                                                                2. Attempt to reserve budget atomically from the user’s remaining daily and monthly variable budget.
Database operation must be atomic:
                                                                                                                                                * If remaining_budget >= reserve_amount then decrement and proceed
                                                                                                                                                * Else apply the cap decision:
                                                                                                                                                * enter degraded mode, or
                                                                                                                                                * block or limit call handling depending on policy
At call end:
                                                                                                                                                3. Compute actual estimated variable cost from measured units:
                                                                                                                                                * actual_voice_units
                                                                                                                                                * actual_ai_units
                                                                                                                                                * sms units if any occurred
                                                                                                                                                4. Refund unused reserved budget:
                                                                                                                                                * refund = reserve_amount - actual_amount (if positive)
This prevents overspend under concurrency, because multiple calls cannot exceed remaining budget if each reserves worst-case budget.
________________


30.2.4 Soft cap behavior (degraded mode rules)
Soft cap means the user is allowed to keep using the service, but the system reduces cost per interaction.
When soft cap is reached for the day or month, apply degraded mode for non-VIP calls:
Degraded mode actions (server enforced):
                                                                                                                                                * Switch call objective to message-only
                                                                                                                                                * Reduce max call length for non-VIP calls to degraded_max_call_length_seconds
                                                                                                                                                * Disable live handoff offers for non-VIP calls
                                                                                                                                                * Reduce conversational verbosity and number of screening questions
                                                                                                                                                * Delay non-critical post-call processing steps:
                                                                                                                                                * advanced labeling
                                                                                                                                                * evidence snippets
                                                                                                                                                * Maintain core artifacts:
                                                                                                                                                * call record exists immediately
                                                                                                                                                * summary still generated, possibly shorter
                                                                                                                                                * transcript still processed if it is part of the core promise, but retries are bounded
User experience requirements:
                                                                                                                                                * The app should show a clear banner for “degraded mode active due to usage limits” for transparency.
                                                                                                                                                * This message must not reveal sensitive cost details to callers.
________________


30.2.5 Hard cap behavior (blocking rules)
Hard cap means the system must prevent further variable spend beyond a hard limit.
At hard cap:
                                                                                                                                                * Block outbound SMS completely (even with approval) except to a small allowlist if the product requires it
                                                                                                                                                * Do not offer live handoff
                                                                                                                                                * For non-VIP calls:
                                                                                                                                                * either do not answer, or
                                                                                                                                                * answer with an ultra-short message capture flow capped at a minimal duration
                                                                                                                                                * For VIP calls:
                                                                                                                                                * allow answering with strict short limits, or
                                                                                                                                                * allow answering if VIP policy explicitly overrides, but still within a bounded reserve
Hard cap must be enforced at these entry points:
                                                                                                                                                * Inbound call acceptance decision
                                                                                                                                                * SMS send action
                                                                                                                                                * Any job that triggers additional billable provider work, if applicable
Hard cap must also trigger an internal alert, because it indicates unusual usage or misconfigured thresholds.
________________


30.2.6 Anti-fraud and anomaly patterns
Cost spikes are often fraud or abuse. Phase 1 includes basic anomaly detection patterns:
Per user:
                                                                                                                                                * many short calls in a short window
                                                                                                                                                * repeated calls from many unique callers
                                                                                                                                                * unusually high call minutes compared to the user’s baseline
                                                                                                                                                * repeated handoff attempts (should be prevented by logic anyway)
Per caller:
                                                                                                                                                * repeated calls within a short window to the same user
                                                                                                                                                * repeated calls across multiple users (if a shared number pool exists)
Global:
                                                                                                                                                * sudden spikes in inbound call rate
                                                                                                                                                * unusual increase in failed calls that still consume billable minutes
                                                                                                                                                * queue backlog causing repeated retries
When anomalies are detected, apply the least disruptive mitigation first:
                                                                                                                                                * tighten per-caller rate limits
                                                                                                                                                * temporarily reduce max call length
                                                                                                                                                * disable handoff
                                                                                                                                                * pause onboarding if attack is platform-wide
________________


30.2.7 Cap state machine and storage
Store cap state so the system is deterministic across services and devices.
Required tables:
A) user_usage_period
                                                                                                                                                * user_id
                                                                                                                                                * period_type (daily, monthly)
                                                                                                                                                * period_start_utc
                                                                                                                                                * reserved_usd
                                                                                                                                                * actual_estimated_usd
                                                                                                                                                * remaining_soft_budget_usd
                                                                                                                                                * remaining_hard_budget_usd
                                                                                                                                                * current_mode (normal, degraded, blocked)
                                                                                                                                                * last_updated_at
B) cost_ledger_events
Append-only cost events used for audit and re-computation:
                                                                                                                                                * event_id
                                                                                                                                                * user_id
                                                                                                                                                * event_type (call, sms, refund_adjustment, number_rental_allocation)
                                                                                                                                                * usage_units_payload (seconds, minutes, segments)
                                                                                                                                                * estimated_cost_usd
                                                                                                                                                * provider_ids (safe identifiers only)
                                                                                                                                                * created_at
Ledger events allow re-estimation if rates change, and provide traceability during disputes and incident response.
________________


30.3 Budget monitoring approach
30.3.1 Monitoring goals
Budget monitoring must detect:
                                                                                                                                                * A single user generating unusually high variable spend
                                                                                                                                                * A platform-wide spike (spam wave or provider misbehavior)
                                                                                                                                                * A silent failure mode where retries generate cost without delivering value
                                                                                                                                                * Approaching monthly budget thresholds early enough to mitigate
Monitoring must avoid sensitive content. It uses numeric signals and internal IDs.
________________


30.3.2 Real-time cost signal pipeline (near real time)
Phase 1 uses near real-time estimation, not invoice reconciliation, for enforcement.
Event production points:
                                                                                                                                                * On call end: emit a cost_ledger_event with measured call units
                                                                                                                                                * On SMS send attempt: emit a cost_ledger_event with segments
                                                                                                                                                * Daily: emit number_rental_allocation events for users with dedicated numbers
Aggregation jobs:
                                                                                                                                                * Update user_usage_period daily and monthly counters
                                                                                                                                                * Compute rolling 1 hour and 24 hour spend estimates per user
                                                                                                                                                * Compute global spend estimate per hour
If background job processing is delayed, cost events must still be emitted and aggregated once the call end data exists.
________________


30.3.3 Provider reconciliation (daily sanity checks)
Even without building billing, Phase 1 should reconcile estimates against provider usage summaries on a daily basis for sanity checking.
Reconciliation outputs:
                                                                                                                                                * estimated_voice_units vs provider_voice_units
                                                                                                                                                * estimated_sms_segments vs provider_sms_segments
                                                                                                                                                * estimated_ai_units vs provider_ai_units
If reconciliation drift exceeds a threshold, trigger an internal alert because it indicates:
                                                                                                                                                * wrong rounding rules
                                                                                                                                                * missed events
                                                                                                                                                * duplicate ledger events
                                                                                                                                                * provider configuration changes
Reconciliation is for operations, not for user billing in Phase 1.
________________


30.3.4 Dashboards required
Phase 1 requires at least these dashboards for operators:
A) Variable cost dashboard
                                                                                                                                                * total estimated variable spend today
                                                                                                                                                * spend split by category:
                                                                                                                                                * telephony voice
                                                                                                                                                * sms
                                                                                                                                                * ai processing
                                                                                                                                                * top users by estimated spend
                                                                                                                                                * top users by call minutes
                                                                                                                                                * anomaly indicators (spike vs baseline)
B) Cap enforcement dashboard
                                                                                                                                                * number of users in degraded mode
                                                                                                                                                * number of users blocked by hard cap
                                                                                                                                                * count of blocked SMS attempts
                                                                                                                                                * count of blocked call acceptances
C) Retry and waste dashboard
                                                                                                                                                * AI pipeline retry counts
                                                                                                                                                * webhook retry counts
                                                                                                                                                * ratio of failed summaries to successful calls
                                                                                                                                                * queue backlog and oldest message age
This dashboard is important because retries can create cost without value.
________________


30.3.5 Alerts (cost spikes and thresholding)
Alerts must trigger on both absolute thresholds and relative spikes:
User-level alerts:
                                                                                                                                                * daily estimated spend exceeds configured alert threshold
                                                                                                                                                * monthly estimated spend reaches soft cap early in the month
                                                                                                                                                * unusually high calls per hour compared to baseline
                                                                                                                                                * repeated cap hits in consecutive days
Global alerts:
                                                                                                                                                * estimated spend per hour exceeds global threshold
                                                                                                                                                * inbound call volume spikes above expected envelope
                                                                                                                                                * large rise in failed calls or provider errors that still consume minutes
                                                                                                                                                * AI pipeline failure spike with rising retries
Alert payload requirements:
                                                                                                                                                * include user_id hash, not direct PII
                                                                                                                                                * include counts, units, and costs
                                                                                                                                                * include top suspected cause classification code
                                                                                                                                                * include the recommended mitigation action (tighten rate limits, reduce call length, disable handoff)
________________


30.3.6 Automated mitigation hooks (optional but recommended)
Phase 1 can optionally automate the first safe mitigation step when a severe spike is detected:
                                                                                                                                                * auto-enable platform-wide degraded call length for non-VIP calls
                                                                                                                                                * auto-disable live handoff temporarily
                                                                                                                                                * auto-tighten per-caller rate limits
Automation must be conservative and reversible. Every automated mitigation must write an audit event with:
                                                                                                                                                * trigger signal
                                                                                                                                                * action taken
                                                                                                                                                * start time and end time
                                                                                                                                                * operator override state
________________


30.3.7 Testing and validation of cost controls
Cost controls must be tested as part of QA, because cost failures are production failures.
Required test cases:
                                                                                                                                                * reservation model prevents overspend under concurrent calls
                                                                                                                                                * soft cap triggers degraded mode deterministically
                                                                                                                                                * hard cap blocks SMS sends and prevents further reservations
                                                                                                                                                * VIP override behavior is correct and bounded
                                                                                                                                                * anomaly patterns trigger alerts without using sensitive content
                                                                                                                                                * reconciliation detects drift when intentional mismatch is introduced
                                                                                                                                                * ledger is append-only and idempotency prevents duplicate cost events for the same call or message
Validation evidence:
                                                                                                                                                * test logs with call_id and cost event IDs
                                                                                                                                                * dashboard screenshots showing counters changing as expected
                                                                                                                                                * confirmation that blocked actions do not proceed even if client is modified
________________


30.4 Completion checklist for this chapter
This chapter is complete when:
                                                                                                                                                * All cost drivers are enumerated and measured in consistent units
                                                                                                                                                * A centralized pricing and caps registry exists with versioning and effective dates
                                                                                                                                                * The reservation model is implemented for call start and SMS send to prevent race-condition overspend
                                                                                                                                                * Soft cap degraded mode is defined and enforced server-side
                                                                                                                                                * Hard cap blocking rules are defined and enforced server-side
                                                                                                                                                * Cost ledger events exist as an append-only source of truth for estimation and audit
                                                                                                                                                * Dashboards and alerts exist for cost spikes, cap hits, and retry waste
                                                                                                                                                * QA includes explicit test cases proving caps and degraded modes work under concurrency and attack-like scenarios
